# Mind Map — Overflow (full archive)

> Live nodes below mirror `MIND_MAP.md` byte-for-byte — grep `^\[N\]` ABOVE the `## Legacy` line for the live node. After Pass B (Task 018) the complete pre-Pass-B per-task archive is preserved verbatim under `## Legacy`, fenced so the **fence-aware** sync/ref-integrity parsers (playbook plugin at or past the fence-aware version) skip it. Those legacy `[N]` are OLD numbers and a bare `^\[N\]` grep would match both them and the live mirror — look up an old node via `.agent/marius/tasks/018-mindmap-passb-subsystem-grouping/mapping.md`, not by grepping.

[1] **Project Overview** - AloVet is a consultation-first veterinary marketplace (Phase 1 remote vet services; Phase 2 merchandise shop), built AI-driven via the playbook workflow [3]. Full-stack: an Expo/React-Native mobile app [13] in two role variants (owner + medic [14]) over a Spring Boot REST backend [6]. Architecture in [2], current status in [4], roadmap/sequence in [5]. **Pivot 2026-06-14:** the generic full-stack foundation was tagged `v0-foundation` (`ba0017d`) as a fork point; `main` now evolves toward AloVet (specs in `docs/`, exec strategy + plans in `docs/specs/`). Backend subsystems: identity/auth/policy [6], domain schema [7], execution kernel [8], owner product APIs [9], medic subsystem [10], consult lifecycle [11], contracts/seed/test-infra [12]. Frontend subsystems: foundation & patterns [13], dev-env/build/variants [14], owner screens [15], medic screens [16]. Cross-cutting: push [17]. Reference index: `CLAUDE.md` (workflow rules + CLI), `MIND_MAP_OVERFLOW.md` (full per-node archive + legacy history), `.agent/tasks/` (execution traces), `.agent/playbooks/` (reusable patterns).

[2] **Architecture** - **Frontend** (`./frontend/`) — Expo 54 / RN 0.81 / React 19 / Expo Router 6 mobile app [13], containerized dev env [14]. **Backend** (`./backend/`) — Spring Boot 4 / Java 21 / PostgreSQL, JWT-secured stateless REST API [6], package-by-feature. **Workflow** (`./.agent/`, `./.claude/`) — playbook task management + Claude Code config [3]. The contract seam is `backend/openapi.yaml` [12] — the frontend/backend agreement, backend-leads one step ahead. Two-dev layer split [3]: Cristian owns `backend/`, Marius owns `frontend/`. Live day-to-day conventions live in `backend/CLAUDE.md` + `frontend/CLAUDE.md` (auto-loaded per subtree); this map is the *why*. See [1].

[3] **Development Workflow** - Playbook task system via `.claude/bin/tasks` (lifecycle design → work plan → pre-review; full CLI in CLAUDE.md). **The mind map is updated per task by editing the OWNING subsystem node in place** (Pass B rule, Task 018 — see header + ownership map). **Two-dev split (from 2026-07-01):** work divided by layer — Cristian `backend/`, Marius `frontend/` — coordinated contract-first, backend-leads (`backend/openapi.yaml` [12] is the agreement, backend one step ahead; the error envelope `{status,message,code,fieldErrors,details}` with stable `code` constants is part of the contract). Isolation: branch-per-person → merge to `main` via `/playbook:merge`; `.agent/<user>/` state is per-person. Fewer, subsystem-keyed nodes make mind-map merges same-node content-merges with rare renumbers. Panel reviews (plan + impl) replace single-judge reviews; since audit Phase-B G5 every review prompt (single + panel, plan + impl) carries the hostile-sequence checklist (two concurrent requests / duplicate distinct-id events / reversed order / external-success+local-rollback / commit+crash / lost-response retry) — it lives in the PLUGIN's `tasks/template.py`, so it is re-applied (alongside the mind-map subsystem gate) by `.claude/bin/reapply-playbook-patches.py` after every `playbook:upgrade` (count-aware 2-occurrence patterns; `--self-test` proves the classification; Task 042 consolidated the two old reapply scripts into this one — budget/timeout now config-resolved, sandbox utf-8 upstreamed). See [1][2][5].

[4] **Status** - **Now (2026-07-16):** Backend complete through **Slice 3c + S4 dispatch/instant**: payments/money [18] shipped (direct charges, webhooks/refunds, cancel/no-show, vouchers, decline recovery, earnings), ratings + consult-summary [10][11], the external-audit Phase-B guardrails G1-G6 [12], and S4 instant-request dispatch fan-out + on-session payment (Tasks 105-110) [11][8][18]; only `003-deploy-to-google-cloud-cicd` remains open. **Frontend** owner + medic apps complete through **Slice 3c (code-complete, device-deferred)**: owner consent/pets/addresses (S1) + discovery/booking (3a) + card-save/voucher/recovery/cancel (3b) + ratings/records (3c) [15]; medic shell/profile/schedule/presence/payouts/requests/earnings/complete-cancel [16], under the Task-040 authoritative not-a-medic gate. Latest: merged main's S4 backend into the FE branch (`0688145`); tooling housekeeping (reapply-script consolidation, this mind-map lean). **Open cross-cutting:** cross-app login isn't role-scoped (Task 110 now returns roles on the auth contract — FE to consume); no owner bookings-list endpoint. Roadmap/sequence → [5]; task-by-task history → git + `MIND_MAP_OVERFLOW.md`. See [5][6][11][18][10].

[5] **Roadmap & Split Plan** - The two-lane BE/FE sequence (companion `docs/specs/2026-07-02-backend-frontend-split-plan.html`); [4] is the *status* source, this is the *sequence* source. **Backend done:** B1-B4 → Slice 3a booking [11] → Slice 3b payments/money [18] → Slice 3c ratings+summary [10][11] → external-audit G1-G6 [12] → **S4 dispatch/instant (Tasks 105-110)** [11][8][18]; remaining paired: 3c video/RTC. **Frontend done:** S1 owner screens [15] → F4 monorepo [13][14] → F5 medic screens [16] → Slice 3a discovery/booking [15][16] → Slice 3b payments UI F7a-d [15][16] → **Slice 3c F7e/F8a/F8b code-complete** [15][16]; **next (FE):** S4 instant-request UI + 3c video (paired), against the shipped [11][18] contracts. **Growth budget (Pass B):** a new node only for a genuinely new subsystem (~1/slice) — 3b→[18] done, 3c→Video/RTC (new, pending), S5→Media (new), S6 travel→[10][11], S8 obs/GDPR→[12] or a new Ops node. **Operative defaults (override by saying so):** pet photos device-local until S5; email/analytics → first consumer; GDPR jobs → S8. **PAIRED phases:** 3c video, S4 dispatch transport, S8 observability. See [3][4][9][10][11][12][18].

[6] **Identity, Auth & Policy** - **owns:** accounts, roles, JWT, refresh tokens, rate limiting, authorization policy, account/profile endpoints, security config, wire-format. Package-by-feature (`auth/`, `account/`, `common/security/`); full conventions in `backend/CLAUDE.md`. **AloVet identity model:** the fused `users` table split into an auth **`Account`** + role-bearing profiles; **roles are DERIVED from profile/grant existence — there is NO role field**. **Authorization:** `PolicyService` is the single authz layer (INV-002). **Admin grants
(Task 081):** `AdminGrant`/`GRANT_REVOKE_ADMIN_ROLES` had existed since Slice-0a but no code
path ever created a grant — `AdminGrantService` (bootstrap-superadmin/grant/revoke, exposed via
the [12] admin CLI) is the first consumer. `bootstrapSuperadmin` is the one deliberate exception
to "every admin action needs an existing privileged actor": it fires only while `admin_grants`
is completely empty (revoked rows count), promoting an already self-registered account —
permanently refuses once any grant has ever existed, on every environment including prod. **Tokens:** access JWT ~15-min, opaque refresh tokens (32-byte SecureRandom, SHA-256-hashed, 30-day absolute, single-use rotation) with reuse-detection (token-family). **Hardening:** per-account lockout (5 wrong → 15-min → 423), per-IP Bucket4j + per-user Caffeine rate limits, CORS fail-closed, JWT-secret fail-fast, 256KB body cap. **Wire-format (load-bearing):** controllers return DTO records via `from(Entity)`, NEVER entities (a serialized entity leaks the BCrypt hash); JPA entities use identity-based equals/hashCode, not Lombok `@Data`. **Profile:** `/me` GET/PATCH + `/me/password` (mints fresh tokens); wrong current-password = 400/VALIDATION_FAILED (distinct from login 401). Config/security gotchas (`JwtAuthenticationFilter` is a `@Bean` not `@Component`; SecurityConfig matchers first-match-wins so specific routes precede wildcards; `/api/auth/refresh` stays public) live in `backend/CLAUDE.md`. See [2][7][12][17]; frontend auth flow [13].

[7] **Domain Schema** - **owns:** the core AloVet clinical-spine schema + Flyway migration conventions. The domain schema every later slice hangs off — **schema + write-guard only, no behavior** (matching/dispatch/payments/state transitions belong to the execution kernel [8] and consult lifecycle [11]). Forward-only migrations `V18-V22` off the v2.6 identity model [6]. **Flyway conventions (`backend/CLAUDE.md`):** wired via `spring-boot-starter-flyway` NOT bare `flyway-core` (SB4 moved `FlywayAutoConfiguration` into the starter — with bare core Spring silently no-ops migrations on startup); all profiles run `ddl-auto: validate` (schema drift fails startup); a NOT-NULL column is added via add-default → backfill → drop-default. **Content tables** carry a JSONB `{ro,en}` `name` map + `sort_order`/`active` (patient_categories/species_map V18, services V32, **temperament_flags V37**); Task 080 also added the **`content_version`** singleton (CHECK id=1) that the app-content ETag [9] derives from — **bump contract:** any migration changing bundled content MUST `UPDATE content_version` (no trigger; convention). **symptom_categories (V48, Task 106)** adds the O9-governed content shape: content-table columns + per-row `content_version`/`reviewed_by`/`signed_off_at`, guarded by the repo's FIRST PL/pgSQL trigger — a VALUE change (`IS DISTINCT FROM`, never target-list: Hibernate full-column UPDATEs) to `code`/`name`/`is_red_flag` must bump the row version AND clear sign-off (re-sign-off convention); `sort_order`/`active` are operational (unsigned-active is INV-030's gate). A migration flipping `is_red_flag` must itself recompute non-terminal interactions' `red_flag_triggered` (no cache trigger — INV-029 is write-time over the selection). See [6][8][9][11][12].

[8] **Execution Kernel** - **owns:** the state-machine runner, background-job engine, concurrency/idempotency primitives, and the invariant test harness. Built kernel-first (primitives before consumers) so slices 3/4/6 don't each hand-roll racy claim logic. **State-machine runner:** the engine that legally moves an Interaction's status (the behavior the schema [7] deferred). **Background-job engine:** a DB-backed, durable, at-least-once job queue (home for ~11 time-based duties); a duplicate `JobHandler` type fails app-start (deliberate guard). **Concurrency & idempotency scaffolding:** the race-safe claim seam (data-model decision 11) the money/dispatch/booking flows consume. **Invariant harness:** formalizes the `INV-xxx` convention into a machine-checked ledger — every invariant a named test, a failing invariant stops the build; invariants advance incrementally *with* their consuming slices, not in a batch. The first product consumer is consult booking [11]. **Push-notification outbox (Task 098, audit F3):** `PushNotificationJobHandler` (`type=push-notification`) is now a consumer — booking writers `enqueue` the FCM push in their own tx so it's atomic with the state change and dispatched AFTER commit (no phantom push on rollback; no FCM network I/O under the row lock); see [17]. **DispatchOffer machine (Task 107):** the engine's SECOND entity family — `dispatch/DispatchOfferMachine` models the persisted `(response, resolution)` pair as the state (`OfferState`, 13 legal pairs), and `DispatchOfferTransitionRunner` is its transactional consumer: race edges execute as repository conditional UPDATEs whose targets are PRE-ASSERTED against the declared table (`assertDeclared` — an undeclared edge throws before SQL; decision 11's "first-accept-wins is one conditional update"), locked edges mutate managed entities under the interaction row lock. The INV-022 fidelity harness now covers both families — the offer machine has its OWN pair-aware YAML parser/test (`InvariantINV022DispatchOfferFidelityTest`; the flat from/event/to parser can't read resolution rows), and the dir-completeness check pairs all 4 YAMLs. Canonical `dispatch_offer.yaml` relocated docs→test-resources and AMENDED in-task (two edges the resolutions list implied but no transition emitted: pending+medic_reserved_elsewhere→voided, pending+auth_succeeded_other→cancelled/superseded). Job/concurrency conventions in `backend/CLAUDE.md`. **Fan-out duties (Task 108):** five dispatch job types (`dispatch-wave`/`-offer-timeout`/`-wallclock`/`-reprompt`/`-confirm-expiry`) run the instant matching loop over the engine; the load-bearing pattern is the **durable duty claim** (`dispatch_duty_claims` V50, unique `(interaction, duty, token)`, `INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` inside the handler tx): the queue's dedupe key frees at job-terminal so it only coalesces concurrently-LIVE duplicates — the claim is the forever exactly-once guard (wave N fans out once; the 60s re-prompt self-loop is invisible to any state guard) AND the wave-resume cursor (`MAX(token)+1` — empty waves leave no offer rows). `JobRunner.run` is one tx: handler writes + claim + successor enqueues + `markSucceeded` commit atomically. A resume enqueue that coalesces with a still-pending future successor must `reschedule` it to NOW. See [7][11][12].

[9] **Owner Product APIs** - **owns:** the owner-facing product HTTP surfaces — pets, consents, addresses. The first AloVet endpoints beyond the pre-AloVet auth/account/push controllers, delivered contract-first backend-leads [3]. **Pet API:** owner-scoped CRUD `/api/pets` — the first AloVet product surface; unblocks the frontend pet screens [15]. **Consent API:** append-only `/api/consents` (record grant / list current / withdraw), per-**Account** (consent is per-person, not a role) — completes onboarding's GDPR consent capture. **Address:** `/api/addresses` extended additively for home-visits (label / geo lat-lng / sector / floor / intercom / parking + an ops-only `flag` + soft-delete; geo is both-or-neither; returns the `PagedResponse` envelope, hard-capped at 100). This is the blueprint for later owner surfaces. **App-Content bundle (Task 080):** `GET /api/app-content/{audience}` (owner|medic), **public/permitAll** — a thin composite of the taxonomy display vocabularies (species+embedded category, patient categories, temperament flags, specialties) the FE renders as pickers/chips; full `{ro,en}` maps on the wire. **Scraper invariant:** only unauthenticated-safe content may enter a bundle. Version-derived strong ETag (per-audience, from the `content_version` singleton [7]) + `If-None-Match`→304 (steady state; content ~monthly) via `checkNotModified` (NOT ShallowEtag); `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` → an audience's absent section omits its key. Retired the authenticated `/api/specialties` (folded in; `SpecialtyResponse`→`taxonomy/dto`). FE hooks (`useAppContent`/`pickLocale`/prefetch) are Marius's layer; medic bundle grows additively. **Task 106:** owner bundle grew `symptomCategories` (id/code/`{ro,en}` name/isRedFlag/sortOrder — the instant-flow picker source; O9 governance fields NEVER on this permitAll wire); medic gets it later with the offer card (paired). See [6][7][10][12]; frontend consumers [15][16].

[10] **Medic Subsystem** - **owns:** the `/api/medic/*` surface — profile, schedule, presence, day-view, Stripe Connect. Slice-2 backend, self-scoped, mirroring the owner blueprint [9]. **Foundation:** schema + self-scoped reads. **ScheduleBlock CRUD:** a medic self-manages recurring working-hours + explicit block-out rows (V34). **Presence:** `/api/medic/presence` ONLINE/OFFLINE with auto-expiry + a re-confirm push [17] before expiry ("the only live status in the system"). **Day-view:** `GET /api/medic/day-view?date=` returns computed bookable windows per channel (windows are computed, never stored) — the Slice-2 shell, with NO booking/SlotHold overlay (that's [11]) and no travel-aware windows (S6). **Stripe Connect:** `/api/medic/stripe/connect-link` + `/status` (test-mode hosted onboarding) — unblocks F5's Stripe screen [16]. **Task 083:** reworked from transfers-only Recipient → full v2 **Merchant** accounts (`card_payments`, `dashboard:full`, fees/losses=stripe) so the medic is merchant of record for **direct charges** [18]; `/status` derives from `card_payments` (field `cardPaymentsStatus`, `state` enum unchanged); V38 nulls stored Recipient ids to force Merchant re-provision; live-verify IOU deferred (real-Stripe merchant create, like Task 076 W9). **Reputation (Task 091, Slice 3c):** `ratings` V46 (one per interaction, UNIQUE) — `Medic.ratingAvg/ratingCount` are CACHES recomputed FROM the table inside every rating-write/moderation tx (never incremented; per-medic `pg_advisory_xact_lock` extracted to `common/concurrency/MedicAdvisoryLock`, shared with SlotHold; recompute filters `hidden_at IS NULL AND is_test = medic's lineage`). Surfaces: owner `POST /api/bookings/{id}/rating` (completed-only) + `GET /api/medics/{id}/ratings` (matchability-gated, petId), medic self-list + one-reply `/api/medic/ratings`; moderation = admin CLI `rating-hide/unhide` (MODERATE_RATINGS_INCIDENTS, recomputes transactionally — never raw SQL). `owner_id` nullable (GDPR severs authorship, stars survive); `by_medic_account` ops flag (roles v1.4 constraint 3). Feeds the 3a ranking composite + "New" badge [11]. **FE-consumed (F8a, Task 036 — code-complete, device-deferred):** owner rates on the completed-booking status screen + reads a medic's reviews on the search-profile [15]; medic reads own reviews + posts the one-time reply [16]; a new rating's `['medics']` prefix invalidation refreshes the cached avg/count. Rated-state is FE session-scoped + 409-reconciled pending a `myRating` contract field (Cristi flag). **FE-consumed (F5c, Task 024 [16]):** presence toggle + server-derived countdown, and Stripe payouts via a hosted-browser link, now live in the medic app; note the plain dev backend returns status `NOT_STARTED` without touching Stripe (no account) while connect-link 503s (`app.stripe.enabled=false`), and a PUT presence while `reserved`/`in_consultation` → 409 `MEDIC_PRESENCE_CONFLICT`. **Dispatch presence edges (Task 107):** `MedicReservationService.tryReserve` got its first consumer (offer accept — the exclusivity gate), and `MedicRepository` grew two system-set CASes: `releaseIfReserved` (reserved→online, REFRESHES expiry to now+onlineTtl per B3 — exposed as `MedicPresenceService.releaseReservation`, which also enqueues the matching reconfirm token in the same tx) and `enterConsultationIfReserved` (auth-success). All are `@Modifying(clearAutomatically)` — callers MUST reload managed entities after any presence CAS. **Offboard voiding (Task 108, INV-016 covered):** `expireStale` is now an atomic native `UPDATE…RETURNING id` (`MedicRepositoryImpl` custom fragment — the returned ids ARE the offer-void set; a pre-SELECT could void a heartbeat-saved medic) and both it and the voluntary OFFLINE toggle notify `PresenceOffboardListener`s (interface in `medic/`, impl in `dispatch/` — no package cycle) in the SAME tx: the medic's pending offers void (`medic_presence_expired_or_offline`, null resolution); accepted front/backups survive (confirm-expiry / promotion re-validation own those). See [9][11][8][6][5][18]; medic screens [16].

[11] **Consult Lifecycle** - **owns:** consult booking, the Interaction/Order lifecycle, and (future) dispatch + matching. **Payments/money split off to [18]** (Task 082). The first PRODUCT consumer of the 0b machinery [8]. **Slice 3a scheduled-consult booking:** Interaction/Order's first real write path, SlotHold's first real caller, PricingConfig's first consumer — booking runs over the state-machine runner + job engine + concurrency primitive [8]. **Same-day booking fix (Task 105):** the min-start-lead floor FILTERS the discretized slot candidates in `AvailabilityService` — never an interval cut (a cut re-anchored today's window to the request instant, so the hold's fresh-`now` recompute never contained the picker's instants → every same-day hold 409'd SLOT_UNAVAILABLE); invariant: slot phase is a pure function of stored schedule/busy inputs, advancing time only removes candidates. `BookingService.hold`'s lead gate sits AFTER the own-`checkoutRef` idempotent path (lost-response retry on the nearest slot must return the committed hold, not 400); grid purity after off-grid medic blockers is a PARKED product question (task 105 Parked). **Ratings (Task 091):** a completed consult is rateable exactly once by its owner — the write + transactional aggregate recompute live with the medic subsystem [10]; the ranking composite here finally has real input. **Consult summary (Task 092):** the assigned medic files a free-text visit summary (`POST /api/medic/consults/{id}/summary`, self-scoped) while in_progress or ≤48h post-completion (config `app.records.summary-window`; the completion clock is the status-event ledger row — Interaction has no completedAt); it lands as a `visit_summary` RecordEntry (V47, `record/` package — the Records-domain seed, full data-model column set but ONE behaving type) on the pet's timeline, read owner-scoped via `GET /api/pets/{petId}/records`. S5 extends this into structured summaries/media/more types and should promote Records to its own node then. **Roadmap (this is the biggest growth node — see [5]):** 3c video/RTC, S4 dispatch/matching, and S6 travel-aware windows land here (**3b payments/money split off to [18]**, Task 082; video will likely spawn its own node too). Consumes the domain schema [7] and medic availability [10]. **S4 intake foundation (Task 106):** `InteractionSymptom` multi-select join + `SymptomSelectionService.applySelection(ownerId, interactionId, ids)` — transaction-owning, owner-scoped row lock (`findByIdAndOwnerIdForUpdate`, cross-owner→404), validate-all-then-replace (immediate bulk delete before inserts — Hibernate flush ordering would trip the unique pair), recomputes `redFlagTriggered = OR(selected.isRedFlag)` in-tx (INV-029 flipped covered; last-writer-wins, no request identity — idempotency keys belong to the calling intake endpoint, next task). The instant-request/DispatchOffer task consumes this seam. **FE consumer (Task 026, F6b):** the owner app now drives this write path end-to-end (availability→hold→confirm→status poll + booking_* push) — owner-side screens/data-layer live in [15]. **FE consumer (Task 037, F8b — code-complete, device-deferred):** the owner READS the `visit_summary` timeline on a pet-detail "Records" drill-in [15]; the medic WRITE slice (`useFileSummary`) is built [16] but its compose SCREEN is PARKED — the medic app has no source of a consult's petIds (no `GET /api/medic/consults/{id}` detail; `openapi.yaml` unchanged on cristi-dev), so `FileSummaryRequest` can't be constructed. **THE Cristi ask:** a completed-serving `GET /api/medic/consults/{id}` detail carrying `pets:[{petId,petName}]` — it unblocks this compose screen AND closes the consult-complete task's GAP 2 + panel-F1 filing-surface hole in one endpoint. **S4 instant entry + offer machine (Task 107, `dispatch/` package — owned here):** owner `POST /api/instant-requests` is create-or-REJOIN (C6/INV-019: SELECT-first + V49 partial-unique `uq_interactions_open_instant_per_owner` backstop, four non-terminal states enumerated in lockstep with `findLiveInstantForOwner`; `InstantRequestWriter` takes IDS not entities — a non-tx orchestrator's detached lazy proxies blow up in the write guard); accept is the two-write tx per v2.7:118 (interaction-lock-FIRST ordering [D3], then `tryReserve`; answer≠accept, late accepts = honest backups with rank, winner's other pending offers void `medic_reserved_elsewhere`); owner/system seams for tasks 3/4: `ownerConfirm`/`ownerDecline(+declinedOfferId — stale decline 409s, never a wrong-medic misfire)`/`confirmExpiry(+expectedFrontOfferId — stale timer no-op)`/`authFailed`/`authSucceeded` (the ONLY supersede, B15/INV-017, + enterConsultation) /`ownerCancel`; promotion re-validates via tryReserve (INV-018), winner gets the full accept-win side-effect set. INV-015/017/018/019 flipped covered (+INV-016 partially wired). **LAUNCH-LEGAL since Task 109:** the INV-037 payment-method-on-file creation guard rejects method-less taps (409, zero rows); the owner-level card flow + confirm/authorize + P0-C2 completion + fault seam live in [18] (payment pkg). 3c video consumes: the fault seam (`technicalFailure(fault, billableSec)`) + the duration SOURCE (ledger in_progress proxy → VideoSession billable). **Fan-out orchestration (Task 108, S4 3/4):** `InstantRequestWriter.create` arms `DispatchOrchestrator.armFanout` in-tx (wave-1 job + 8-min wall-clock watchdog; THE call the payment task gates behind the SetupIntent); `DispatchWaveJobHandler` locks the interaction, checks the 5-min CUMULATIVE-open budget (`DispatchOpenBudget` ledger walk), claims the wave (durable claim [8]), selects (`findInstantConsultCandidates`: active+ONLINE+unexpired+lineage+category — INV-032's instant half; `DispatchWaveSelector`: rating-only w/ New-neutral, guaranteed C1 exploration slot, D3 exclusions: live/declined/timeout/owner_declined out, voided-family + released_no_fault re-offerable), sends via `sendOffer` + pushes through `DispatchNotifier` (default = push outbox [17]; high-priority FCM/VoIP = PAIRED swap-in), chains successor at min(cadence 45s-then-30s, 15s red-flag; budget-exhaustion instant); chain PARKS while a proposal is on the table, release paths resume DUE-NOW (+ reschedule a coalesced pending successor). Confirm window: accept/promotion arm 60s re-prompt (claim-guarded `confirm_timeout_60s` self-loop + owner push) + 90s `confirmExpiry`. ALL terminal paths (total_timeout, wall-clock, owner cancel) go through ONE `DispatchTerminalWriter` (lock-first, re-check, transition+sweep+release+`InstantOrderTerminationPort`+owner push) — the port's unfunded impl cancels draft orders (closes 107's order.cancel gap; funded outcome = payment-task plug-in; NEVER the REQUIRES_NEW booking `CancellationTerminalWriter` — self-deadlock). Entry now requires a matchable pet category (INV-027 code at create, not in a background job). INV-016/020/023 flipped covered; narrated fan-out story test. New 409 codes: `OFFER_NOT_ACTIONABLE`/`OFFER_MEDIC_UNAVAILABLE`/`INSTANT_REQUEST_CONFLICT`. Controller method names = springdoc operationIds — keep globally unique (a collision renumbers EXISTING endpoints' ids under the FE). See [8][7][10][9][5][15][16].

[12] **Contracts, Seed & Test Infra** - **owns:** the OpenAPI contract, test lineage (`isTest`), seed/config tooling, the scripted demo, and the backend test runner + perf tiers. **OpenAPI contract:** springdoc generate-from-code + committed `backend/openapi.yaml` + a build-stopping drift guard (`OpenApiContractTest`) — the frontend/backend agreement [3]. **`Account.isTest`:** the data-model decision-8 test-lineage root + first derivation. **B2 seed/config:** a `Clinic` entity + audited CLI (clinic-upsert / medic-onboard / affiliate /
demo-env-refresh / bootstrap-superadmin / grant-admin / revoke-admin, Task 081 added the last
three), scripts with no HTTP surface. **Runbook gotcha (Task 081):** never put a literal `--`
before an admin CLI invocation's flags — `docker compose run --rm backend -- --spring.main...`
is NOT a safe defensive separator, it's actively harmful: Spring's own arg parser treats a bare
`--` as an end-of-options marker, so every flag after it silently fails to bind (boots as a
normal web server, `AdminCommandRunner` no-ops with zero output) — this reproduces exactly what
a frontend-lane bug report attributed to compose's own flag parsing; it wasn't that. Plain
`docker compose run --rm backend --spring.main...` (no `--`) is verified working on Compose
v5.1.4. See `docs/specs/2026-07-05-manual-onboarding-runbook.md` §0/§6. **B3 scripted demo:** the Slice-0b demo built as a narrated integration test exercising the kernel [8] end-to-end. **Test runner (`backend/CLAUDE.md`):** `.claude/bin/run-backend-tests` — off-mount build (a Docker/WSL2 update made bind mounts report root:root → EPERM), uid-1000 / `HOME=/tmp` / `.m2` pinned; Tier-1 query-count/N+1 guards (in the default suite) + Tier-2 perf benchmarks; conventions for FK indexing + the `PagedResponse` list envelope. See [6][7][8][9][10].

[13] **Frontend Foundation & Patterns** - **owns:** the RN app's stack, routing, add-a-feature recipe, theming, error-handling framework, forms + i18n. Full rules in `frontend/CLAUDE.md` + `frontend/README.md`. **Stack:** TanStack Query v5 + Zustand (cache persisted to AsyncStorage, gated on auth-store hydration), RHF + Zod, NativeWind v4. **Routing:** auth gating via `<Stack.Protected guard>` at the root `_layout.tsx`, NOT effect-redirects / `<Redirect>` (both tried and rejected — loop / unmatched-route). **Monorepo (Task 021):** `frontend/` is a pnpm-workspace root — `apps/owner`+`apps/medic` (each owns `app/`, `src/{features,hooks}`, full config surface + native trees), `packages/core` (`@alovet/core`: api/lib/config/theme/i18n/auth-store+bridge+services), `packages/ui` (`@alovet/ui`: primitives + `cn`/`formScroll`/`stackHeader`); consumed as SOURCE via workspace links + metro `watchFolders`=workspace-root. Package graph **app→ui→core**, never into app. **Add-a-feature:** `apps/<role>/src/features/<name>/` (per-app; shared infra → `packages/core/<name>/`, e.g. auth store/services/bridge in core but the login/register hooks stay per-app); shared UI → `packages/ui`; vendor SDKs only behind `@alovet/core/lib/` facades; no `console.*`; aliases — app-local `@/`→own `src/`, cross-package `@alovet/core|ui` (each wired in that app's 4 configs). **Task 019 primitives:** `TextArea` (the standard multiline field — auto-grows 2→10 rows via `minHeight`/`maxHeight`, NOT `onContentSizeChange`+fixed-height which froze at min on iOS; `forwardRef` for RHF focus; merge caller `style` as an array); `src/navigation/stackHeader.ts` `buildStackHeaderOptions(tokens)` = app-wide chevron-only back (`headerBackButtonDisplayMode:'minimal'`) + themed header chrome; DOB `DateField` = `@gorhom/bottom-sheet` content-height sheet (needs `GestureHandlerRootView`+`BottomSheetModalProvider` at root `_layout.tsx`) on iOS / Material calendar on Android. **Test gotcha:** a `jest.mock` factory that `createElement`s an RN component trips NativeWind's CSS-interop (`_ReactNativeCSSInterop` out-of-scope, hoist error) — return children/null from mock components instead. **Theming gotchas:** theme colors via inline `style={{color: tokens.X}}` NOT NativeWind `text-*` (`cn` has no tailwind-merge); rebuild `vars()` per render (a module-level memo breaks palette switching). **Error framework:** typed parser → `ValidationError`/`ConflictError`/`Network`/`Offline`/`Server`; `resolveQueryView` + `routeFormError` + `useRevalidateOnFocus`. **`QueryErrorState` connectivity copy (Task 039):** `network`+`timeout` (online-but-unreachable) → WifiOff icon + `errors.serverUnreachable` ("Can't reach AloVet", matching `ConnectivityBanner`), NOT the `errors.server` crash copy that mislabeled a connectivity outage as a backend fault; `offline`→`noInternet`, `404`→`notFound`, true 5xx→`server`. **Query reads need their own error UI (no global handler); drive staleness off `failureReason` not `isError`; the global `refetchOnMount:false` makes a server outage silent unless a query overrides it.** **Auth bridge cache purge (Task 040):** `onUnauthorized` (a forced 401-expiry after refresh is exhausted, `auth/bridge.ts`) clears BOTH the session AND the in-memory query cache (`queryClient.clear()`, mirroring each app's explicit-logout `useLogout`) — else the prior account's reads survive under account-agnostic keys (`['medic','profile']`, `['me']`) and a DIFFERENT account logging in within `staleTime` reads them (the medic-app cross-account gate-unlock + `useMe` store-overwrite leak; both apps). Backend coupling: hikari `connection-timeout=5000` must stay under the 15s client timeout [6]. **i18n:** `assets/locales/en.json` is the type source; every key in both en + ro. **App-content (Task 025):** `packages/core/content` (`useAppContent` — audience from `appRole`, `readQueryOptions`+`gcTime:Infinity`+long staleTime so the persisted cache serves chips offline; `pickLocale(name, i18n.language, fallbackCode)` — resolved-language keyed, NOT device locale) is the first FE consumer of the public `GET /api/app-content/{audience}` [9]; `@alovet/core/lib/{money,datetime}` (money `formatPriceMinor`, datetime `formatInstantInZone`/`formatInstantDateTimeInZone`/`todayInZone`/`shiftDate`/`CIVIL_ZONE`) were promoted here from medic-app-local so both apps share them (wall-clock→UTC two-pass idiom stays in the medic schedule feature). `ValidationError` now preserves the top-level `code` (additive) so screens branch on semantic 400s (e.g. `PET_NEEDS_REVIEW`). **Typed-routes gotcha:** `typedRoutes:true` — after adding a route, the gitignored `.expo/types/router.d.ts` must be regenerated (Metro's watcher misses container-side edits) or `tsc` fails on `Href`; headless regen via expo-router's `getTypedRoutesDeclarationFile` (see `frontend/CLAUDE.md`). **Task 028 primitive `@alovet/ui/WebPageSheet`** (generic in-app static-page viewer — RN `Modal` iOS `presentationStyle="pageSheet"` / Android full-screen, custom header with the doc title + a plain right-side X, `onShouldStartLoadWithRequest` blocks link taps for a read-only doc, `injectedJavaScript` sets a mobile viewport + readable font): exposed only as the `@alovet/ui/WebPageSheet` SUBPATH, NOT the main barrel — else a `@alovet/ui` barrel import would force-bundle react-native-webview into medic. **Native-module autolink gotcha (hoisted pnpm):** with `nodeLinker: hoisted`, Expo/RN autolinking keys off each APP's own `package.json` deps — a native module declared only in a shared `packages/*` is NOT linked (→ runtime `TurboModuleRegistry … could not be found`); declare it directly in `apps/owner`+`apps/medic` (kept in `packages/ui` too for source import/types). Adding it needs a real `expo prebuild` (regenerate native + pod install), not just `run:ios --build` (which only prebuilds when `ios/` is absent). **Task 029 refinement:** a native module used by only SOME apps must be an OPTIONAL `peerDependency` in the shared package, NOT `dependencies` — autolinking follows the dependency GRAPH, so a shared-package `dependencies` leaks the module into EVERY dependent app (Stripe leaked into medic via `medic→@alovet/core` despite no import — found on-device, medic iOS `Podfile.lock` 63→0 Stripe lines after moving `@stripe/stripe-react-native` to core `peerDependencies`; subpath-not-barrel stops only JS bundling, never native autolink — details in `frontend/CLAUDE.md`). Stripe RN SDK sits behind the `@alovet/core/lib/stripe` subpath facade (SetupIntent PaymentSheet, platform account, optional `EXPO_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`→keyless unwrapped). **Cross-platform WebView font gotcha:** Android WebView renders the same CSS markedly LARGER than iOS WKWebView, device-independently (NOT the system `font_scale` — that was 1.0; `textZoom` is a red herring) → size the injected body font PLATFORM-SPECIFICALLY (`vw`, iOS ~2× Android's value). See [2][14][15][17]; auth backend [6].

[14] **Dev Env, Build & Variants** - **owns:** the Docker `rn-dev` container, the macOS + Windows launcher scripts, Metro management, app-role variants, offline/release builds. **Canonical rule: ALL frontend commands run inside `rn-dev`, never the host** (macOS is a sanctioned orchestrate-from-host exception — Xcode/CocoaPods are macOS-only; the runners still exec into the container). Container ships Expo 54 / Node 24 / Java 21 / Android SDK 36; adb is wired to the host adb server; port 8081 is exposed via the compose port map (the host wsl-relay squats 8081). **Launchers:** Windows `scripts/dev-android.ps1` (Android-only), macOS `scripts/dev-android.sh` + `dev-ios.sh` (`--role owner|medic`, `--release`); idempotent — skip the build if installed, reuse a healthy Metro (never restart on a mere URL mismatch). **Native-fingerprint gate (Task 033):** the launchers auto-reprebuild + relink when a native-affecting input (deps / config plugins / app config / dependency patches) changed since the last successful build — via `@expo/fingerprint` (`scripts/native-fingerprint.js` + the sourced `scripts/_fingerprint.sh`; ps1 mirrors it), extending the existing role-switch reprebuild path with a `native_fp_changed` trigger and stamping `native_fp_write` after a successful build. Gated behind a cheap `find -newer` mtime pre-check (the ~9s fingerprint runs only when a manifest is newer) so fast-resume stays instant; per-`(role,platform)` stamp at `apps/<role>/.expo/.native-fingerprint-*`; iOS computes host-side, Android in-container (same env each platform builds in); escape hatch `ALOVET_SKIP_FP=1`. Closes the class of bug where a newly-added native module (Stripe, Task 029) ran against a stale/never-prebuilt binary → runtime `TurboModuleRegistry … could not be found`. A sibling **auto dep-sync** (`scripts/_deps.sh`, same mtime-gate pattern) runs `pnpm install` when a manifest changed (iOS: host+container trees; Android: container) BEFORE the fingerprint gate, so adding a module / merging a branch with new deps needs no manual dual `pnpm install` either (`ALOVET_SKIP_INSTALL=1`). **Backend URL by device:** emulator `10.0.2.2:8080`, physical `localhost:8080` via `adb reverse`, written to `frontend/.env` (authoritative — needs a `--clear` restart when it changes). **Order-independent iOS+Android (Task 034):** iOS-sim + Android-emulator share ONE Metro whose baked `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` can't be both `localhost` (iOS) and `10.0.2.2` (emulator), so whoever ran first used to poison the other (`dev-android.sh` then `dev-ios.sh` → iOS "Can't reach AloVet"). Fix = both launchers resolve the SAME loopback fallback `10.0.2.2` (`dev-ios.sh` fallback flipped `localhost`→`10.0.2.2`, matching the emulator's always-`10.0.2.2` so Metro-reuse never diverges) + an **iOS-always** runtime rewrite `10.0.2.2`→`localhost` on iOS in `packages/core/config/apiBaseUrl.ts` (the sole layer `apiClient.baseURL` binds; safe because iOS never legitimately needs `10.0.2.2`; **Task 038 dropped the original `__DEV__` gate — it had stranded `--release` iOS builds on the unroutable `10.0.2.2` → "Can't reach AloVet"**). Physical Android / LAN-IP override / staging-prod untouched (rule is iOS-only + host==`10.0.2.2`). **Task 039 refinement:** `dev-ios.sh` now bakes the FIXED loopback `10.0.2.2` directly (was `resolve_api_base_url`, which preferred the Mac's LAN IP when reachable) — a baked LAN IP went STALE when DHCP reassigned the Mac's address (the 2026-07-14 iOS-`--release` outage `.111`→`.70` black-holed every request into a 15s timeout). Fixed loopback is DHCP-immune + matches the emulator (better Metro sharing); the `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` override still serves a physical-iOS-on-LAN edge. Scope: `--release` + fresh/restart Metro (`ensure_env_url` self-heals a stale `.env`); dev Metro-REUSE still serves a stale LAN-baked bundle → needs `--restart-metro` (parked). **App variants:** two apps (owner/medic) selected by `EXPO_PUBLIC_APP_ROLE` (default owner) — idiomatic Expo app-variants, NOT native flavors. **Offline/`--release`:** Metro-less, JS as Hermes bytecode, debug-keystore signed; arm64 hermesc source-built in-image; traps = hermesc tag sync, autolinking role-poison, release cleartext block, **release API-URL poison (Task 038: the iOS `10.0.2.2`→`localhost` rewrite must NOT be `__DEV__`-gated, else release iOS can't reach the host)**, **release expo-router app-root (Task 041: `react.root` must be the app dir, not the workspace, or Android `--release` globs no routes → "No routes found" crash)**. **Apple Silicon:** arm64 NDK/aapt2, grpcfuse xattr self-heal, all gated on `TARGETARCH`. **Windows Metro 9P-watcher gap → stale bundle → `pnpm dev:reset`.** **Monorepo launchers (Task 021):** `--role` now selects the APP DIR (`apps/<role>`); Metro is reused WITHIN an app but RESTARTS on an app/role switch (two Expo project roots can't share one Metro — this is the role-divergence path in `decide_metro_action`); each app's `app.config.ts` defaults to its OWN role so a no-`.env` clone/CI never mis-builds identity. **Release-bundle trap (Task 021):** `expo export:embed` resolves `--entry-file` against Metro's server root = the workspace root (watchFolders), so the RN gradle plugin's app-dir-relative `index.js` fails — **`withMonorepoReactRoot`** config plugin pins `react.root` (debug is unaffected; this bites release only). **Task 041 CORRECTION:** it now pins `react.root` to the **APP dir**, NOT the workspace. `BundleHermesCTask` uses `react.root` as BOTH the export:embed CWD (→ Expo projectRoot → expo-router app-root) AND the `--entry-file` base; but `Os.cliPath` returns the entry ABSOLUTE on macOS/Linux, so the workspace root was never needed for `--entry-file` and it forced projectRoot=workspace → expo-router globbed `<workspace>/app` (empty) → `--release` Android crashed on launch with `Error: No routes found` (verified: 1.6 MB/0 routes at workspace root vs 9.2 MB/routes at app-dir root). A 4th `--release` trap in the list below. **Local config plugins** live in the workspace-shared `frontend/plugins/*.js` (both apps reference `../../plugins/…`) — the durable way to touch CNG-regenerated `android/` (a hand edit is wiped by `prebuild --clean`, which every role switch triggers): `withArm64Aapt2`/`withArm64Hermesc` (arm64, arch-guarded) + **`withCropToolbarColors`** (Task 020 — expo-image-picker ships its crop toolbar color TRANSPARENT, so light-mode black icons were invisible on the dark window chrome; this sets a solid white `expoCropToolbarColor` in `values/` only, leaving `values-night` = dark mode intact; platform-agnostic, no arch guard). Detail in `frontend/DOCKER.md`. See [2][13][15][16].

[15] **Owner App Screens** - **owns:** the owner-role RN screens — account, consent, pets, addresses, logout. Slice-1 owner side, all shipped and demo-driven on-device (the v3.3:38 script). **Account tab:** `useProfile` mirrors into the Zustand store; graceful degradation via `resolveAccountView` (renders cached store data on error, `failureReason`-driven staleness — see the data patterns in [13]). **Logout:** sign-out POSTs `/api/auth/logout` best-effort (try/catch/finally, not a mutation — avoids 401-interceptor recursion). **Consent (F1):** first consumer of `/api/consents` [9]. **Pets (F2):** `/api/pets` CRUD [9], the demo centerpiece. **Addresses (F3):** home-visit fields over the extended `/api/addresses` [9]. Pet photos stay device-local until S5 media [5]. **Form-UX polish (Task 019):** pet + address add/edit are fullscreen **push** (not modals) with a SINGLE **nav-bar** title — large titles were dropped (don't render at rest under New-Arch/Fabric, screens 4.16); the addresses LIST shows its nav-bar title + a `headerRight` "+" (`router.navigate`), no in-body h1. **F6a medic DISCOVERY (Task 025, Slice-3a read side — code+tests done, on-device verified):** pet-first `find-vet/` flow nested in the **`(home)` tab Stack** (`app/(owner)/(home)/`) so the picker is a PUSHED screen with the genuine NATIVE nav-bar back button — NOT a hidden-tab own-Stack (that made the picker a stack root with no native back; a custom `headerLeft` sat at the wrong inset — user-caught on-device). Home moved into the `(home)` group (URL-stripped, still the default route); hrefs are `/(owner)/(home)/find-vet…` — pet picker (needs-review pets shown disabled, no dead-end) → medic list (`useMedicSearch` over `/api/medics?petId&specialtyId&sort`, single `size=50` page + `hasNext` truncation hint, NOT infinite-scroll) → search-profile (`/api/medics/{id}`, read-only, disabled "Book" CTA stub for F6b at the stable `medic/[id]/book` route). `apps/owner/src/features/discovery/` (services/hooks/`discoveryView` resolver/`card.ts` `resolveRating`+`specialtyLabel`/`MedicCard`). Card render matrix: "Nou" badge (newMedic wins) / `★avg (count)` / neutral "no ratings yet" — every wire field OPTIONAL except `medicId`. Specialty filter chips from the app-content bundle's `specialties` (`.id`→query, `pickLocale(.name)`→label); sort = 4 SERVER-ranked modes (no client re-sort). **GOTCHAS:** (1) matching is test-isolated (INV-032, backend `a.isTest=:ownerIsTest`) — a REAL owner sees zero TEST-seeded medics → "no vets"; smoke-test as a test account; (2) demo seed is degenerate for sort (all 0-rating, identical availability, price∝id → every sort returns id-order — needs varied-rating seed to demo); (3) filter chip rows are full-bleed with the inset INSIDE the ScrollView (`contentContainerClassName` px) so first/last chip doesn't clip; (4) pull-to-refresh via `RefreshControl`. **F6b BOOKING FLOW (Task 026, Slice-3a write side — code+tests done, on-device verified):** the Book CTA now pushes `find-vet/medic/[id]/book` (slot picker) → `.../book/checkout` → `bookings/[id]` status (a `href:null` hidden tab with its OWN Stack, precedent pets/_layout). New `apps/owner/src/features/booking/` (services/hooks/`checkoutRef` idempotency-map/`countdown`/`slots`/`bookingView` resolver/`orderLine`+`OrderSummary`). Picker: 7-day strip (FE pilot constant — server availability is unbounded-by-date but a far-future date returns [], W2-verified), slots in Bucharest wall-time via `slotsForDay`+the core-promoted `dateInZone` [13]; tap→hold, 409 `SLOT_UNAVAILABLE`→inline re-pick, per-slot checkoutRef map so a retry/back-nav resumes the SAME hold. Checkout: hold read from the seeded cache by `holdId` (missing→hold-expired state, not a crash), mm:ss TTL countdown zero-cross→expired without a tap, confirm single-flight, 409→re-pick / 400·404→alert+back. Status: `useBooking` poll (v5 fn-form, focus+non-terminal gated) + `refetchOnMount` override; requested (24h decision deadline h:mm, elapsed→"checking status…", NEVER local-expire — the server sweep owns it) / booked / declined·expired (find-another CTA) / unknown-fallback keeps polling; `OrderSummary` renders server subtotal/fees/total VERBATIM + code→i18n line labels (`teleconsult_scheduled`, W2-corrected from the panel's guess). Push: `routeFromNotification` converted to `switch(data.type)` (booking_confirmed/declined/expired→`/bookings/[id]`, default preserves `/notifications/[id]`). **GOTCHA (from W8):** the `(home)` route GROUP hoists the find-vet subtree to the (owner) Tabs level → a stray icon-less `find-vet` tab (latent since the 025 migration); hide it with `<Tabs.Screen name="find-vet" href:null>` (group-ELIDED name — `(home)/find-vet` warns + fails). Booking-flow gotchas live in `frontend/CLAUDE.md`. **PARKED:** no owner bookings-list endpoint → no in-app return to a booking after leaving the status screen (backend-first, flag to Cristi). **Task 028 (3a demo-follow-ups, on-device verified):** (a) consent T&C/Privacy VIEWER — inline doc-name links (`ConsentAcceptLabel` via `<Trans>`, RO/EN word order) inside the gate + register checkbox labels, and the account-privacy row TITLES, open the shared `@alovet/ui/WebPageSheet` [13] through `useLegalDoc()` (docType→url+title; placeholder ringdoc.ro URLs pending S7 legal copy). (c) booking post-decline nav fixed at the ROOT: checkout confirm-success now `router.dismissAll()`+`navigate` (was cross-tab `replace`) so the `(home)` find-vet→book→checkout stack is UNMOUNTED — no surviving consumed-hold checkout, no unbounded decline→"find another slot" stacking, no picker "resume" banner for a spent hold; belt-and-suspenders `useHold` is now REACTIVE (`useSyncExternalStore` over the QueryCache) + confirm-success evicts the hold so any surviving/re-mounted checkout renders its expired branch. Screen conventions inherit [13]. **F7a CARD-SAVE (Task 029, Slice-3b — code+tests + ON-DEVICE both platforms, keyed test backend):** the status-screen order block gained `CardSaveCard` (ABOVE `OrderSummary`) driven by the pure `payment/cardSaveView` resolver over the WHOLE booking (renders when `order.status ∈ {draft,method_saved}` + a payment-dead-booking predicate — NOT `view.kind`); `useCardSave` orchestration is `retry:false` + a re-invocable pure branch table (start→retrieve SI→SKIP-sheet-if-already-succeeded→present PaymentSheet→verify; 503 `STRIPE_NOT_CONFIGURED`→static not_configured with the global toast suppressed via mutation `meta`; verify 409 branches on the preserved `ConflictError.code`). Stripe SetupIntent PaymentSheet → `order draft→method_saved`, chip "Unpaid"→"Card saved" WITHOUT nav (booking query invalidated via `bookingKey`). `OrderView.paymentStatus` additive; a `failed` "payment issue" chip only (F7c owns the fix CTA). Consumes [18]'s card-save contract; medic-Stripe-isolation fix in [13]. Verified on-device: happy path (Android+iOS), sheet-cancel (no verify), decline (sheet-owned, no verify), backend-503 + keyless→not_configured, medic ZERO Stripe pods. FINDING (accepted, Cristi flag): the SI's `automaticPaymentMethods` surfaces Link/Satispay/Klarna — true card-only needs backend `payment_method_types:['card']`. **F7b VOUCHER REDEMPTION (Task 030, Slice-3b — code+tests + ON-DEVICE Android + W0 live-backend round-trip):** the status-screen order block gained `VoucherEntry` (BETWEEN `CardSaveCard` and `OrderSummary`) — collapsed "Have a voucher code?" → single code `Input` + Apply, gated on the pure `voucher/voucherView` `voucherEntryVisible` (order ∈ {draft,method_saved} + booking ∈ {requested,booked} + `paymentStatus == null` + no discount line + `totalMinor > 0` — mirrors backend admission: ANY primary payment record blocks). `useRedeemVoucher` is **SEED-ONLY** (`cancelQueries` then `setQueryData(bookingKey)` from the returned full `BookingResponse`, NO invalidate — a poll GET must not clobber the seed; onError invalidates only on `VOUCHER_NOT_APPLICABLE`/`NotFoundError`). Pure `voucherErrorKind` maps the wire to inline states: `VALIDATION_FAILED`+fieldErrors[code]→field / opaque `VOUCHER_INVALID` (empty fieldErrors)→"invalid" / 409 `VOUCHER_LIMIT_REACHED`→limit / 409 `VOUCHER_NOT_APPLICABLE`→not-applicable (409s keyed on F7a-preserved `ConflictError.code`). `OrderSummary` discount line shows the raw voucher CODE as a `numberOfLines=1` detail under the localized "Discount", negative amount `-50 RON` pinned right. `cardSaveView` extended with a `not_required` state (`totalMinor === 0`, precedence) → CardSaveCard renders null + "covered by voucher" note — the 100%-voucher free path needs NO card. Idempotent same-code retry + all 4 error envelopes + free-path (total 0, order stays draft/paymentStatus null, async authorize backend-owned) CONFIRMED via the W0 live round-trip. Vouchers are FIXED-AMOUNT only (applied=min(face,price)); FE renders server lines/totals verbatim, never re-derives. Cristi flags: dev seeds ZERO vouchers (ops path `POST /api/admin/vouchers` + superadmin CLI); **same-day booking is broken backend-side** (`~/Downloads/BUG-same-day-booking-slot-unavailable.md` — availability emits now-anchored off-grid slots the hold endpoint 409s; future dates fine). **F7c RECOVERY + CANCEL (Task 031, Slice-3b — code+tests green, partial on-device) closes the owner 3b loop:** (1) decline recovery — F7a `useCardSave` re-invoked behind a `fix_payment` banner (replaces the neutral failed chip; async re-authorize → session "retrying" state); (2) owner CANCEL — `useCancelBooking`→`POST /api/bookings/{id}/cancel`, pure `cancelVisible` (booked-only + strict `now<windowStart`) + `cancelResult`/`cancelledCopy` render the server outcome VERBATIM (`no_charge`/`released`→"not charged", `captured`→exact `capturedAmountMinor` with null≠0 discipline, `already_terminal`→quiet; GENERIC pre-confirm copy — NO FE policy numbers), all source-confirmed against backend `CancellationDecision`/`CancellationOutcome` + a live 409 probe. `resolveBookingView` promotes `cancelled` out of `unknown` (all sources write plain `cancelled` — every wire outcome maps to two FE result copies). Cancel success seeds the booking cache to `cancelled` (immediate view flip, survives a failed refetch). **NAV (2 device-discovered fixes):** the hidden Bookings tab (`href:null`, only `[id]`, no list root) accumulated stale bookings — terminal-state "Find another slot" now `dismissAll`+`navigate`s to Home, and push routing switched `push`→`navigate` so NAVIGATE's route-name dedup keeps ONE `[id]` (back → Home); the screen is keyed by `bookingId` so the reused instance's per-booking mutation state can't leak across bookings (impl-panel regression fix). On-device: >2h released verified; <2h captured + decline recovery DEFERRED (Cristi's same-day-slot fix + a T-48h authorize-job knob). Flags to Cristi: `cancelReason`/actor + `capturedAmountMinor` on re-read (re-entry copy is generic without them), a policy-read surface (real cancel numbers), a bookings-list endpoint (a real Bookings-tab root). **F8a RATINGS owner side (Task 036, Slice 3c — code+tests, DEVICE-DEFERRED):** the completed-booking status screen gains a `RatingCard` (above OrderSummary) driven by pure `rating/ratingView.resolveRatingCard` over MUTATION state — **INVERTED design (panel): no durable rated-state cache** (`submitted` session-scoped, `already_rated` from the 409 code, `prompt` only on `completed`); `bookingViewKind` gained `completed`. New SHARED primitive **`StarRating` (`@alovet/ui`)** — pure `roundToHalfStar` (round-half + clamp) display + tap-to-set input (≥44pt targets, a11y). `useRateBooking` (`POST /api/bookings/{id}/rating`, global-default retry — POST is retry-safe) success → **single `['medics']` prefix invalidate** (covers search+profile+the reviews list, all under `['medics']`); `ALREADY_RATED`→card flip (no toast), `RATING_NOT_ALLOWED`→`bookingKey` invalidate+toast (documented Conflict-toast deviation). New reviews screen `find-vet/medic/[id]/reviews` (pushed on `(home)` stack, petId-forwarded, `ReviewRow` shared component, real empty + truncation + pull-to-refresh) reached from an ALWAYS-visible "Reviews (N)" profile row. Also hit a Flyway **V10 checksum mismatch** — a Pass-B mindmap commit edited a COMMENT in the applied `V10__push_tokens.sql`; repair the schema-history checksum or revert the comment (breaks any pre-existing DB). **F5b SHIPPED + on-device VERIFIED (Task 023):** medic schedule CRUD + computed day-view. Shape-exclusive block form (recurring weekday+`HH:mm` wall-clock XOR explicit date+time→UTC instant; unused shape OMITTED not null — `shapeValid` @AssertTrue counts present-null as "provided"). List grouped by TYPE (working/time-off, sorted weekday-then-time), day-view renders server-computed windows per channel (NO client math) + block-out subtraction. **TZ DISCIPLINE (load-bearing):** recurring times = wall-clock components (NEVER `toISOString`); explicit + all day-view instants convert via `Intl` `timeZone:'Europe/Bucharest'` (two-pass DST) — device-tz-INDEPENDENT; **Hermes `Intl` timeZone WORKS on Android** (day-view 06:00Z→09:00 verified). **GOTCHAS (F5b):** (4) synthetic 400 names `shapeValid`/`recurringTimeOrdered`/`explicitTimeOrdered` remapped to visible RHF slots + localized (`routeScheduleFormError`); (5) form Zod must be `mode:'onChange'` — the date/time pickers commit via onChange with NO blur, so `mode:'onBlur'` never fired the cross-field time-order check (end-before-start only errored on submit); (6) edit freshness gate MUST block on ANY refetch error — `isFetchedAfterMount` flips true on an ERRORED fetch too, so gating on it alone lets a stale list-cache seed become an editable full-replace PUT (the D6 hazard); (7) promoted `DateField` + net-new `TimeField` to `@alovet/ui` (were pets-local) — explicit shape = separate date+time fields (Android has no combined datetime mode). **VERIFIED on-device** (Android, real seed): list both-channel grouping, day-view windows-minus-block-out in Bucharest wall-time, edit prefill+freshness, delete round-trip. **PARKED** (Task 023): DST spring-forward/fall-back nonexistent-time rejection, not-a-medic state on the SCHEDULE screen's own resolver (day-view CLOSED by F7e Task 035; Profile+day-view had it — but the WHOLE per-tab question is now **CLOSED-by-mootness by Task 040's single authoritative gate**: an owner never reaches ANY tab), `DateField`/`TimeField` dedup, list virtualization. **F5c SHIPPED + on-device VERIFIED (Task 024) — Slice-2 medic app core COMPLETE:** presence toggle + Stripe payouts + the medic app's first push wiring. the countdown ticks 1s off the server `expiresAt` with a zero-crossing ONE-SHOT refetch (never a tight-loop vs the sweep); NO optimistic flip (`useSetPresence` setQueryData+invalidate, invalidate-on-error too so a 409 reconciles); `reserved`/`in_consultation` render read-only. **Stripe** payouts via the net-new `@alovet/core/lib/browser` facade (`openBrowser`, NO Stripe SDK) — the mutation `await`s the browser then invalidates (dismiss = "refresh on return"; no AppState/focus wiring exists). **Push** (F5a deferred it here): medic-local `notifications` (owner is app-local, so NOT a shared refactor) [17]. **GOTCHAS (F5c):** (8) presence `ConflictError` DROPS the `MEDIC_PRESENCE_CONFLICT` code (same class as the 404 code-drop) → key the 409 branch on `error instanceof ConflictError`, not the code; (9) the plain dev backend's Stripe status GET returns `NOT_STARTED` WITHOUT touching Stripe (no account) — the 503 fires on CONNECT-LINK, so "not configured" is a mutation-error state; (10) connect-link needed its OWN retry-stop-on-503 (`retryUnlessServiceUnavailable`, shared with the status query) — the global mutation retry retries 5xx → a ~7s backoff stall before the not-configured card (found on-device W7). **Panel-hardening (W9, gotchas 11-13):** (11) **`openBrowserAsync` resolves at OPEN time on Android** (`{type:'opened'}`, not dismiss — installed typings) → "refresh on return" CANNOT key on the browser promise; Payouts refetches Stripe status on `AppState`→`active`-while-focused instead (works both platforms); (12) a mounted-tab screen's own `setInterval`/imperative `refetch()` must be focus-gated (`useIsFocused`) — the query hook's focus-gating doesn't cover a screen-level tick, so the presence countdown ran + refetched on a blurred tab until gated; (13) presence toggle busts `['medic','profile']` too (the profile Availability badge reads the profile snapshot, not the presence query) + Payouts/Profile got `useRevalidateOnFocus` (tab screens stay mounted → `refetchOnMount` fires only once). Also: `expo-web-browser` now DECLARED in `@alovet/core` deps (4 other expo-* facades already were; `imagePicker` remains the undeclared exception). **VERIFIED on-device** (Android, real seed): toggle both ways, kill/reopen → server-truth Online + server-derived countdown, Stripe NOT_STARTED + connect-503 not-configured (+ the accepted global "server problem" toast). **PARKED → demo-env pass:** re-confirm push tap (needs shortened `APP_MEDIC_PRESENCE_*` TTL + FCM), `reserved` 409 (DB-flip), push-token row (FCM device), Stripe test-key full loop — all unit-covered. **F6c SHIPPED + two-phone on-device VERIFIED (Task 027) — closes FE Slice 3a:** medic booking-requests inbox + confirm/decline + day-view consults localization + `booking_request` push. canComplete=`(booked|in_progress) && now≥windowStart(UTC-Z, `Date.parse`)`, **compute-at-render, NO ticker** — server 409 + refetch-on-focus cover the boundary), `completeConsult`/`cancelConsult` services, `useCompleteConsult`/`useCancelConsult` hooks (on-SUCCESS `setQueriesData` PRUNE of the acted row from EVERY cached day-view date — terminal-vanish is statically proven `MedicDayViewService`; complete ALSO busts `['medic','earnings']`; `onSettled` **RETURNS/awaits** the day-view refetch so the screen's per-row lock spans reconciliation; deliberately NOT `['medic','booking-requests']`). `day-view.tsx` `ConsultRow`: Complete(primary)/Cancel(secondary, destructive `Alert` stating "owner not charged"), per-row lock = `Map<id,'complete'|'cancel'>` (both buttons render on a booked-post-slot row, so the value spins ONLY the tapped one), `useRevalidateOnFocus(true)` (now an action surface). **CAUSE-NEUTRAL 409 copy** — the two `INVALID_PAYMENT_STATE` causes (slot-not-started vs order-not-authorized) are indistinguishable by design (Cristi flag). **GOTCHAS (F7e):** (20) a 502 `STRIPE_ERROR` is a `ServerError` the global MutationCache toasts as a transient "server problem" blip → new **`meta.suppressPaymentErrorToast`** in `queryClient.ts` (mirrors Task-029 `suppressNotConfiguredToast`) suppresses the global toast for `STRIPE_ERROR`+any-503 so the hook shows a distinct "payment couldn't be processed" copy — branch MUST precede the `isTransient` fall-through; (21) day-view not-a-medic added to `resolveDayViewView` (404 + `data===undefined`, not-a-medic-FIRST like `inboxView`; a background 404 with data stays content-stale) → owner-in-medic-app gets the shared `NotAMedic` full-screen — the **SCHEDULE** screen's own resolver stays PARKED. Tests: consults services/resolver/hooks + day-view screen + retry-lock + queryClient suppress-flag (medic 255/255, owner core green). **DEVICE-DEFERRED (W7):** backend uses REAL `Instant.now()` (NO clock-shift override exists) → seed consult windows RELATIVE TO REAL NOW; verify Complete→capture toast+row-gone+earnings, cancel→released, pre-slot 409, free-voucher complete, not-a-medic — needs device + Stripe-keyed backend. **F8a RATINGS medic side (Task 036, Slice 3c — code+tests, DEVICE-DEFERRED):** new `apps/medic/src/features/ratings/` — self-list (`GET /api/medic/ratings`, single `?size=50` page, **NO `sort` — order hardcoded server-side, W1**) + one-time reply (`POST .../{ratingId}/reply`); `resolveRatingsView` not-a-medic-FIRST (`inboxView` precedent); `MedicRatingRow` (stars/comment/date/author-`formerUser`-fallback + existing-reply display OR an inline expandable composer — controlled `TextArea`, 2000 counter, send disabled when `reply.trim()` empty/>2000/pending, NOT RHF); `useReplyToRating` success→invalidate `['medic','ratings']`, `ALREADY_REPLIED`→neutral toast (documented Conflict-toast deviation), unknown-409/404→generic, transient→global. Screen `app/(medic)/ratings.tsx` = `href:null` Profile drill-in (Star row after Earnings), per-row lock via **`mutateAsync().finally()`** (proactively applies the Task-035 F1 shared-observer lesson). i18n `medic.ratings.*` (en+ro). Uses the shared `StarRating` `@alovet/ui` primitive [15]. See [10][15]. **F8b SUMMARY medic write slice (Task 037, Slice 3c — code+tests, SCREEN PARKED):** new `apps/medic/src/features/summary/` — `fileSummary(interactionId, petId, text)` → `POST /api/medic/consults/{id}/summary` body `{petId,text}`; `useFileSummary` (success = local confirmation, nothing cross-app to invalidate; `SUMMARY_ALREADY_FILED`→neutral info toast, `SUMMARY_NOT_ALLOWED`→distinct window-closed, 404→generic+log, transient→global). i18n `summary.*` (incl. the parked prompt/compose keys). **The compose SCREEN + complete→summary chain (W6) is PARKED** — the medic app has NO source of a consult's petIds (no consult-detail endpoint; see the [11] Cristi ask) and 035 isn't merged; the slice is ready to wire once that endpoint ships. See [11][15]. **F040 — SINGLE AUTHORITATIVE post-login medic gate (Task 040, code+tests, device-deferred):** `app/(medic)/_layout.tsx` probes `/api/medic/profile` ONCE and branches BEFORE the tab shell — a current 404 → the shared `NotAMedic` (**AUTHORITATIVE: overrides cached `data`**, deliberately NOT the display resolver's data+404→content-stale, else a cached medic profile would unlock the tabs for an owner after an in-memory account switch / mid-session revocation — plan-panel Critical), transient→`QueryErrorState` retry (a real medic isn't locked out by a blip), loading→spinner, content→the extracted **`<MedicTabs>`** (Tabs shell + `useRegisterPushNotifications` moved inside — so a non-medic NEVER registers an FCM token). Conditional RENDER not `<Redirect>` (locked rule; cites the `app/_layout.tsx:71` null-layout precedent). Root-cause: the `Stack.Protected isMedic` guard is a build constant (always true in the medic build), so medic-ness is only knowable at runtime. This makes the per-tab not-a-medic inconsistency MOOT + CLOSES the Task-023 Schedule/Presence parked items. `useMe` stays UNCONDITIONAL in the layout (parallel, no waterfall). `medic.notAMedic.body` reworded (en+ro) to point owners at the owner app. Accepted trade-offs: every-cold-launch loading gate before the tab bar (persisted profile restores fast), push tap-routing gated behind `content` (cold-launch-from-push during a transient profile error lands on retry until reconciled, self-heals), offline-first-launch=loading (networkMode pause). **Impl-panel Critical (8/9 judges):** the authoritative-404 override ALONE was insufficient — a FRESH cached profile (no current 404) still mounted the shell + registered push for a DIFFERENT account, because `onUnauthorized` (401-expiry) cleared only the SESSION, not the query cache (account-agnostic key `['medic','profile']`, 60s staleTime; a medic-expiry→owner-login within 60s read the prior cache). ROOT FIX: `bridge.ts onUnauthorized` now `queryClient.clear()`s — SYMMETRIC with explicit logout — closing the whole cross-account in-memory-cache-leak class (incl. `useMe` overwriting the new user's store) [13]. The `content`-from-cache fast-restore is therefore trustworthy: auth transitions purge the cache, so any cached `data` belongs to the CURRENT account. Test = `_layout.test.tsx` (6 branches incl. the data+404 Critical guard, push-only-for-medic, transient-stale-content-keeps-tabs) + `auth/__tests__/bridge.test.ts` (onUnauthorized purges the cache). DEVICE-DEFERRED (Marius): confirm the non-navigator layout resolves cleanly (no expo-router "must render a navigator" warning) + a real-medic push deep-link still routes once content mounts; if it misbehaves, escalate to the root-`Stack.Protected`+sibling-route fallback [13]. Follows app-variant infra [14] + foundation patterns [13]. See [10][14][13][11][15][5][18].

[17] **Push Notifications** - **owns:** the FCM push rail, both platforms. Backend token registry + `FcmClient` (gated, no-ops when credentialless) + a frontend RNFirebase/Notifee facade [13]. **Decisions:** direct FCM not Expo; a `notification` payload NOT data-only; NORMAL priority (HIGH duplicates on a killed dev client); bind listeners once. **iOS push** is enabled end-to-end (system push requires the Xcode signing Team), landed alongside cross-platform build durability [14]. **Correction:** `app.firebase.enabled` defaults **TRUE** — a credentialless backend FAILS to start (tests force it false via `application-test.yml`). Consumed by the medic presence re-confirm push [10]. **Push outbox (Task 098, audit F3):** booking writers (`BookingService.confirm`, all `BookingDecisionService` paths) no longer call `sendToSelf` inline inside their `@Transactional` row-locked methods (extended the lock-hold; a phantom push could fire for a rolled-back booking). They `PushNotificationOutbox.enqueueToSelf(...)` a `push-notification` job in the SAME tx (durable, atomic with the state change) and `PushNotificationJobHandler` dispatches AFTER commit via the engine [8]. Contract: at-least-once post-commit dispatch **attempt** — NOT idempotent/reliable (per-token FCM stays best-effort; a crash between send and success-commit re-displays, the documented reconfirm replay window). The G1 boundary-rules task closed the parked direct senders: `MedicPresenceReconfirmHandler` now enqueues via the outbox in its per-job tx (staleness check moved to enqueue time — one benignly-stale prompt possible in the worker-poll window, same race the inline send had) and `PaymentDeclineNotifier` enqueues in a PROGRAMMATIC `REQUIRES_NEW` tx (`TransactionTemplate` INSIDE its catch — impl-panel: declarative `@Transactional` would surface a failed enqueue INSERT as `UnexpectedRollbackException` OUTSIDE the catch, replacing `AuthorizeJobHandler`'s intended dead-letter `PermanentJobException` with an engine retry-storm; REQUIRES_NEW itself is load-bearing: the prompt's row must commit while the authorize job's tx dead-letter-rolls-back — both proven by `DeclineRecoveryIntegrationTest.hardDecline_throughTheEngine_…` + `enqueueDbFailure_neverEscapesTheNotifier`). `NotificationController` (debug) is the deliberate remaining direct send — authenticated, self-scoped, rate-limited, NO transaction, so the F3 mode doesn't exist there and the R1 rule doesn't match it. The ArchUnit rules ([12]) now enforce all this at build time: `PushNotificationJobHandler` is the ONE R2-exempt FCM dispatcher. **First FE medic-side consumer SHIPPED (F5c, Task 024 [16]):** the medic app got its OWN app-local `notifications` feature (mirrors owner — notifications wiring is per-app, NOT shared core), with `routeFromNotification` = `switch(data.type)` → `presence_reconfirm` deep-links the presence tab (no owner `/notifications/[id]` branch, no `isMedic` gate — inherently the medic app); registration mounted in the authed layout, deregister on logout. **Owner payment pushes routed (F7c, Task 031 [15]):** `routeFromNotification` handles `payment_failed` + `booking_cancelled_payment_unresolved` → `/bookings/[id]` via `router.navigate` (NOT push — keeps the hidden Bookings tab single-instance so back → Home); the medic app routes `booking_cancelled_payment_unresolved` → day-view (invalidate-first, neutral IDs-only landing). `payment_failed` fires only on the SYNC T-48h hard-decline (webhook authorized→failed does NOT push — a `failed` status can appear with no push, so the screen stands alone). Full gotchas in `frontend/CLAUDE.md` + overflow. See [6][13][10][14].

[18] **Payments & Money** - **owns:** the money model — the consult split, commission, voucher funding, and the monthly medic reconciliation. Split off from [11] (which flagged payments would spawn its own node). **Charge type (Task 082, O8 RESOLVED):** Stripe **DIRECT charges** — the **vet is merchant of record** (invoices the owner); the platform's commission is the live `application_fee_amount`; the platform invoices the vet for it. Supersedes the earlier separate-charges-&-transfers posture. **Split (`money/MoneySplit` — pure, `long` bani, INV-003):** `ownerCharge = price − voucher`; `platformFee = max(0, commission − voucher)` (the voucher eats the platform's commission first, floors at 0); `commission = floor(price·bps/10000)` on the **pre-voucher** price (corrected the old post-discount `:173` rule). **Reconciliation (`money/ReconciliationService`; `GET /api/admin/reconciliation?month=yyyy-MM`, capability `VIEW_PLATFORM_RECONCILIATION` = platform-finance/superadmin):** monthly, per medic — makes the medic whole to `price − commission` via a **manual** platform→medic payment = `Σ max(0, voucher − commission)`. Grain = completed Interaction joined to its Order (Order has no medic FK); period key = the `completed` `InteractionStatusEvent.occurredAt` bucketed in `PilotZone` (Europe/Bucharest); excludes `isTest` + null-commission (logged); voucher = a **negative** `discount` OrderLine (positive = corrupt). Fee-free pilot form; the platform **absorbs Stripe fees**, trued up in reconciliation once the live flow reports them. **Merchant onboarding (Task 083, [10]) + payment schema/config (Task 084) DONE:** connected accounts are `card_payments` Merchant accounts; `pricing_config` carries per-interaction-type commission bps + `PricingService.resolveCommissionRateBps` (V39; pilot **20% placeholder**, ops-tunable — PRD O2); `PaymentRecord` reshaped for direct charges (`stripeFeeMinor`/`applicationFeeAmountMinor`; vestigial `payoutTransferId`/`payoutAmountMinor` dropped, V40). **Charge flow A+B DONE (Task 085, `payment/` pkg, live-verify IOU):** the test-mode direct-charge flow behind a `StripePaymentGateway` seam (`app.stripe.enabled=false`→503; sibling of `StripeConnectGateway`). **card-save** (`POST /api/bookings/{id}/card-save[/verify]`; **platform** Customer + SetupIntent per W1 — owner `stripe_customer_id` V41 — saved PM cloned onto the medic's account per charge; `Order draft→method_saved`) → **authorize** at T-48h (`payment-authorize` JobHandler enqueued in the `booked` transition tx, `runAt=slotStart−48h`; snapshots `commissionRateBps`+`payeeStripeAccountId` on the Order; PaymentIntent **manual-capture** on the medic account with `application_fee_amount`; `card_payments`-active merchant guard) → **capture** at completion (`POST /api/medic/consults/{id}/complete`; fires session_start/ended, captures, writes `commissionMinor`/`stripeChargeId`/`stripeFeeMinor` — **makes the reconciliation report live**; **FE SURFACE LIVE (code-complete, device-verify pending): the medic day-view Complete action [16], Task 035/F7e — the money loop's on-device capture trigger; medic cancel [16] is its release sibling**) → **release** (`AuthorizationReleaseService` voids the hold on any non-completed terminal; production callers = the Task-087 cancel/no-show orchestrators). All money ops idempotent (deterministic Stripe keys + PaymentRecord `idempotency_key` UNIQUE); **no Stripe call inside a write tx** — readOnly-loader / `NOT_SUPPORTED`-orchestrator / `REQUIRES_NEW`-writer split (extends the MedicStripeService precedent to suspend the job tx). **Webhooks + refunds DONE (Task 086, increment C):** one platform-level Connect webhook endpoint `POST /api/stripe/webhook` (permitAll, `Webhook.constructEvent` HMAC verify on the raw body, one `app.stripe.webhook-secret`; connected-account events carry `account`) routing through the existing `StripeEventDedupeService`; handlers **reconcile + backfill ONLY, never re-drive** the interaction lifecycle — `payment_intent.succeeded` backfills the settled `stripeFeeMinor` (`fetchChargeFee`), `canceled`/`payment_failed` reconcile an authorized-not-captured record, unknown-PaymentRecord throws → 503 → Stripe re-delivers (not consumed). **Refunds:** `Refund`/`RefundStatus` (V42, `stripe_refund_id` UNIQUE = durable idempotency anchor) + `refund` gateway op (`refund_application_fee` per `app.payments.return-commission-on-refund`; no `reverse_transfer` on direct charges); ops endpoint `POST /api/admin/refunds` (capability `ISSUE_REFUNDS_RESOLVE_DISPUTES`, authz-before-lookup + audit); ONE locked `RefundWriter` shared by the ops path AND the `refund.created`/`refund.updated` webhook (dashboard-initiated converges), setting the primary PaymentRecord `refunded`/`partially_refunded` and deriving Order status (projection rule). **Refund status is monotonic (Task 097, audit F2):** `RefundWriter.isAllowedTransition` gates the existing-row write — only `pending→succeeded|failed|canceled` + the documented late `succeeded→failed` are applied; every regression (above all a late out-of-order `refund.created(pending)` arriving after `refund.updated(succeeded)` — distinct event types, so per-event-id dedupe doesn't cover them) is refused (logged, event still CONSUMED so Stripe won't retry) rather than downgrading a refunded order back toward captured; the succeeded-total ≤ captured invariant now guards the `pending→succeeded` transition too, not only inserts (shared `requireWithinCaptured`). REGISTERED as INV-039 by the G3 task, alongside INV-040 (Σsucceeded ≤ captured, both write paths, over-capture rejected NOT consumed), INV-041 (voucher discount bounds), INV-042 (MoneySplit conservation + exact overflow oracle) and INV-043 (webhook-permutation convergence, seeded) — the registry grew 38→43 ([12]). **Cancellation/no-show DONE (Task 087):** `cancellation_policy` per-kind config (V43; scheduled_consult 120min/50%/100%/10min-grace — seeded ONLY for that kind; `instant`/`home_visit` rows land with their consuming tasks) + pure `payment/CancellationDecision` driving three surfaces: owner `POST /api/bookings/{id}/cancel` (free release strictly before slotStart−cutoff; LATE capture from the cutoff instant; **REJECTED at/after slotStart** — else an absent owner games 50% instead of the 100% no-show), medic `POST /api/medic/consults/{id}/cancel` (ALWAYS full release), ops `POST /api/admin/consults/{id}/no-show` (capability `ISSUE_REFUNDS_RESOLVE_DISPUTES`, grace-guarded, party=owner→100% capture / medic→release; the audit row is the pilot reliability flag — ops-only because no-video has no automatic no-show signal). **Policy captures collect ZERO commission** (decision 2026-07-08; gateway capture gained a nullable `applicationFeeOverrideMinor`). **Two orderings that matter:** release paths fire the terminal FIRST and leave the order authorized — `release()`'s writer settles it (marking cancelled beforehand makes release a silent no-op and strands the hold); capture legality is decided BEFORE the Stripe call, and crash-repair is ALWAYS release-only (impl-panel: capture paths commit terminal+record atomically, so a terminal with a still-authorized order can only be an unreleased release — re-deriving free-vs-late there would launder a medic's crashed cancel into an owner 50% charge). **Reconciliation gained a second ledger:** captured policy terminals reimburse the settled `stripeFeeMinor` (medic keeps the full captured amount); `policyCaptureCount` + `excludedPendingFeeCount` on the report; deliberate fee asymmetry vs completed consults — do NOT merge the branches when the completed-consult fee term lands. **Vouchers DONE (Task 088, `voucher/` pkg):** Voucher/VoucherRedemption (V44; one-redemption-per-order UNIQUE; V44 also added the `payment_records(order_id) WHERE purpose='primary'` partial-unique — the one-primary-per-order backstop). Owner `POST /api/bookings/{id}/voucher` (interaction-row lock FIRST, then voucher-row lock — the card-save/authorize serialization seam; idempotent same-code retry; caps count `redeemed` rows only), ops `POST|GET /api/admin/vouchers` (FIRST consumer of `MANAGE_VOUCHERS_PROMOTIONS`; `fundedBy=payee` REJECTED at creation until its distinct split lands). Redemption = negative `discount` OrderLine + INV-008 totals recompute (`order/OrderTotals`, shared with BookingService); **`money/OrderMoneyLines.summarize` is THE single price/voucher line-aggregation rule** (reconciliation + authorize + capture + redeem — never hand-copy it). Authorize prices from LINES (never `totalMinor` — post-discount once a discount line exists!), keys the PI with an **amount-fingerprinted idempotency key** and the writer **re-derives the split under the interaction lock** (a redemption racing the unlocked pricing read → stale authorize refused, retry mints a fresh PI, the orphan auto-expires); capture re-derives `commissionMinor` from the snapshot rate on the pre-voucher price (≠ appFee once a voucher exists). **100%-voucher FREE consult: zero Stripe objects ever** — a zero-amount null-PI `authorized` record (ONE uniform shape recognized by the completion/release/cancellation guards), no card-save, deliberately no merchant requirement. Every non-captured terminal (decline, hold-expiry, cancel, release) flips the redemption → `released` (voucher reusable); policy captures keep it spent. **Decline recovery DONE (V45):** the sync T-48h hard decline records the declined PI on the failed marker + a **`payment_intent_attempts` ledger row** (every PI the platform ever creates gets one; webhook lookup falls back to it — known-but-superseded PI → ack, unknown → the 086 retryable posture; without it a retry would orphan the declined PI into a webhook 503-loop) and pushes the owner (`payment_failed`, IDs-only, SYNC path only);
