wire signal = `BookingResponse.order.paymentStatus` (`failed` = the app's fix-payment CTA). **Retry** = re-run card-save (admitted on `method_saved`+failed-primary; verify is PM-ref-idempotent; the marker's `attempt`++ under the interaction lock → authorize re-enqueued, a DEAD job frees its dedupe key); **`payment/PaymentAttemptKeys` is THE key derivation** — attempt-0 byte-identical `-v1`, recovery `-r{n}` (a Stripe idempotency key permanently pins its FIRST result including a decline — never reuse one across attempts); the authorize writer UPDATEs the failed marker in place (one-primary V44 never challenged; attempt-mismatch refused like a re-price). **T-12h sweep** (`payment-cancel-unauthorized`, enqueued WITH the authorize in the booked tx; runAt = max(now + 2h recovery floor, slot − 12h), deliberately not capped at slot): a still-unauthorized booking gets the free-terminal cancel (zero Stripe — incl. never-card-saved bookings, closing the uncharged-no-show gap; voucher released; both parties pushed best-effort AFTER the durable write); a lagging 100%-voucher free order **self-heals** (local free authorize) instead of cancelling; lost-race-to-authorize is an OUTCOME (`lost_race_funded`), not an exception; a live retry-authorize DEFERS the sweep as a `deferred` outcome + a keyless follow-up job (~15m, `cancelSweepDeferInterval`) — NEVER a thrown engine-retry (the ~150s backoff budget would dead-letter and strand the booking; impl-panel Critical) — and every card-save re-arms the keyed sweep's run_at via `JobService.reschedule` (enqueue's dedupe coalescing deliberately never moves it). **Earnings ledger DONE (the last 3b pilot bullet — medic-facing read):** self-scoped `GET /api/medic/earnings?month=yyyy-MM` (`money/MedicEarningsService`+`Controller`, non-medic 404 like day-view — `VIEW_OWN_EARNINGS` stays matrix documentation) over the SAME grain+derivation as reconciliation (completed events + captured policy terminals, `OrderMoneyLines`+`MoneySplit`) so the medic's view and Finance's report can't drift; completed entries require a SETTLED primary record (captured|partially_refunded|refunded; unsettled = corruption, excluded+counted), policy entries enter at capture with NO fee gate and STAY VISIBLE after refunds (settled ORDER statuses — the refund projection would otherwise vanish them); `earned = price − commission` (the make-whole target, NOT live net), refunds surfaced succeeded-only and **UN-NETTED** (user 2026-07-09 — netting is ONE parked product decision to land on BOTH money read-surfaces together; reconciliation equally un-netted AND still drops refunded policy captures — same parked task); `settledBy` from the frozen `Order.payeeType` (always `self` until clinic-payee routing lands — never derive from `Medic.clinicId`, that would claim a settlement that didn't happen); `month` REQUIRED, fixed `page`/`size` (deliberately NO Pageable — springdoc would advertise a dead `sort`), deterministic `occurredAt desc, id desc` order (stable pages); keeps the `isTest=false` filter (user decision — demo medics see an empty ledger; workaround parked with the 3b demo). **INSTANT PATH SHIPPED (Task 109, S4 4/4 — deferred item E resolved):** owner-level payment method (V51 `owners.saved_payment_method_ref` + `pm_setup_attempt`-versioned keys; `POST /api/payment-methods/setup|verify`, always-allowed replacement; booking card-save ALSO writes it) feeds the INV-037 creation guard (typed 409 `PAYMENT_METHOD_REQUIRED`, zero rows — C13: SCA never inside matching); the instant order is PRICED AT CREATION (platform-standard consult line — a line-less order would authorize 0) and starts `method_saved`. **Confirm = the on-session authorize** (`POST /api/instant-requests/{id}/confirm` + `/confirm/verify`, payment-pkg controllers — payment→dispatch only, no cycle): NOT_SUPPORTED orchestrator, resumable loader (medic_accepted first-entry | confirmed resume | settled replay), gateway grew `onSession`+`clientSecret`+`retrievePaymentIntent` (additive); success writer = record+order+`authSucceeded` ONE tx; declines reuse the failed-marker/attempt-ledger/`-r{n}`-key recovery (owner re-saves owner-level, re-confirm re-copies the CURRENT ref); SCA outcome is VERIFY-driven, never webhook-driven (abandoned-SCA orphan hold auto-expires — the re-priced-orphan posture, documented). **P0-C2 completion** (`POST /api/medic/instant-consults/{id}/complete`): decision FROZEN under the interaction lock (new `payment_records` decision columns + `capture_adjustment IS NULL` CAS + durable `payment-instant-settlement` repair duty enqueued in the SAME tx) BEFORE Stripe; waive<5m / reduce<10m server-enforced (`PaymentProperties`, ops-tunable), sub-60s full needs attestation (INV-012's gate, ledger-duration proxy until 3c video), commission on the ADJUSTED pre-voucher base, waive = captured-0 + PI void (order still `captured`, :158); presence in_consultation→online restore (`restoreOnlineAfterConsultation` CAS). **Fault seam** `InstantFaultSettlementService.technicalFailure(fault, billableSec)` (no HTTP — 3c calls it): terminal + frozen decision + duty in ONE tx; platform/party<2m → release (voucher back), party≥2m → pro-rata capture. **Funded termination port** (`payment/FundedInstantOrderTermination`): authorized → order STAYS authorized at terminal + release duty (release-ordering rule); `AuthorizationReleaseService` terminals grew `no_supply`. Vouchers redeem on instant in open|medic_accepted ONLY (post-confirm FENCED — amounts freeze at confirm); 100%-voucher = zero Stripe end-to-end. INV-037/INV-011 flipped covered; INV-012/013 reasons updated. **Still DEFERRED:** on-session add-ons (D), decline-recovery UI (Marius; contract + `payment_failed`/`booking_cancelled_payment_unresolved` push types shipped), reconciling the async webhook authorized→failed Order divergence (unrecoverable-by-card-save state; parked in the decline-recovery task), duration-gated capture waive/reduce, disputes/chargebacks (C17), owner cancel of a `requested` booking (spec gap — the machine has no such transition), payee-funded voucher split semantics, voucher-refund policy on captured orders, and voucher redemption-abuse monitoring (spec: ops statistic). Spec: `docs/specs/alovet-data-model-v2.7.md` §Money. See [11][10][7][12][5]. (Node well over the ~250-word budget — overflow-flip candidate.)

## Legacy pre-Pass-B per-task archive (kept 2026-07-06)

The verbatim 58-node per-task archive as it stood at Pass B (commit a853c7b). Fenced so `[N]` lines here are NOT parsed as live nodes and their in-prose refs are ignored. Old→new node mapping: `.agent/marius/tasks/018-mindmap-passb-subsystem-grouping/mapping.md`.

```text
# Mind Map — Overflow (full archive)

> Full text of every node, single-line canonical form. `MIND_MAP.md` is the working view (routing + anchor nodes full, others summarized with ↗). Keep the two in sync via `tasks mindmap-sync` (`--fix` copies main→overflow for full nodes that drift).

[1] **Project Overview** - AloVet is a consultation-first veterinary marketplace (Phase 1 remote vet services; Phase 2 merchandise shop), built AI-driven via the playbook workflow [3]. Full-stack: an Expo/React-Native mobile app [13] in two role variants (owner + medic [14]) over a Spring Boot REST backend [6]. Architecture in [2], current status in [4], roadmap/sequence in [5]. **Pivot 2026-06-14:** the generic full-stack foundation was tagged `v0-foundation` (`ba0017d`) as a fork point; `main` now evolves toward AloVet (specs in `docs/`, exec strategy + plans in `docs/specs/`). Backend subsystems: identity/auth/policy [6], domain schema [7], execution kernel [8], owner product APIs [9], medic subsystem [10], consult lifecycle [11], contracts/seed/test-infra [12]. Frontend subsystems: foundation & patterns [13], dev-env/build/variants [14], owner screens [15], medic screens [16]. Cross-cutting: push [17]. Reference index: `CLAUDE.md` (workflow rules + CLI), `MIND_MAP_OVERFLOW.md` (full per-node archive + legacy history), `.agent/tasks/` (execution traces), `.agent/playbooks/` (reusable patterns).

[2] **Architecture** - **Frontend** (`./frontend/`) — Expo 54 / RN 0.81 / React 19 / Expo Router 6 mobile app [13], containerized dev env [14]. **Backend** (`./backend/`) — Spring Boot 4 / Java 21 / PostgreSQL, JWT-secured stateless REST API [6], package-by-feature. **Workflow** (`./.agent/`, `./.claude/`) — playbook task management + Claude Code config [3]. The contract seam is `backend/openapi.yaml` [12] — the frontend/backend agreement, backend-leads one step ahead. Two-dev layer split [3]: Cristian owns `backend/`, Marius owns `frontend/`. Live day-to-day conventions live in `backend/CLAUDE.md` + `frontend/CLAUDE.md` (auto-loaded per subtree); this map is the *why*. See [1].

[3] **Development Workflow** - Playbook task system via `.claude/bin/tasks` (lifecycle design → work plan → pre-review; full CLI in CLAUDE.md). **The mind map is updated per task by editing the OWNING subsystem node in place** (Pass B rule, Task 018 — see header + ownership map). **Two-dev split (from 2026-07-01):** work divided by layer — Cristian `backend/`, Marius `frontend/` — coordinated contract-first, backend-leads (`backend/openapi.yaml` [12] is the agreement, backend one step ahead; the error envelope `{status,message,code,fieldErrors,details}` with stable `code` constants is part of the contract). Isolation: branch-per-person → merge to `main` via `/playbook:merge`; `.agent/<user>/` state is per-person. Fewer, subsystem-keyed nodes make mind-map merges same-node content-merges with rare renumbers. Panel reviews (plan + impl) replace single-judge reviews. See [1][2][5].

[4] **Project Status** - Backend production-hardened (Task 007, 2026-05-15) and extended with auth-contract prereqs (Task 013, closed 2026-05-18): structured fieldErrors + refresh-token rotation + extended register payload (phone + acceptedTermsAt). **Task 017 (backend review fixes, 2026-05-20)**: fixed JWT expiration (was hardcoded 24h in application.yml, now `${JWT_EXPIRATION:900000}` default 15 min); fixed refresh_tokens TIMESTAMP→TIMESTAMPTZ (V4 migration, Hibernate 7 maps Instant to TIMESTAMPTZ); added `AuthFlowIntegrationTest`. **Task 018 (embedded-postgres, 2026-05-20)**: replaced Testcontainers with `io.zonky.test:embedded-postgres:2.0.7` — integration tests now run via plain `mvn test` inside Docker on any OS, no Docker socket needed (`-u 1000 -e HOME=/root` required: PostgreSQL initdb refuses root). 52 tests, 0 skipped. **Task 019 (Flyway autoconfig fix, 2026-05-20)**: restored Flyway in `AuthFlowIntegrationTest` under `ddl-auto: validate`. Root cause was NOT embedded-postgres-specific — SB4 modularized auto-configuration: `FlywayAutoConfiguration` moved out of `spring-boot-autoconfigure` into the separate `spring-boot-flyway` module that ships only via `spring-boot-starter-flyway`. The pom previously declared `flyway-core` directly, so Flyway was on the classpath but the autoconfig wiring wasn't — Spring Boot silently skipped Flyway in dev/prod/test alike. Fix: replace `flyway-core` with `spring-boot-starter-flyway`. Schema-drift validation now active. **Frontend foundation closed 2026-05-18 (Task 012)** — runtime-verified on Pixel 8, end-to-end backend-integrated, 21-test jest suite + husky pre-commit live. **Task 002 (frontend MVP) closed 2026-05-20**: register now sends phone+acceptedTermsAt, error parser migrated to structured fieldErrors[] (details[] fallback retained), refresh-token persisted + 401-retry interceptor live, addresses feature complete (list/create/edit/delete with optimistic delete). 24 frontend tests pass. **Task 026 (test coverage, 2026-05-20)**: backend 52→59 tests (AddressRequest @NotBlank validation ×3, AuthService.refresh() unit ×2, integration unauthenticated-401 regression guard + address CRUD via real DB); frontend 24→34 tests (useRegister ×2, useCreateAddress ×2, useUpdateAddress ×1, client.ts 401-interceptor ×5 via `axios-mock-adapter` devDep); fixed pre-existing env.test.ts failure (babel cache + jest.config.js env seed); added `spring-security-test` to pom.xml (needed to apply security filter chain in `AuthFlowIntegrationTest`); fixed client.ts interceptor infinite-recursion bug (added URL-exclusion guard so `/api/auth/refresh` responses never trigger a recursive refresh attempt). **Task 027 (auth security hardening, 2026-05-21)**: backend 59→91 tests. Per-account lockout (5 wrong → 15-min lock → 423 ACCOUNT_LOCKED + Retry-After) + per-IP Bucket4j rate limiting on /api/auth/{login,register,refresh} (default 100/min, 429 RATE_LIMITED + actual time-to-refill from ConsumptionProbe). Six new HTTP integration test classes anchored on attack surface (forged/expired/malformed JWT, refresh-token replay/expiry/unknown/malformed, cross-user authorization 404 on others' addresses, login user-enumeration parity, validation field-error aggregation). See [12]. Deploy/CI-CD (Task 003) pending. See [7][9][10][11][12] for architecture details.

[5] **Roadmap & Split Plan** - The two-lane backend/frontend sequence — source `docs/specs/2026-07-02-backend-frontend-split-plan.md` (+ swimlane `.html`): lane assignment of every pilot-plan v3.3 bullet (BE/FE/PAIRED/OPS), current position, each dev's next units. The mind map is the *status* source [4]; this node + the doc are the *sequence* source. **Backend done:** B1 address-metadata → B4 medic APIs [10] → B2 seed/demo [12] → B3 scripted demo [12]; then Slice 3a booking [11]. **Frontend done:** F1-F3 owner screens [15] + owner demo; F4 monorepo restructure (Task 021, `apps/`+`packages/` [13][14][16]); **next:** F5 medic screens — F5a shell+profile (022) → F5b schedule/day-view → F5c presence/Stripe [16]. **Growth budget (Pass B):** new nodes only for genuinely new subsystems, ~1 per slice — 3b→Payments&Money ([18] added, Task 082: money model + reconciliation report; live Stripe flow pending), 3c→Video/RTC (new), S4 dispatch→edits [11]+[8], S5→Media Pipeline (new), S6 travel→edits [10][11], S7 legal→cross-cutting, S8 observability/GDPR→edits [12] or a new Ops node. **Operative defaults (override by saying so):** pet photos device-local until S5 media; email rail → first consumer (S3/S5); analytics → first emitter; GDPR delete/export jobs → S8. **PAIRED (synchronous) phases:** 3c video SDK, S4 dispatch transport, S8 observability. See [3][4][9][10][11][12].

[6] **Backend Architecture (Task 007 foundation; layers grown through Task 042)** - **Package-by-feature** — `auth/`, `address/`, `account/` (was `user/`, renamed Task 047 [25]), `notification/`, with cross-cutting code under `common/` (`security/`, `exception/`, `boot/`, `scheduling/`); each feature owns its `dto/`. The `user/` profile layer arrived with Task 034 [18], the `notification/` push rail with Task 014 [23] — drill into those nodes rather than re-inventorying files here. The live package tree + day-to-day conventions now live in **`backend/CLAUDE.md`** (auto-loaded when working under `backend/`); this node is the *why*. **Decisions that still hold:** `JwtAuthenticationFilter` is a `@Bean`, not `@Component` (a `@Component` double-registers it in the servlet chain). `SecurityConfig` matchers are first-match-wins, so specific authenticated routes (`/api/auth/logout`) must precede the `/api/auth/**` permitAll wildcard; `/api/auth/refresh` stays public (the access token is what just expired). Flyway is **wired via `spring-boot-starter-flyway`**, NOT bare `flyway-core` — SB4 moved `FlywayAutoConfiguration` into the starter module, so with only `flyway-core` Spring silently no-ops migrations on startup. All profiles run `ddl-auto: validate` (schema drift fails startup); a `NOT NULL` column is added via add-default → backfill → drop-default (V2, V9). **Config binding (Task 044):** cohesive multi-value config clusters bind to `@Validated @ConfigurationProperties` records (`RateLimitProperties`/`UserRateLimitProperties`/`JwtProperties`/`LockoutProperties`, scanned via `@ConfigurationPropertiesScan`) so a misconfigured security control fails at startup; lone unrelated values stay `@Value`. The JWT-secret guard stays a profile-aware `@PostConstruct` (Bean Validation can't express "placeholder OK in dev, fatal in prod"). **Error contract (Task 013):** `ErrorResponse` = `{status, message, code, fieldErrors:[{field,code,message}], details:["field: msg"]}`; `code` is a stable API-contract constant (`VALIDATION_FAILED`, `EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS`, `INVALID_CREDENTIALS`, `INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN`, `NOT_FOUND`, `INTERNAL_ERROR`), `fieldErrors[].code` is the Bean-Validation annotation name. **Wire-format [13]:** controllers return DTO records (each with a hand-rolled `Response.from(entity)`), NEVER entities — a serialized entity leaks the user's BCrypt hash (Task 028 B4-F1). **Auth tokens (Task 013):** access JWT 15-min default (`jwt.expiration`, env-overridable), opaque refresh tokens (32-byte SecureRandom, stored SHA-256-hashed), 30-day absolute expiry, single-use rotation in a `@Transactional` revoke-then-issue. **SB4 test conventions:** unit = `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` + `@InjectMocks` + `standaloneSetup`, `@Value` fields set via `ReflectionTestUtils`; integration shares one embedded-postgres via `support/AbstractAuthIntegrationTest` and must `.addFilter(...)` servlet filters explicitly (`webAppContextSetup` won't auto-register `@Component` filters); tests anchor on attacker entry-points. Suite count is volatile — run `.claude/bin/run-backend-tests` [24] for the live total, don't hardcode it here. **Once-parked, now built:** refresh-token reuse detection (V7 token-family + `RefreshTokenFamilyAttackIntegrationTest`) and logout / all-device revocation (V6 `users.token_invalidated_after`, enforced in `JwtAuthenticationFilter`); Testcontainers was dropped in favour of embedded-postgres (Task 018, see [4]). **Still parked:** MapStruct (the hand-rolled `from()` is the chosen pattern) and dropping the legacy `ErrorResponse.details` fallback (RBAC is **no longer parked** — built as the policy middleware in [27]) (kept until the frontend fully migrates off colon-split parsing — still present today). Connected to [12][13][14][15][16][18][23][24]; live conventions in `backend/CLAUDE.md`.

[7] **Frontend Foundation (Task 012, closed 2026-05-18)** - Expo SDK 54 + React Native 0.81 + React 19.1 + Expo Router 6 + TypeScript 5.9 (strict) + pnpm 11 (native Windows). Stack: TanStack Query v5 + Zustand for data/state with `persistQueryClient` (AsyncStorage, 7-day, buster=user.email) gated on auth-store hydration. RHF + Zod for forms; libphonenumber-js + `@zxcvbn-ts/core` (defaults: min 12 chars + score 4) for inputs. NativeWind v4 + Tailwind 3 (CSS-variable pipeline, ThemeProvider with NativeWind as single source of truth for `'system'/'light'/'dark'` — direct `Appearance` reads race with NativeWind's own override). Owned UI primitives in `src/components/ui/` (Button, Text, Input with `rightSlot`, Card, Skeleton, FormField, PhoneInput, PasswordInput) — copy-not-depend convention. axios client + typed error parser (`ValidationError`/`ConflictError`/`UnauthorizedError`/`NetworkError`/`OfflineError`/`ServerError`) maps backend `details: ["field: msg"]` → `fieldErrors[]`. Storage facade routes `secure:*` keys to SecureStore (prefix stripped at handoff — colon is illegal there). Auth bridge decouples client from store. i18next + expo-localization with typed `t()` (`en.json` is the type source). Sentry + analytics facade (dev-log / prod no-op until Firebase wired). `react-native-keyboard-controller` (KeyboardAwareScrollView + KeyboardProvider) for form scroll-into-view. `react-native-safe-area-context` for status-bar safe insets. **Dev client required** (Sentry, keyboard-controller native code). Verification chain: Gate 7.5 (UI + theme + cold-start persist on Pixel 8), Gate 11 (end-to-end register/login/logout against real Spring Boot backend + duplicate-email 409 + offline-toast + weak-password client-block), Gate 13 (21-test jest suite covering env validation / errors parser / PhoneInput RO formatting / useLogin state transitions; husky pre-commit runs lint-staged + tsc). Connected to [2] Architecture, [3] Development Workflow, [9] Routing, [10] Architecture.

[8] **Frontend Build Environment — Docker container `rn-dev` (canonical, NEVER host)** - The frontend dev environment is `frontend/docker-compose.yml` service `rn-dev` (image `ai-ring-vet-rn:dev` built from `frontend/Dockerfile`). **ALL frontend commands run inside the container** — `pnpm`, `expo`, Metro, Gradle, adb. The host-native Windows toolchain referenced in earlier mind-map revisions was an early experiment that the user dropped; running Metro/pnpm/Gradle directly on the **Windows** host is a documented anti-pattern (saved as [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]]). **macOS (Apple Silicon) is a sanctioned exception, not a violation:** the host-side runners [36][37] still keep Metro/pnpm/Gradle IN this container (every tool call is `docker compose exec`); they only ORCHESTRATE from the host, and the iOS native build (Xcode/CocoaPods, which are macOS-only) is the one genuinely-host step ([37]). So the macOS dev loop [35]-[40] is the blessed macOS path. Container ships Expo 54 / RN 0.81 / Node 24 LTS / pnpm 11 / Java 21 / Android SDK 36 / NDK 27.1.12297006 / cmake 3.22.1. **adb routing**: container's adb client is wired to the host's adb server via `ADB_SERVER_SOCKET=tcp:host.docker.internal:5037`. **Prerequisite**: host adb server must listen on `0.0.0.0:5037` (Windows scheduled task documented in `frontend/DOCKER.md` §First-time setup §1 — runs `adb -a -P 5037 nodaemon server` at login). Without that the container's `adb devices` returns empty. **Port conflict gotcha**: Docker Desktop's `wsl-relay` squats on port 8081 on the Windows host — but the container exposes 8081 via Docker port mapping, which doesn't clash. Running `expo start` natively on the host hits the wsl-relay binding and fails; running it inside the container works because Metro binds inside the Linux VM and gets exposed via the compose port map. **Daily loop** (canonical, from `frontend/DOCKER.md`): `docker compose up -d` once → `docker compose exec -d rn-dev bash -c "cd /workspace && pnpm expo start --port 8081 > /tmp/metro.log 2>&1"` → `docker compose exec rn-dev adb reverse tcp:8081 tcp:8081 && docker compose exec rn-dev adb reverse tcp:8080 tcp:8080`. **Windows one-command launcher (Task 064):** `frontend/scripts/dev-android.ps1` — the PowerShell analog of the macOS `dev-android.sh` (Windows is Android-only, no iOS analog). Same loop: container up → detect device → reverse tunnel(s) → build/install-if-needed (ABI auto-detected via `getprop ro.product.cpu.abi`, NOT the Mac's hardcoded arm64-v8a) → start/reuse Metro → cold-launch → tail `/tmp/metro.log`. Deliberately simpler than the Mac trio — no LAN-IP/iOS-concurrency machinery, no `_metro.sh` sharing; **Metro model = detached + foreground tail (no interactive r/j/m keys** — util-linux `script` PTY is macOS-only). PS-5.1 specifics baked in: scriptblock-based `Invoke-Native` (advanced-function `ValueFromRemainingArguments` mis-binds `-T`/`-s`/`-d`), UTF-8-**no-BOM** `.env` writes (default `Set-Content` BOM corrupts the Expo key), ASCII markers (no `` `u{} `` PS6 escapes). Reuse restarts Metro on baked-URL divergence (single-platform, so no attached-app to protect, unlike Mac). **Fast Refresh stays broken through the Windows bind mount** (pre-existing, [8] watcher gap) — the script documents the `pnpm dev:reset` workaround, doesn't fix it. Task 064 also **removed the inert `CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING`/`WATCHPACK_POLLING` compose vars** (read only by chokidar/watchpack, never Metro → zero benefit, only wasted-CPU risk). Connected to [7] Frontend Foundation, [35]-[40] (macOS host-side runners), [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]].

[9] **Owner Product APIs** - **owns:** the owner-facing product HTTP surfaces — pets, consents, addresses. The first AloVet endpoints beyond the pre-AloVet auth/account/push controllers, delivered contract-first backend-leads [3]. **Pet API:** owner-scoped CRUD `/api/pets` — the first AloVet product surface; unblocks the frontend pet screens [15]. **Consent API:** append-only `/api/consents` (record grant / list current / withdraw), per-**Account** (consent is per-person, not a role) — completes onboarding's GDPR consent capture. **Address:** `/api/addresses` extended additively for home-visits (label / geo lat-lng / sector / floor / intercom / parking + an ops-only `flag` + soft-delete; geo is both-or-neither; returns the `PagedResponse` envelope, hard-capped at 100). This is the blueprint for later owner surfaces. **App-Content bundle (Task 080):** `GET /api/app-content/{audience}` (owner|medic), **public/permitAll** — a thin composite of the taxonomy display vocabularies (species+embedded category, patient categories, temperament flags, specialties) the FE renders as pickers/chips; full `{ro,en}` maps on the wire. **Scraper invariant:** only unauthenticated-safe content may enter a bundle. Version-derived strong ETag (per-audience, from the `content_version` singleton [7]) + `If-None-Match`→304 (steady state; content ~monthly) via `checkNotModified` (NOT ShallowEtag); `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` → an audience's absent section omits its key. Retired the authenticated `/api/specialties` (folded in; `SpecialtyResponse`→`taxonomy/dto`). FE hooks (`useAppContent`/`pickLocale`/prefetch) are Marius's layer; medic bundle grows additively. See [6][7][10][12]; frontend consumers [15][16].

[10] **Frontend Architecture — add-a-feature playbook (Task 012 Gate 14, 2026-05-18)** - Also captured in `frontend/README.md`. Recipes: - **New screen** → `app/(app)/<route>.tsx` (authed) or `app/(auth)/<route>.tsx` (anonymous). Auth-group screens auto-gated by `<Stack.Protected>` at root layout (see [9]); no per-screen guard logic. - **New feature** (screens + hooks + services + types) → `src/features/<name>/` with `services.ts` (pure API calls over `apiClient`), `hooks.ts` (TanStack mutations/queries that route success to local state), `store.ts` (Zustand if persistent state needed; manual persist via storage facade for cross-backend split), `types.ts`, optional `bridge.ts` (for cross-module wiring), `index.ts` barrel. Template: `src/features/auth/`. - **New shared UI primitive** → `src/components/ui/<Name>.tsx`, copy-not-depend convention (no external UI-kit lock-in), then export via `index.ts` barrel and add a Section to `app/dev/components.tsx`. Light/dark/RTL-safe, typed props. - **External service wrapper** → `src/lib/<thing>.ts` as a thin facade; vendor SDKs never imported from screens (analytics, logger, toast, storage, network all follow this). Provider swaps become one-file changes. - **Translation string** → `assets/locales/{en,ro}.json`; `en.json` is the type source for the augmented `t()` (Gate 10) — unknown keys are TS errors. Path alias `@/` → `src/` is wired in 4 places: `tsconfig.json`, `babel.config.js` (module-resolver), `metro.config.js` (extraNodeModules), `jest.config.js` (moduleNameMapper). Keep all four aligned. Error-routing convention (`src/lib/queryClient.ts:24-38`): global `MutationCache.onError` toasts `Offline`/`Network`; screen `onError` handles `Validation` (RHF `setError` per field), `Conflict` (`setError('root')`), `Server` (inline). Screens MUST NOT toast for Validation/Conflict and MUST NOT inline-render for Offline/Network — double-handling is forbidden. **Generalized + partly superseded by [26]** (Task 049): mutation routing is now the shared `routeFormError` helper (transient buckets → global toast, not per-screen inline), and reads get their own handler (`resolveQueryView`/`QueryErrorState`) — [10]'s convention covered mutations only. Test layout (Gate 13): `__tests__/` folders co-located with code. `jest.config.js` uses `jest-expo` preset; `jest.setup.ts` mocks AsyncStorage / SecureStore / NetInfo. **Version pins**: jest @29.7 + jest-expo @54 + react-test-renderer @19.1.0 — Expo SDK 54 ships jest-expo 54 which expects jest 29 internally; jest 30 breaks with `clearMocksOnScope is not a function`. Connected to [7] Foundation, [9] Routing, [8] Build Environment.

[11] **Consult Lifecycle** - **owns:** consult booking, the Interaction/Order lifecycle, and (future) dispatch + matching. **Payments/money split off to [18]** (Task 082). The first PRODUCT consumer of the 0b machinery [8]. **Slice 3a scheduled-consult booking:** Interaction/Order's first real write path, SlotHold's first real caller, PricingConfig's first consumer — booking runs over the state-machine runner + job engine + concurrency primitive [8]. **Ratings (Task 091):** a completed consult is rateable exactly once by its owner — the write + transactional aggregate recompute live with the medic subsystem [10]; the ranking composite here finally has real input. **Consult summary (Task 092):** the assigned medic files a free-text visit summary (`POST /api/medic/consults/{id}/summary`, self-scoped) while in_progress or ≤48h post-completion (config `app.records.summary-window`; the completion clock is the status-event ledger row — Interaction has no completedAt); it lands as a `visit_summary` RecordEntry (V47, `record/` package — the Records-domain seed, full data-model column set but ONE behaving type) on the pet's timeline, read owner-scoped via `GET /api/pets/{petId}/records`. S5 extends this into structured summaries/media/more types and should promote Records to its own node then. **Roadmap (this is the biggest growth node — see [5]):** 3c video/RTC, S4 dispatch/matching, and S6 travel-aware windows land here (**3b payments/money split off to [18]**, Task 082; video will likely spawn its own node too). Consumes the domain schema [7] and medic availability [10]. See [8][7][10][9][5].

[12] **Auth Security Hardening (Task 027, closed 2026-05-21)** - **Account lockout** (`AuthService.login`, `users.failed_login_count`, `users.locked_until` from V5 migration): 5 consecutive wrong-password attempts within the window set `locked_until = now + 15min`; while locked, even the right password returns 423 ACCOUNT_LOCKED (no bypass-on-correct-guess); window-expiry naturally unlocks on the next successful login (counter + lock cleared). Externalized as `auth.lockout.max-failures` (default 5) and `auth.lockout.duration` (default PT15M). **Concurrency gotcha**: `findByEmailForUpdate` (new repo method with `@Lock(LockModeType.PESSIMISTIC_WRITE)`) is required so two parallel wrong-password calls don't both read count=N and both write N+1. `login()` is `@Transactional(noRollbackFor = {InvalidCredentialsException, AccountLockedException})` — without `noRollbackFor`, throwing the credentials exception rolls back the failedLoginCount increment and lockout never fires. **Rate limiting** (`RateLimitFilter` at `@Order(HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE)`, runs before Spring Security): Bucket4j token-bucket per client IP, stored in a bounded Caffeine cache (10k entries, 1h expire-after-access). Applies only to `/api/auth/{login,register,refresh}` via `shouldNotFilter` whitelisting. Defaults `app.rate-limit.{capacity,refill-tokens}=100`, `refill-period=PT1M`. **XFF trust is gated**: client IP is `remoteAddr` by default; `X-Forwarded-For` first-hop is honored only when `remoteAddr` matches one of the CIDRs in `app.rate-limit.trusted-proxies` (default empty = never trust). Without this gate the limiter is trivially bypassed — an attacker rotating spoofed XFF values cycles through fresh buckets. Configure to the actual reverse-proxy CIDR(s) when deployed behind one. Response: 429 RATE_LIMITED with `Retry-After: <secondsToWait>` from `bucket.tryConsumeAndReturnRemaining(1).getNanosToWaitForRefill()` (actual time-to-refill, not the configured window length). JSON body is hand-written (autowired ObjectMapper isn't available at this filter's startup precedence). **Test infrastructure**: `AbstractAuthIntegrationTest` (in `test/java/com/airingvet/support/`) shares one `EmbeddedPostgres` JVM-wide and wires the `RateLimitFilter` into MockMvc explicitly via `.addFilter(...)`. **Gotcha**: `MockMvcBuilders.webAppContextSetup()` does NOT auto-register `@Component` servlet filters — only what `.addFilter(...)` or `springSecurity()` add. Five integration test classes (`LoginAttack`, `JwtAttack`, `RefreshTokenAttack`, `AccountLockout`, plus extensions to `AuthFlow`) inherit this; `RateLimitIntegrationTest` adds its own `@TestPropertySource` to shrink the bucket to capacity=3 and to whitelist `127.0.0.1/32` so MockMvc's XFF values produce distinct buckets per test method. **Attack-surface anchoring**: tests organize per attacker entry-point (forged JWT, malformed JWT, expired JWT direct hit on protected endpoint, refresh-token replay, cross-user 404 via `ResourceNotFoundException` — never leak existence with 403), not per code-path. Connected to [6] Backend Architecture.

[13] **Frontend Foundation & Patterns** - **owns:** the RN app's stack, routing, add-a-feature recipe, theming, error-handling framework, forms + i18n. Full rules in `frontend/CLAUDE.md` + `frontend/README.md`. **Stack:** TanStack Query v5 + Zustand (cache persisted to AsyncStorage, gated on auth-store hydration), RHF + Zod, NativeWind v4. **Routing:** auth gating via `<Stack.Protected guard>` at the root `_layout.tsx`, NOT effect-redirects / `<Redirect>` (both tried and rejected — loop / unmatched-route). **Monorepo (Task 021):** `frontend/` is a pnpm-workspace root — `apps/owner`+`apps/medic` (each owns `app/`, `src/{features,hooks}`, full config surface + native trees), `packages/core` (`@alovet/core`: api/lib/config/theme/i18n/auth-store+bridge+services), `packages/ui` (`@alovet/ui`: primitives + `cn`/`formScroll`/`stackHeader`); consumed as SOURCE via workspace links + metro `watchFolders`=workspace-root. Package graph **app→ui→core**, never into app. **Add-a-feature:** `apps/<role>/src/features/<name>/` (per-app; shared infra → `packages/core/<name>/`, e.g. auth store/services/bridge in core but the login/register hooks stay per-app); shared UI → `packages/ui`; vendor SDKs only behind `@alovet/core/lib/` facades; no `console.*`; aliases — app-local `@/`→own `src/`, cross-package `@alovet/core|ui` (each wired in that app's 4 configs). **Task 019 primitives:** `TextArea` (the standard multiline field — auto-grows 2→10 rows via `minHeight`/`maxHeight`, NOT `onContentSizeChange`+fixed-height which froze at min on iOS; `forwardRef` for RHF focus; merge caller `style` as an array); `src/navigation/stackHeader.ts` `buildStackHeaderOptions(tokens)` = app-wide chevron-only back (`headerBackButtonDisplayMode:'minimal'`) + themed header chrome; DOB `DateField` = `@gorhom/bottom-sheet` content-height sheet (needs `GestureHandlerRootView`+`BottomSheetModalProvider` at root `_layout.tsx`) on iOS / Material calendar on Android. **Test gotcha:** a `jest.mock` factory that `createElement`s an RN component trips NativeWind's CSS-interop (`_ReactNativeCSSInterop` out-of-scope, hoist error) — return children/null from mock components instead. **Theming gotchas:** theme colors via inline `style={{color: tokens.X}}` NOT NativeWind `text-*` (`cn` has no tailwind-merge); rebuild `vars()` per render (a module-level memo breaks palette switching). **Error framework:** typed parser → `ValidationError`/`ConflictError`/`Network`/`Offline`/`Server`; `resolveQueryView` + `routeFormError` + `useRevalidateOnFocus`. **Query reads need their own error UI (no global handler); drive staleness off `failureReason` not `isError`; the global `refetchOnMount:false` makes a server outage silent unless a query overrides it.** Backend coupling: hikari `connection-timeout=5000` must stay under the 15s client timeout [6]. **i18n:** `assets/locales/en.json` is the type source; every key in both en + ro. See [2][14][15][17]; auth backend [6].

[14] **Backend Security Fixes (Task 029, closed 2026-05-22)** - Closes Task 028's audit verdict (FAIL-for-production). 120 backend tests, was 91 at start of task. **Block A — password-hash leak (B4-F1, CRITICAL)**: controllers return DTO records via static `from(Entity)` mapper, never entities. `User.{password,failedLoginCount,lockedUntil}` also get `@JsonIgnore` as belt-and-braces. See [13] for the wire-format convention. **Block B — CORS lockdown (B1-F1/B2-F2/B3-F2, CRITICAL)**: all `@CrossOrigin` removed; `SecurityConfig.corsConfigurationSource` reads `@Value("${app.cors.allowed-origins:}")` (empty = fail-closed, literal `*` rejected at startup with `IllegalStateException`). Headers tightened to `[Authorization, Content-Type]`. **Block C — JWT secret fail-fast (B2-F1/B2-F4/B2-F5/B6-F1, CRITICAL/HIGH)**: `JwtUtil.@PostConstruct` rejects the placeholder secret OR secrets <32 bytes UTF-8 unless ALL active profiles are in `{dev,test}`. `StandardCharsets.UTF_8` everywhere — platform charset can no longer change the signing key. **Block D — Revocable JWT (B2-F3, HIGH)**: V6 migration adds `users.token_invalidated_after TIMESTAMPTZ NULL`. JWT carries a custom millisecond `iatMs` claim (RFC 7519 `iat` is seconds-truncated → races same-second logout). `JwtAuthenticationFilter` drops auth when `iatMs < user.tokenInvalidatedAfter.toEpochMilli()`. `POST /api/auth/logout` (`authenticated()` matcher placed BEFORE the `/api/auth/**` permitAll wildcard — order matters or unauth POST would hit `@AuthenticationPrincipal null` → NPE) bumps tia and bulk-revokes the user's refresh tokens. **Block E — Refresh-token reuse detection (B1-F4/B6-F11, MED→HIGH)**: V7 migration adds `refresh_tokens.family_id UUID NOT NULL` (PG 13+ `gen_random_uuid()`, no pgcrypto needed). All tokens descended from one login share `family_id`. `RefreshTokenService.rotate` detects replay of a revoked token (compromise signal) and (a) bulk-revokes the entire family, (b) bumps `user.tokenInvalidatedAfter` so JWTs in the family also stop working. **Critical**: `@Transactional(noRollbackFor = InvalidRefreshTokenException.class)` — without it, the throw rolls back the family revocation + tia bump and the compromise response silently evaporates. Same idiom as `AuthService.login`'s lockout transaction. **Block F — Timing oracle + register enumeration (B1-F2/B1-F3/B5-F1, MED)**: `AuthService.login` user-miss path runs `passwordEncoder.matches(request.getPassword(), dummyPasswordHash)` (dummy hash computed once in `@PostConstruct`) so wall-time matches the wrong-password path. `EmailAlreadyExistsException` is now no-arg; message stripped of the user-supplied email. **Block G — Input size caps + body cap (B1-F5/B3-F3/B2-F11/B6-F8, MED)**: DTO `@Size` on all string fields (`RegisterRequest.password max=72` to match BCrypt's silent-truncation boundary). New `ContentLengthLimitFilter` at `@Order(HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE)` (`RateLimitFilter` bumped to `+1`) rejects `Content-Length > 256 KB` with 413 before Jackson sees the body. Default `app.body-limit.max-bytes=262144`. **Block H — Rate-limit URI/IPv6 normalization (B2-F8/B2-F9, MED)**: `canonicalize(uri)` strips trailing slashes and collapses doubled slashes; `normalizeIp(addr)` collapses IPv6 to `/64` (typical residential allocation = 2^64 free buckets per attacker without this). **Block I — Container + supply-chain (B6-F3/B6-F4/B6-F5, HIGH)**: Dockerfile runs as `app` (uid 100, no shell, no home, no login); `--chown=app:app` on the JAR COPY. `.dockerignore` excludes build/VCS/IDE/env/log artifacts. `org.owasp:dependency-check-maven:10.0.4` configured with `failBuildOnCVSS=7`; CI to wire `mvn dependency-check:check` in Task 003. **Parked**: B2-F10 (user-keyed rate limit on `/api/addresses/**` — needs filter inside SecurityFilterChain so principal is populated), Block J (operational visibility / actuator / headers / log-PII cleanup), email verification flow (B4-F3 prerequisite), sliding-window refresh-token cleanup (B1-F8), Lombok `@Data` → identity-based equals on JPA entities. Connected to [6] Backend Architecture, [12] Auth Security Hardening, [13] Wire-Format Convention.

[15] **Backend Ops Hygiene (Task 030, closed 2026-05-23)** - Closes the Block-J cluster parked from Task 029 + B1-F8 cleanup. 144 backend tests, was 128 at start. **Block A — actuator + dead route**: `spring-boot-starter-actuator` on classpath, `management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info` (every other endpoint unmapped). `app.actuator.public=true` in application.yml (and dev), `false` in application-prod.yml — switches the SecurityConfig matcher between `permitAll()` and `authenticated()`. Belt-and-braces: `/actuator/**` catch-all is `denyAll()` so a future widened exposure-include can't accidentally leak `/env`, `/beans`, etc. Removed `AuthController.health()` and corresponding test. Follow-up: `GlobalExceptionHandler.handleNoResource(NoResourceFoundException)` → 404 NOT_FOUND so probes against the now-deleted route (and any other unmapped path) don't get swallowed by the catch-all `Exception` handler and emerge as 500 INTERNAL_ERROR. **Block B — headers (B2-F12)**: `SecurityConfig.filterChain.headers(...)` adds CSP `default-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'` and Referrer-Policy `no-referrer`. Spring's default X-Frame-Options DENY and X-Content-Type-Options nosniff kept as belt-and-braces. **Block C — JWT invalid-token DEBUG log (B2-F14)**: `JwtAuthenticationFilter` now logs `JWT validation failed for token prefix={}` at DEBUG when validation fails — first 8 chars only (`LOG_PREFIX_LEN`), full token never logged. DEBUG by design so prod logs aren't flooded by background forgery scans; blue team enables on demand via `logging.level.com.airingvet.common.security=DEBUG`. **Block D — refresh log PII (B6-F12/B1-F10)**: `AuthService.refresh` log line changed from `Token refreshed for user: {email}` to `Token refreshed for userId={id}` — PK is meaningless without DB access; the email is directly identifying and lands in SIEM/log shippers. **Block E — profile boot guard (B6-F6)**: new `com.airingvet.common.boot.ProfileGuard` (POJO, not `@Component` — registered via `app.addListeners(new ProfileGuard())` in `Application.main` so it fires on `ApplicationEnvironmentPreparedEvent`, before bean creation). Refuses boot unless active profiles ∩ `{dev,prod,test}` is non-empty. All 14 `@SpringBootTest` integration tests now carry `@ActiveProfiles("test")` (`AbstractAuthIntegrationTest` covers 13 via inheritance, `AuthFlowIntegrationTest` is standalone). `validate(Environment)` extracted as package-private so tests don't need to synthesize an `ApplicationEnvironmentPreparedEvent` (the Spring Boot 4 constructor requires a `ConfigurableBootstrapContext` that's awkward to fabricate). **Block F — refresh-token cleanup (B1-F8)**: new `RefreshTokenCleanupService @Component @Scheduled(cron="${app.refresh-token.cleanup.cron:0 0 3 * * *}")` (3am daily) calling new `RefreshTokenRepository.deleteAllByExpiresAtBefore(cutoff)` with cutoff = `now - app.refresh-token.cleanup.grace` (default `P7D`). 7-day grace past the 30-day natural expiry gives forensics a window. Logs `Deleted {} expired refresh tokens older than {}` at INFO only when deleted>0. Dedicated `SchedulingConfig @EnableScheduling` for narrow scope. Multi-replica safety as Javadoc — concurrent DELETEs are idempotent under MVCC. **Build-infra side-improvement**: `pom.xml` excludes `embedded-postgres-binaries-{darwin-amd64,darwin-arm64v8,windows-amd64}` from `embedded-postgres` (the test JVM only runs on Linux containers, so the 28-30 MB platform binaries waste bandwidth and were the recurring failure mode of mvn-test downloads on flaky networks). **Parked**: B2-F10 (user-keyed rate limit on `/api/addresses/**` — needs filter inside SecurityFilterChain so principal is populated, separate Task 031), Lombok `@Data` → identity-based equals on JPA entities, email verification flow. Connected to [14] Backend Security Fixes, [12] Auth Security Hardening, [6] Backend Architecture.

[16] **Backend Per-User Rate Limit (Task 031, closed 2026-05-23)** - Closes Task 028 B2-F10, the last MED rate-limit gap from the audit. 149 backend tests (was 144). New `com.airingvet.common.security.UserRateLimitFilter @Component extends OncePerRequestFilter`. **Position**: wired via `http.addFilterAfter(userRateLimitFilter, JwtAuthenticationFilter.class)` inside `SecurityConfig.filterChain` — so `SecurityContextHolder` is already populated and the filter can read `auth.getPrincipal()` cast to `com.airingvet.user.User` and key buckets by `user.getId()` (database PK). The pre-auth IP-based `RateLimitFilter` (Task 027, `@Order(HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE+1)`, runs before Spring Security entirely) still owns `/api/auth/{login,register,refresh}`; this new filter owns `/api/addresses/**` (and any future authenticated paths added to `USER_RATE_LIMITED_PREFIXES`). **Caffeine cache** `<Long, Bucket>` bounded at 10k entries / 1h expire-after-access, mirrors the IP filter. **Defaults**: `app.user-rate-limit.{capacity,refill-tokens}=300`, `refill-period=PT1M` (generous for legitimate address scrolling, tight enough to block authenticated DoS). **Response**: 429 with `Retry-After` from `Bucket4j.getNanosToWaitForRefill` and hand-written JSON matching `RateLimitFilter`'s wire shape verbatim (`{status,message,code:"RATE_LIMITED",fieldErrors,details}`) so the frontend's existing 429 handler doesn't need a per-source branch. **Anonymous traffic**: skipped — null principal → `chain.doFilter` → Spring Security's `AuthorizationFilter` returns 401 (matches existing contract; avoids accidentally rate-limiting anonymous probes against /api/addresses which would also pollute the per-user bucket of whoever was authenticated last in the cache). **Per-replica buckets**: same trade-off as the IP filter — distributed bucket store is overkill for a daily-traffic backend. **Path normalization**: reuses `RateLimitFilter.canonicalize` (Task 029 Block H carry-over) so future Spring upgrades that re-enable trailing-slash routing don't reopen a bypass. **Test class** `UserRateLimitIntegrationTest` extends `AbstractAuthIntegrationTest`, shrinks bucket via `@TestPropertySource` to capacity=3, covers 5 attacker-entry-points: under-limit succeeds, over-limit 429 with Retry-After + RATE_LIMITED code, two distinct users get distinct buckets (per-user keying), anonymous 401 not 429, auth endpoints not touched by user limiter. Live: env-var `APP_USER_RATE_LIMIT_CAPACITY=20` honoured via Spring Relaxed Binding; 20× 200 + 1× 429 with `Retry-After: 54`. Connected to [15] Ops Hygiene, [12] Auth Security Hardening, [14] Backend Security Fixes.

[17] **Frontend Logout Wiring (Task 032, closed 2026-05-23)** - Completes the revocable-JWT story from [14] Block D end-to-end. Until this, the "Sign out" button only cleared local Zustand + SecureStore — the server-side `POST /api/auth/logout` (which bumps `users.token_invalidated_after` and bulk-revokes refresh tokens) never fired, so a stolen JWT/refresh-token pair survived "logout" until natural expiry. Frontend changes: (a) `src/features/auth/services.ts` adds `export async function logout(): Promise<void>` — POSTs `/api/auth/logout` with no body (backend reads principal from the Bearer header). (b) `src/features/auth/hooks.ts` rewrites `useLogout` to return a `useCallback`-stabilized `() => Promise<void>` that does `try { await service.logout(); } catch {} finally { clearSession(); }` — best-effort: network failure or 4xx (already-expired JWT, multi-tab race) must not strand the user, so the `finally` always clears local state. **NOT a TanStack mutation** — the single caller (`<Button onPress={logout}>`) doesn't surface success/error UI and the intent is fire-and-clear-and-forget. **Interaction with the 401 interceptor in src/api/client.ts**: if logout 401s, the interceptor attempts /api/auth/refresh; on refresh success it retries logout with the fresh JWT (correct outcome — actually revoke the now-current session); on refresh failure it clears the session through `bridge.onUnauthorized → clearSession()`, making our `finally clearSession()` a defensive no-op. **NOT touched**: `bridge.ts` `onUnauthorized` keeps calling `clearSession()` directly, not `service.logout()` — calling backend logout from the 401 interceptor would recurse infinitely (a 401 on the logout request would trip the same interceptor). New `__tests__/useLogout.test.tsx` (4 tests) covers: success POSTs and clears; network error still clears; 401 still clears; idempotent when called while already anonymous. Frontend suite: 34 → 38 tests, all green. Live verified end-to-end via curl: register → GET /api/addresses 200 → POST /api/auth/logout 204 → re-GET /api/addresses 401 (JWT revoked via tia) → POST /api/auth/refresh 401 (refresh tokens revoked). Connected to [14] Backend Security Fixes (Block D), [16] Per-User Rate Limit, [12] Auth Security Hardening.

[18] **Payments & Money** - **owns:** the money model — the consult split, commission, voucher funding, and the monthly medic reconciliation. Split off from [11] (which flagged payments would spawn its own node). **Charge type (Task 082, O8 RESOLVED):** Stripe **DIRECT charges** — the **vet is merchant of record** (invoices the owner); the platform's commission is the live `application_fee_amount`; the platform invoices the vet for it. Supersedes the earlier separate-charges-&-transfers posture. **Split (`money/MoneySplit` — pure, `long` bani, INV-003):** `ownerCharge = price − voucher`; `platformFee = max(0, commission − voucher)` (the voucher eats the platform's commission first, floors at 0); `commission = floor(price·bps/10000)` on the **pre-voucher** price (corrected the old post-discount `:173` rule). **Reconciliation (`money/ReconciliationService`; `GET /api/admin/reconciliation?month=yyyy-MM`, capability `VIEW_PLATFORM_RECONCILIATION` = platform-finance/superadmin):** monthly, per medic — makes the medic whole to `price − commission` via a **manual** platform→medic payment = `Σ max(0, voucher − commission)`. Grain = completed Interaction joined to its Order (Order has no medic FK); period key = the `completed` `InteractionStatusEvent.occurredAt` bucketed in `PilotZone` (Europe/Bucharest); excludes `isTest` + null-commission (logged); voucher = a **negative** `discount` OrderLine (positive = corrupt). Fee-free pilot form; the platform **absorbs Stripe fees**, trued up in reconciliation once the live flow reports them. **Merchant onboarding (Task 083, [10]) + payment schema/config (Task 084) DONE:** connected accounts are `card_payments` Merchant accounts; `pricing_config` carries per-interaction-type commission bps + `PricingService.resolveCommissionRateBps` (V39; pilot **20% placeholder**, ops-tunable — PRD O2); `PaymentRecord` reshaped for direct charges (`stripeFeeMinor`/`applicationFeeAmountMinor`; vestigial `payoutTransferId`/`payoutAmountMinor` dropped, V40). **Charge flow A+B DONE (Task 085, `payment/` pkg, live-verify IOU):** the test-mode direct-charge flow behind a `StripePaymentGateway` seam (`app.stripe.enabled=false`→503; sibling of `StripeConnectGateway`). **card-save** (`POST /api/bookings/{id}/card-save[/verify]`; **platform** Customer + SetupIntent per W1 — owner `stripe_customer_id` V41 — saved PM cloned onto the medic's account per charge; `Order draft→method_saved`) → **authorize** at T-48h (`payment-authorize` JobHandler enqueued in the `booked` transition tx, `runAt=slotStart−48h`; snapshots `commissionRateBps`+`payeeStripeAccountId` on the Order; PaymentIntent **manual-capture** on the medic account with `application_fee_amount`; `card_payments`-active merchant guard) → **capture** at completion (`POST /api/medic/consults/{id}/complete`; fires session_start/ended, captures, writes `commissionMinor`/`stripeChargeId`/`stripeFeeMinor` — **makes the reconciliation report live**) → **release** (`AuthorizationReleaseService` voids the hold on any non-completed terminal; production callers = the Task-087 cancel/no-show orchestrators). All money ops idempotent (deterministic Stripe keys + PaymentRecord `idempotency_key` UNIQUE); **no Stripe call inside a write tx** — readOnly-loader / `NOT_SUPPORTED`-orchestrator / `REQUIRES_NEW`-writer split (extends the MedicStripeService precedent to suspend the job tx). **Webhooks + refunds DONE (Task 086, increment C):** one platform-level Connect webhook endpoint `POST /api/stripe/webhook` (permitAll, `Webhook.constructEvent` HMAC verify on the raw body, one `app.stripe.webhook-secret`; connected-account events carry `account`) routing through the existing `StripeEventDedupeService`; handlers **reconcile + backfill ONLY, never re-drive** the interaction lifecycle — `payment_intent.succeeded` backfills the settled `stripeFeeMinor` (`fetchChargeFee`), `canceled`/`payment_failed` reconcile an authorized-not-captured record, unknown-PaymentRecord throws → 503 → Stripe re-delivers (not consumed). **Refunds:** `Refund`/`RefundStatus` (V42, `stripe_refund_id` UNIQUE = durable idempotency anchor) + `refund` gateway op (`refund_application_fee` per `app.payments.return-commission-on-refund`; no `reverse_transfer` on direct charges); ops endpoint `POST /api/admin/refunds` (capability `ISSUE_REFUNDS_RESOLVE_DISPUTES`, authz-before-lookup + audit); ONE locked `RefundWriter` shared by the ops path AND the `refund.created`/`refund.updated` webhook (dashboard-initiated converges), setting the primary PaymentRecord `refunded`/`partially_refunded` and deriving Order status (projection rule). **Cancellation/no-show DONE (Task 087):** `cancellation_policy` per-kind config (V43; scheduled_consult 120min/50%/100%/10min-grace — seeded ONLY for that kind; `instant`/`home_visit` rows land with their consuming tasks) + pure `payment/CancellationDecision` driving three surfaces: owner `POST /api/bookings/{id}/cancel` (free release strictly before slotStart−cutoff; LATE capture from the cutoff instant; **REJECTED at/after slotStart** — else an absent owner games 50% instead of the 100% no-show), medic `POST /api/medic/consults/{id}/cancel` (ALWAYS full release), ops `POST /api/admin/consults/{id}/no-show` (capability `ISSUE_REFUNDS_RESOLVE_DISPUTES`, grace-guarded, party=owner→100% capture / medic→release; the audit row is the pilot reliability flag — ops-only because no-video has no automatic no-show signal). **Policy captures collect ZERO commission** (decision 2026-07-08; gateway capture gained a nullable `applicationFeeOverrideMinor`). **Two orderings that matter:** release paths fire the terminal FIRST and leave the order authorized — `release()`'s writer settles it (marking cancelled beforehand makes release a silent no-op and strands the hold); capture legality is decided BEFORE the Stripe call, and crash-repair is ALWAYS release-only (impl-panel: capture paths commit terminal+record atomically, so a terminal with a still-authorized order can only be an unreleased release — re-deriving free-vs-late there would launder a medic's crashed cancel into an owner 50% charge). **Reconciliation gained a second ledger:** captured policy terminals reimburse the settled `stripeFeeMinor` (medic keeps the full captured amount); `policyCaptureCount` + `excludedPendingFeeCount` on the report; deliberate fee asymmetry vs completed consults — do NOT merge the branches when the completed-consult fee term lands. **Vouchers DONE (Task 088, `voucher/` pkg):** Voucher/VoucherRedemption (V44; one-redemption-per-order UNIQUE; V44 also added the `payment_records(order_id) WHERE purpose='primary'` partial-unique — the one-primary-per-order backstop). Owner `POST /api/bookings/{id}/voucher` (interaction-row lock FIRST, then voucher-row lock — the card-save/authorize serialization seam; idempotent same-code retry; caps count `redeemed` rows only), ops `POST|GET /api/admin/vouchers` (FIRST consumer of `MANAGE_VOUCHERS_PROMOTIONS`; `fundedBy=payee` REJECTED at creation until its distinct split lands). Redemption = negative `discount` OrderLine + INV-008 totals recompute (`order/OrderTotals`, shared with BookingService); **`money/OrderMoneyLines.summarize` is THE single price/voucher line-aggregation rule** (reconciliation + authorize + capture + redeem — never hand-copy it). Authorize prices from LINES (never `totalMinor` — post-discount once a discount line exists!), keys the PI with an **amount-fingerprinted idempotency key** and the writer **re-derives the split under the interaction lock** (a redemption racing the unlocked pricing read → stale authorize refused, retry mints a fresh PI, the orphan auto-expires); capture re-derives `commissionMinor` from the snapshot rate on the pre-voucher price (≠ appFee once a voucher exists). **100%-voucher FREE consult: zero Stripe objects ever** — a zero-amount null-PI `authorized` record (ONE uniform shape recognized by the completion/release/cancellation guards), no card-save, deliberately no merchant requirement. Every non-captured terminal (decline, hold-expiry, cancel, release) flips the redemption → `released` (voucher reusable); policy captures keep it spent. **Decline recovery DONE (V45):** the sync T-48h hard decline records the declined PI on the failed marker + a **`payment_intent_attempts` ledger row** (every PI the platform ever creates gets one; webhook lookup falls back to it — known-but-superseded PI → ack, unknown → the 086 retryable posture; without it a retry would orphan the declined PI into a webhook 503-loop) and pushes the owner (`payment_failed`, IDs-only, SYNC path only); wire signal = `BookingResponse.order.paymentStatus` (`failed` = the app's fix-payment CTA). **Retry** = re-run card-save (admitted on `method_saved`+failed-primary; verify is PM-ref-idempotent; the marker's `attempt`++ under the interaction lock → authorize re-enqueued, a DEAD job frees its dedupe key); **`payment/PaymentAttemptKeys` is THE key derivation** — attempt-0 byte-identical `-v1`, recovery `-r{n}` (a Stripe idempotency key permanently pins its FIRST result including a decline — never reuse one across attempts); the authorize writer UPDATEs the failed marker in place (one-primary V44 never challenged; attempt-mismatch refused like a re-price). **T-12h sweep** (`payment-cancel-unauthorized`, enqueued WITH the authorize in the booked tx; runAt = max(now + 2h recovery floor, slot − 12h), deliberately not capped at slot): a still-unauthorized booking gets the free-terminal cancel (zero Stripe — incl. never-card-saved bookings, closing the uncharged-no-show gap; voucher released; both parties pushed best-effort AFTER the durable write); a lagging 100%-voucher free order **self-heals** (local free authorize) instead of cancelling; lost-race-to-authorize is an OUTCOME (`lost_race_funded`), not an exception; a live retry-authorize DEFERS the sweep as a `deferred` outcome + a keyless follow-up job (~15m, `cancelSweepDeferInterval`) — NEVER a thrown engine-retry (the ~150s backoff budget would dead-letter and strand the booking; impl-panel Critical) — and every card-save re-arms the keyed sweep's run_at via `JobService.reschedule` (enqueue's dedupe coalescing deliberately never moves it). **Earnings ledger DONE (the last 3b pilot bullet — medic-facing read):** self-scoped `GET /api/medic/earnings?month=yyyy-MM` (`money/MedicEarningsService`+`Controller`, non-medic 404 like day-view — `VIEW_OWN_EARNINGS` stays matrix documentation) over the SAME grain+derivation as reconciliation (completed events + captured policy terminals, `OrderMoneyLines`+`MoneySplit`) so the medic's view and Finance's report can't drift; completed entries require a SETTLED primary record (captured|partially_refunded|refunded; unsettled = corruption, excluded+counted), policy entries enter at capture with NO fee gate and STAY VISIBLE after refunds (settled ORDER statuses — the refund projection would otherwise vanish them); `earned = price − commission` (the make-whole target, NOT live net), refunds surfaced succeeded-only and **UN-NETTED** (user 2026-07-09 — netting is ONE parked product decision to land on BOTH money read-surfaces together; reconciliation equally un-netted AND still drops refunded policy captures — same parked task); `settledBy` from the frozen `Order.payeeType` (always `self` until clinic-payee routing lands — never derive from `Medic.clinicId`, that would claim a settlement that didn't happen); `month` REQUIRED, fixed `page`/`size` (deliberately NO Pageable — springdoc would advertise a dead `sort`), deterministic `occurredAt desc, id desc` order (stable pages); keeps the `isTest=false` filter (user decision — demo medics see an empty ledger; workaround parked with the 3b demo). **Still DEFERRED:** on-session add-ons (D), instant-path authorize timing (E), decline-recovery UI (Marius; contract + `payment_failed`/`booking_cancelled_payment_unresolved` push types shipped), reconciling the async webhook authorized→failed Order divergence (unrecoverable-by-card-save state; parked in the decline-recovery task), duration-gated capture waive/reduce, disputes/chargebacks (C17), owner cancel of a `requested` booking (spec gap — the machine has no such transition), payee-funded voucher split semantics, voucher-refund policy on captured orders, and voucher redemption-abuse monitoring (spec: ops statistic). Spec: `docs/specs/alovet-data-model-v2.7.md` §Money. See [11][10][7][12][5]. (Node well over the ~250-word budget — overflow-flip candidate.)

[19] **Frontend Account Tab (Task 035, closed 2026-05-23)** - Consumes the Task 034 backend profile contract end-to-end. **Route topology**: `app/(app)/_layout.tsx` Stack → Tabs (Home + Account); the addresses CRUD moved from top-level `app/(app)/addresses*` into `app/(app)/account/addresses*` so they live inside the Account tab's inner Stack. Modal presentation on `new` + `[id]` is preserved by `<Stack.Screen presentation="modal">` (which only works on Stack — Tabs.Screen errors at compile, see Task 035 W4 outcome). The `<Stack.Protected guard={isAuthenticated}>` at root from [9] still gates the (app) group cleanly with Tabs underneath — no per-layout redirect logic added. **Tab icons** via `@expo/vector-icons/Ionicons` (`home-outline`, `person-outline`). **Account feature module** at `src/features/account/{types,services,hooks,index}.ts`. Three hooks: `useProfile` (TanStack query, key `['profile']`, `enabled: isAuthenticated`, **auto-mirrors result into the Zustand auth store via a useEffect on `query.data`** — single mechanism handles both fresh-login population AND cold-start-stale-partial-profile self-heal), `useUpdateProfile` (mutation onSuccess writes through to BOTH `queryClient.setQueryData(['profile'], data)` AND `useAuthStore.getState().setProfile(data)`), `useChangePassword` (mutation onSuccess calls `updateTokens(token, refreshToken)` from the existing auth store — installs the fresh AuthResponse without re-login, per [[feedback-password-change-ux]]). **Write-through path uses `useAuthStore` directly, not the api/bridge** — bridge has no setProfile and adding one would be coupling for a single caller (plan-review Critical #2). **`AuthUser` widened** from `{email}` to `{email, firstName: string | null, lastName: string | null, phone: string | null}` — null on the type bridges the login→profile-fetch window even though backend NOT NULL + @NotBlank means non-null at rest (Task 034 W13). **useLogin seeds null** for name/phone (Skeleton renders until useProfile populates); **useRegister seeds from the form variables** because register already carries them (no Skeleton on first Account render for fresh signups). **`useProfile` is mounted at `app/(app)/_layout.tsx`** so it fires the moment <Stack.Protected> lets the user in — option (a) of W3 (immediate auth flip + async profile populate). Option (b) ("don't flip until profile loads") was rejected because it strands users with valid tokens on a transient GET /me 5xx. **Register form now requires firstName + lastName** (zod `.min(1).max(100).refine(trim non-empty)`) matching backend `@NotBlank`. Old deployed Task 002 frontend would now 400 against the current backend; this task closes the window. **Account screens**: `account/index.tsx` (profile card + Edit/Change-pw/My-addresses/Sign-out), `account/edit.tsx` (RHF: firstName/lastName/phone), `account/password.tsx` (RHF: current/new/confirm with the same `passwordSchema` strength check as register; UnauthorizedError routes to the `currentPassword` field via setError, not form root — user needs to know which input is wrong). **Home screen pared back** to just app name + dev-only component gallery link (W8). **i18n**: new `account.*` namespace + `auth.firstName`/`auth.lastName` added to both `en.json` and `ro.json`. **Tests** (38 → 43): `useProfile` fires-when-authed + mirrors, `useProfile` blocked-when-anonymous, `useUpdateProfile` write-through to cache + store, `useChangePassword` happy-path token rotation, `useChangePassword` error-path doesn't rotate. Existing `useLogin.test.tsx` updated to assert the new AuthUser shape (login → null name/phone, register → seeded from variables). **router.d.ts manually edited** with the new account routes per [11] convention; Metro will overwrite on next start (typed-routes generator runs at server start, not on file change). **Pixel 8 live verification (W12) deferred** — needs hands-on device interaction; implementation evidence is the green `tsc --noEmit` + 43/43 jest suite + W1 backend register smoke (201 with the new payload). Connected to [18] backend, [9] routing convention, [10] feature playbook, [11] auth store, [17] logout (parallel pattern).

[20] **Step-up Auth Wire-Format (Task 036, closed 2026-05-25)** - Fixed the parked Task 035 finding where wrong-currentPassword and expired-JWT both produced 401, causing the password-change form to mis-route case (a) (expired JWT mid-flow) as a wrong-current field error. **Root cause** was status-code conflation at the server, papered over in W14 by an `isCredentialEndpoint` URL exclusion in `frontend/src/api/client.ts` that ALSO swallowed legitimate JWT refresh-retry on the same endpoint. **Fix is server-side**: `UserService.changePassword` now throws a new `IncorrectCurrentPasswordException` (distinct from `InvalidCredentialsException` so `/api/auth/login` keeps its 401 contract — same wire shape as before for the login path), and `GlobalExceptionHandler.handleIncorrectCurrentPassword` maps it to **400 / VALIDATION_FAILED with `fieldErrors:[{field:"currentPassword", code:"INVALID_CREDENTIALS", message:"Current password is incorrect"}]` + dual-populated `details:["currentPassword: ..."]` (legacy fallback during transition). `@Transactional(noRollbackFor=...)` updated to list the new exception or the lockout counter increment rolls back (regression-tested in `postPassword_fiveWrongCurrents_thenCorrect_returns423` — 5x400 then 423 LOCKED). **Client-side invariant restored**: `client.ts` 401 interceptor's only exclusion is `/api/auth/refresh` (structural recursion guard); no business-logic URL exclusions. The user's directive ("I want 401 to be tokenRefresh no matter what, I do not want hidden exclusions in the client") is enforced as a regression test in `client.test.ts` named "refreshes and retries on 401 from /api/users/me/password (no hidden URL exclusions)" — that test would have caught the W14 patch. **`FieldError` type widened** at `frontend/src/api/errors.ts` to carry `code: string` (was `{field, message}` only); `fromBackendBody` propagates `fe.code` for the structured-fieldErrors path, legacy `parseDetails` colon-split path emits `code: ''`. **`password.tsx` simplified**: dropped the `UnauthorizedError` special-case (now dead code — wrong-current never produces 401), routed wrong-current via the existing `ValidationError` branch with a `fe.code === 'INVALID_CREDENTIALS'` → `t('account.currentPasswordWrong')` re-localization (the backend message "Current password is incorrect" would otherwise leak English copy to RO users). **Tests**: backend 168→169 (+1 unit test on the new handler in `GlobalExceptionHandlerTest`; updated 2 `UserProfileIntegrationTest` methods + 1 `GlobalExceptionHandler` assertion to assert 400/VALIDATION_FAILED+fieldError). Frontend stays at 44 — updated 5 `errors.test.ts` assertions for the new `code` field, inverted 1 `client.test.ts` regression test, swapped 1 `hooks.test.tsx` mock from raw Error to `ValidationError`. **HTTP-level smoke** against running backend at `JWT_EXPIRATION=30000`: case (b) wrong-current → 400 with the expected structured body verbatim; case (a) expired JWT → 401/Content-Length:0 unchanged; login wrong password → 401/INVALID_CREDENTIALS unchanged. **Live verification on Pixel 8 deferred to user** (W12 — driving the dev client + UI taps not reachable from this session; equivalent unit/integration coverage in place). Connected to [19] Account Tab (deletes its W14 patch + `UnauthorizedError` handler), [18] Profile Endpoints (the contract whose wire shape this revises for one path), [10] Frontend error routing (the ValidationError path this taps), [[feedback-password-change-ux]] (no forced re-login, no client silent re-login).

[21] **Theme Palette System (Task 037, closed 2026-05-28)** - Three curated palettes (sage / indigo / teal) live in `src/theme/colors.ts` as `palettes: Record<PaletteName, Record<ColorScheme, Palette>>` — same 14 semantic-token keys per palette, only values differ. `defaultPalette = 'sage'`. The legacy `colors` export is kept as a thin alias (`palettes.sage`) so `tailwind.config.ts`'s `Object.keys(colors.light)` still enumerates token names (stable across palettes). User picks at runtime from `app/(app)/account/appearance.tsx`; `useThemeStore` persists `{preference, palette}` under AsyncStorage key `theme-prefs-v2` (the old `theme-preference` key is abandoned — pre-prod, no migration). **Key gotcha**: `ThemeProvider`'s `vars()` payload MUST be rebuilt per render (`useMemo` on `[palette, scheme]`) — the previous module-level memo only worked when there was exactly one palette. If you keep the memo at module scope, palette switching silently no-ops. Picker UI uses primary-colored radio dots so the chosen palette is its own preview — the dot color flips the moment the row is tapped. i18n keys at `account.appearance.{title, buttonLabel, palette.{sage,indigo,teal}+Description, theme.{system,light,dark}+Description}` in both en/ro. Connected to [7] Frontend Foundation.

[22] **Account Page Polish + NativeWind/Docker Gotchas (Task 040, closed 2026-05-29)** - **Visual redesign of `app/(app)/account/index.tsx`**: avatar hero (centered `Avatar` primitive in `src/components/ui/Avatar.tsx` — initials in a palette-tinted circle via `bg-primary`) + name `Text variant="h2"` + contact card (`Mail`/`Phone` lucide icon rows) + two grouped action-row cards (`ACCOUNT`: Edit profile / Change password; `PREFERENCES`: My addresses / Appearance) + destructive Sign-out at bottom. Mirrors `appearance.tsx:107-117`'s rounded-card + bordered-Pressable pattern. Action rows: `min-h-16 gap-4 px-4 py-4` with lucide icons at size 22 left, label `Text variant="body"` `flex-1`, `ChevronRight` size 20 right. Icons colored via `useAppTheme().tokens.{foreground,mutedForeground}` (lucide takes a string `color` prop, not Tailwind). Added `lucide-react-native@1.17` as a new dep. New i18n keys `account.section.{account,preferences}` in en + ro. `getInitials` helper exported separately with 4-case unit test (`__tests__/Avatar.test.ts`). **Global Button primitive change**: sizes bumped — `default` h-11→h-12 (48px), `lg` h-12→h-14 (56px) — every primary-action button across the app (login submit, register submit, save-profile, address forms, sign-out, etc.) now renders proportionally taller. Material list-item / filled-button territory. **Critical gotcha #1 — NativeWind `text-*Foreground` class collision via custom `Text` variants**: `cn` in `src/components/ui/cn.ts` is a plain join (no tailwind-merge — the comment says "last-wins via Tailwind's specificity is enough", but that assumption doesn't hold for conflicting `text-*` color tokens through the variant-then-className composition in `Text.tsx`). The custom `Text` component's default `variant="body"` adds `text-foreground` to the className FIRST; an external `text-primaryForeground` / `text-destructiveForeground` passed via className appears AFTER but doesn't reliably win on the device (NativeWind v4's resolution of conflicting color utilities through the variant composition is unreliable here). Symptom: Avatar initials + destructive Button label rendered as near-black on saturated backgrounds. **Fix pattern: for theme-aware colors that MUST apply reliably, resolve via `useAppTheme().tokens.X` and pass as inline `style={{ color: tokens.X }}` — bypass NativeWind entirely.** This pattern is now used in `Avatar.tsx` and `Button.tsx` (label color resolved through a `labelColorKey: Record<ButtonVariant, keyof Palette>` lookup + inline style; the old `text-primaryForeground` className was removed). The same latent bug almost certainly affects other places — audit candidate when revisiting: grep `<Text className=".*text-` and confirm each override actually applies. **Critical gotcha #2 — Metro file watcher misses changes through Docker-on-Windows bind mounts**: Docker Desktop on Windows uses a virtual filesystem (Hyper-V/WSL2 9P) for bind mounts; fsevents/inotify from the host don't propagate reliably into the container. Metro's `jest-haste-map` watcher sees no change → keeps serving the cached transform of `Button.tsx` etc. even after edits. Confirmed by grepping the served bundle for the new code — absent, while `grep` of the same source file inside the container shows the change is on disk. **The pre-existing `CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING` / `WATCHPACK_POLLING` env vars in `frontend/docker-compose.yml:71-73` do NOT help — those are for `chokidar` / webpack-watchpack, not Metro's `jest-haste-map` (no polling option exists in Metro).** Mitigation: new `pnpm dev:reset` script (`frontend/scripts/dev-reset.js`) that does the full force-stop → rm cached `BridgelessReactNativeDevBundle.js` → `expo start --clear` cycle. Caller still needs to swipe-from-Recents + cold-launch after. Build-stamp diagnostic added to `app/(app)/index.tsx` (`const BUNDLE_STAMP = new Date().toLocaleTimeString(...)` at module-load, displayed as "build HH:mm:ss" under the `isDev` gate) so future "is my code live?" debugging takes seconds instead of minutes. **Parked**: row touch-feedback strength on Account chevron rows — current state combines `android_ripple={{ color: tokens.foreground }}` + pressed-state `style.backgroundColor = tokens.border`, but user reports the visual cue is still subtle. Probable root cause: `onPress` navigates immediately so the ripple/bg-flash animation barely renders before the screen transitions. Future attempt: lingering active state via `onPressIn`/`onPressOut` + setTimeout, or Reanimated scale/opacity animation. Apply same treatment to `appearance.tsx` `PickerRow` for consistency when we revisit. Connected to [7] Frontend Foundation, [21] Theme Palette.

[23] **Push Notifications — Firebase/FCM Stack (Task 014, code 2026-06-05; on-device verified 2026-06-08)** - Full delivery rail: backend token registry + FCM send + frontend registration/handling. **Backend** (188 tests): V10 `push_tokens` (bigserial PK, `user_id` FK→users ON DELETE CASCADE, `token` TEXT UNIQUE, `platform` VARCHAR, TIMESTAMPTZ timestamps); `PushTokenService` with atomic `INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` upsert (avoids StrictMode double-invoke 500); `FcmClient` via `firebase-admin:9.4.3`, gated on `app.firebase.enabled` (default false — dev/CI run credentialless, `FcmClient` no-ops); `NotificationService.sendToSelf` fans out with post-loop dead-token pruning (UNREGISTERED → delete after iteration, never mid-loop); `POST/DELETE /api/users/me/push-tokens` + `POST /api/notifications/test` (self-only, all envs); `UserRateLimitFilter` extended with `/api/notifications` prefix. **Design decision — Expo Push Service rejected**: EAS projectId-free, direct FCM chosen; iOS later = APNs `.p8` key uploaded to Firebase → FCM relays, one server SDK forever. **Wire-format**: token never on any response DTO. **DELETE body → query param**: `DELETE /api/users/me/push-tokens?token=…` — proxies may strip DELETE bodies; query param is safe through Cloud Run. **Frontend** (69 jest, tsc clean): `expo-notifications` replaced with `@react-native-firebase/messaging ^24` + `@notifee/react-native ^9.1.8`. `@react-native-firebase/app` config plugin + `android.googleServicesFile: ./google-services.json` in `app.json` (NOT app.config.ts — that only spreads `...config`). `package.json main` → `./index.js` (custom entry): `setBackgroundMessageHandler` + `notifee.onBackgroundEvent` registered via `require` BEFORE `require('expo-router/entry')` — ES `import` hoists and would boot React first. **Android 13+ permission**: must call `notifee.requestPermission()` (not just `messaging().requestPermission()`) — the latter returns AUTHORIZED without the OS prompt on Android 13+. `src/lib/notifications.ts` facade isolates all vendor SDK calls; `src/features/notifications/` follows the add-a-feature playbook [10]. **Logout deregister**: `useLogout` wraps `deregisterCurrentDevice()` in its OWN try/catch before the `service.logout()` try — a shared try would let a deregister throw skip the Task 029 server-side revoke. **pnpm-workspace.yaml `allowBuilds`**: RNFirebase pulls `@firebase/util` + `protobufjs` whose postinstalls pnpm blocks — set both to `false` (pure-JS, not needed for native build). **WF8 VERIFIED on-device (Pixel 8, 2026-06-08)** — foreground + killed + tap-to-navigate all confirmed single-notification. Final payload/priority decisions (changed from the original draft): **(a) notification payload, NOT data-only** — data-only FCM messages are never delivered to a force-stopped app, so they can't show when the app isn't running; a `notification` payload (+ `data` for the deep link) is OS-displayed when bg/killed, goes to `onMessage`→Notifee in foreground, and the background handler (`index.js`) is a deliberate NO-OP (displaying there too = duplicate). **(b) NORMAL priority, NOT HIGH** — HIGH + a data payload force-wakes the killed app to deliver data; a killed **dev client** can't ack (needs Metro) so FCM **retries ~10-15s later → duplicate notification**. NORMAL stops the retry; `data` still arrives on tap via `getInitialNotification`. *Prod trade-off (parked):* NORMAL can delay delivery in Doze; HIGH likely fine in a release build (no Metro) — revisit HIGH for prod reminders. **(c) duplicate display fix**: bind `onMessage`/tap/refresh listeners ONCE at module scope (`bindListenersOnce` in features/notifications/hooks.ts) — per-effect binding leaked duplicate listeners under StrictMode/reload. **Backend credential resolution (Task 014, on-device session)**: `FirebaseConfig` takes an **absolute** `app.firebase.credentials` path (CWD-independent across `java -jar` vs Maven-container runs) and falls back to `GoogleCredentials.getApplicationDefault()` when blank (Cloud Run uses its runtime SA, no key file). Default path `/app/<key>.json`; root `docker-compose.yml` mounts the gitignored key there read-only. Tests stay credentialless via `application-test.yml` (`app.firebase.enabled=false`). Secret gitignored (`*firebase-adminsdk*.json`, `google-services.json`). **Notifee Android native config gotchas (Expo CNG)**: (1) `app.notifee:core` resolves only via an `expo-build-properties` `extraMavenRepos` entry → `../../node_modules/@notifee/react-native/android/libs` (relative to `android/app`; one `../` resolves wrong) — Notifee's own `allprojects` repo injection is ignored by RN 0.81's centralized repo management.
```
