(2) Stale generated `android/` won't pick up new config plugins — needs `expo prebuild --clean`, which **fails EBUSY on the `rn_android_cxx` named-volume mount** → temporarily comment that volume out of `frontend/docker-compose.yml`, recreate, clean-prebuild, then **restore it** (detached `.cxx` makes Gradle C++ output hammer the Windows bind mount → `java.io.IOException` build failures). **Dev device/networking (containerized Metro + physical Pixel 8)**: host `adb` server must run with `-a` (all interfaces) so the `rn-dev` container reaches the device via `ADB_SERVER_SOCKET=host.docker.internal:5037`. Device reaches Metro + backend via `adb -d reverse tcp:8081` + `tcp:8080`; the app's `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` must be **`http://localhost:8080`** for a physical device (the compose default `host.docker.internal:8080` is unresolvable on-device) — start Metro with that env override. Relaunch the dev client at `frontend://expo-development-client/?url=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8081`. Connected to [6] Backend Architecture, [7] Frontend Foundation, [10] Add-a-feature playbook, [17] Logout Wiring.

[24] **Backend Test Runner — machine-agnostic helper (Task 042, closed 2026-06-08)** - `.claude/bin/run-backend-tests` is the single entry point for the backend Maven suite — the `run-tests` skill and CLAUDE.md "Backend Build" both invoke it (no more hardcoded `C:/Users/matao/...` paths, which were wrong on every non-matao machine). Derives REPO_ROOT from `${BASH_SOURCE[0]}` (two dirs up from `.claude/bin`), backend = `$repo/backend`, m2 = `$HOME/.m2`. Extra args pass through to `mvn` (`run-backend-tests -Dtest=Foo test`); default is `test`. **Two non-obvious requirements baked in:** (1) cleans `target/` as **root first** — a stale root-owned `target/` from any prior root container run makes Maven resource filtering fail with `Operation not permitted` once the build drops to uid 1000; (2) runs tests as `-u 1000 -e HOME=/tmp` with `.m2` mounted at **`/tmp/.m2`** (NOT `/root/.m2`) — uid 1000 is mandatory (embedded-postgres initdb refuses root, Task 018) but then can't write `/root`, so HOME must be a writable dir. **Windows/Git-Bash gotcha:** mount sources are converted with `cygpath -m` (→ `C:/Users/...`, Docker-Desktop-compatible) and the script sets `MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1` + `MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL='*'` — without this, MSYS rewrites the container-side `-w /app` arg into a host path (`C:/Program Files/Git/app`) and `docker run` fails. Both are no-ops on Linux/Mac. Verified: 188 tests green on the `crist` machine, no manual edits. **Apple-Silicon / arm64 (Task 009):** `embedded-postgres` ships only `linux-amd64` binaries (Task 030), so on an `aarch64` Maven container the suite errored `Missing embedded postgres binaries` at every integration test's `<clinit>`. Fixed by an `os.arch=aarch64`-activated pom `<profile>` adding a BOM-managed (version-less) `embedded-postgres-binaries-linux-arm64v8` — amd64 (Windows/CI) dependency graph proven byte-identical (profile inactive there); suite now 242/0/0 on arm64. Test container is glibc; details in `backend/CLAUDE.md`. Connected to [6] Backend Architecture, Task 018 embedded-postgres.

[25] **AloVet Identity Restructure** (Task 047, Slice 0a task 1) - First AloVet step [4]: split the fused `users` table into an auth **`Account`** + role-bearing profiles, **roles derived from profile/grant existence — there is NO role field**. Java pkg `user/`→`account/`, `User`→`Account` (URL paths stay `/api/users`, `/api/auth`; refresh/push token `user_id` columns kept — cosmetic rename deferred). New tables (forward-only **V12–V15**): `accounts` (+`locale`/`status` enums), `owners` (own PK + UNIQUE `account_id`; the OWNER role; every account backfilled + `register` now `@Transactional` creating Account+Owner), `medics` (scalar cols only — presence/rating/Stripe deferred to later slices), `admin_grants` (multi-grant, revocable). **`addresses` repointed Account→Owner** (`owner_id` FK, `idx_addresses_owner_id`; AddressService resolves the caller's Owner, 404 if none; query-count baseline 3→4). `RoleResolver`/`AccountRoles` derive roles + the `sameUnderlyingAccount` basis INV-001 will assert. Data model bumped **v2.6** (names → Account; `Owner.displayName`/`Medic.fullName` dropped). Gotcha: raw-SQL table refs in `PerfDataSeeder` weren't caught by the entity rename — only broke when the table was renamed (V12). **Deferred (later slices):** policy middleware + INV-002 (**done — [27]**), Pet/Interaction/Order clinical spine (**done — [28]**), invariant harness, `Account.isTest` (**done — task 060 [34]**), Consent table, Clinic table + `clinic_id` FKs, Medic child tables, `/api/users`→`/api/accounts` URL rename. **Account-module drift cleanup (Task 061, account/ main code review):** 4 fixes, suite 370 green, OpenAPI unchanged. **D1** `Account` dropped Lombok `@Data` → `@Getter/@Setter` + identity equals/hashCode (mirrors Owner/Medic) — `@Data`'s all-field `toString()` leaked the BCrypt hash/phone/lockout to logs (the Task-028 B4-F1 leak class, log-side; `@JsonIgnore` only guards Jackson). **D2** legacy zoneless `accounts.created_at`+`accepted_terms_at` → `TIMESTAMPTZ` (**V27**, the V4 refresh-tokens precedent finally applied to accounts) + `Account.createdAt`/`AccountProfileResponse.createdAt` `LocalDateTime`→`Instant` (Instant-over-zoneless-TIMESTAMP made stored wall-clock JVM-TZ-dependent); contract-compatible (both serialize `date-time`). **D3** field `@Autowired`→constructor injection in AccountController/AccountService/RoleResolver (matches MedicReservationService). **D4** stale `userService`/`userRepository`→`accountService`/`accountRepository`. **Then iterated module-by-module + a project-wide sweep (same task 061), final suite 368 green:** **Q2/Q3 (account follow-up):** CLAUDE.md "Current user" rule rewritten to bless `@AuthenticationPrincipal Account` (was doubly stale: `(User)` type + manual cast); server password floor raised `min=8`→`12` (RegisterRequest+ChangePasswordRequest, aligned to frontend zxcvbn-12; OpenAPI `minLength` regen). **auth/ (A1–A3):** RefreshToken dropped `@Data`→`@Getter/@Setter`+identity equals/hashCode (toString leaked `tokenHash`, the stored credential secret); field-injection→constructor in AuthService/RefreshTokenService/RefreshTokenCleanupService/AuthController; `userRepository`→`accountRepository`; **+ a RED-FLAG removal:** deleted `RefreshTokenService.validate()` — a dead (zero prod callers) yet **security-incomplete fork** of `rotate()`'s check that OMITTED reuse-detection (`handleReuse` family-burn), a trap for a future caller; ported its unknown/expired coverage onto `rotate()` unit tests, fixed stale `validate()` comments. **address/ (B1–B3+B5):** Address dropped `@Data` (lazy-`owner` walk); `addresses.created_at` `TIMESTAMP`→`TIMESTAMPTZ` (**V28**) + `Address`/`AddressResponse.createdAt` `LocalDateTime`→`Instant`; field-injection→constructor; **B5 closed the parked Q2-followup** — `AddressController.currentUser()` manual `SecurityContextHolder` cast → `@AuthenticationPrincipal Account` on all 4 endpoints (test needed `AuthenticationPrincipalArgumentResolver` in standaloneSetup; springdoc hides the param → no spec drift). **Project-wide sweep (all remaining modules):** the 3 cluster patterns swept — `@Data`-on-entity and `LocalDateTime`/zoneless-`TIMESTAMP` were **already fully eradicated** (every zoneless table now TIMESTAMPTZ via V4/V27/V28; newer tables born TIMESTAMPTZ); field-`@Autowired` = 7 last sites (notification ×5 + `common/security/JwtUtil` ×2, the oldest Task-013/014/028 code) all converted, FcmClient's `@Autowired(required=false)`→`@Nullable` ctor param. **Codebase now 100% constructor injection** (`@Autowired` in main = 0); 4 tests rewritten off `ReflectionTestUtils` onto constructors. **Pattern: drift was concentrated entirely in pre-AloVet (User-era) code; AloVet-era code (interaction/order/pet/jobs/taxonomy/policy/audit/statemachine) was already clean.** **Still open (parked):** Q1 no static analysis in the build — **RESOLVED Task 074:** SpotBugs + find-sec-bugs wired (unbound plugin like OWASP, run via `.claude/bin/run-backend-spotbugs`, Lombok-generated methods skipped via `backend/lombok.config`, baseline 126 findings triaged to a clean gate — EI_EXPOSE (124) + 1 intended CT_CONSTRUCTOR_THROW excluded with rationale in `spotbugs-exclude.xml`; the 1 find-sec-bugs PATH_TRAVERSAL (Firebase creds path) **hardened** (absolute+normalize+toRealPath+regular-file) then the residual taint-FP suppressed precisely via method-level `@SuppressFBWarnings`; see backend/CLAUDE.md §Static analysis). Checkstyle/PMD/Spotless/ErrorProne still not added (SpotBugs chosen for correctness+security with the cleanest Lombok fit). Q4 RoleResolver.rolesOf 3 queries/call (collapse if it lands on the policy hot path); low-value nits (request DTOs still `@Data` not `record` — but `record` wouldn't fix the password-in-toString risk anyway; AddressService not `@Transactional` on read-modify-write — last-write-wins acceptable for single-row). Plan: `docs/specs/2026-06-14-slice0a-task1-identity-restructure-plan.md`. See [6][12][13][18].

[26] **Frontend Error-Handling Framework** (Task 049, device-verified Pixel 8 2026-06-17) - App-wide layered handling that generalizes [19]'s Account fix across every read/write; supersedes [10]'s mutation-only error-routing convention. **Classification** (`errors.ts`, extends [7]): adds `TimeoutError`; `NetworkError`=online connection-fail, `ServerError`=5xx-only, `OfflineError`=pre-flight. `client.ts` splits a no-response failure into offline/timeout/network via `error.code` (axios `transitional.clarifyTimeoutError:true`→`ETIMEDOUT`). `isRetryable`=5xx||Network (timeout/offline/4xx NEVER — no backend idempotency keys, so the client gate is the only duplicate guard); `isTransient`=Offline|Timeout|Network|Server (the buckets the global toast owns). **Refresh cascade — the core fix:** logout ONLY on a real refresh-401 (`UnauthorizedError` from `/api/auth/refresh`); a transient refresh failure (timeout/5xx/network) PRESERVES the session (`client.ts:140-153`). Single-flight refresh (`refreshInFlight`, cleared in `finally`) collapses a 401 burst to one rotation. **Reads:** pure `resolveQueryView<T>` (`lib/queryView.ts`) → loading|error|empty{stale}|content{data,stale} (branch-2 applies `isEmpty` to cached symmetrically with branch-1); `readQueryOptions()` bakes refetchOnMount+retry+60s staleTime; `QueryErrorState` (connectivity-vs-server copy + Retry with a `retrying` spinner); `ConnectivityBanner` (offline→`noInternet` / unreachable→`serverUnreachable`, mounted once at root above both stacks); `serverReachable` (`lib/network.ts`) flips false ONLY on transport no-answer, NEVER on 5xx (user directive). `accountView.ts` delegates to `resolveQueryView`. Reads with no Zustand fallback set `gcTime:Infinity` so the persisted cache is their last-known-good (addresses). **`useRevalidateOnFocus(stale, refetch)`** (`src/hooks/`): refetch on screen RE-focus when stale — tab nav keeps screens mounted so `refetchOnMount` can't re-probe on a tab switch. Reads `stale` via a ref + skips the initial (mount) focus → fires only on genuine re-entry; if `stale` were a `useCallback` dep, RN's `useFocusEffect` re-runs on identity change and TanStack flips `isStale` every staleTime → a 60s **poll loop** (caught on-device via backend logs). **Writes:** global `MutationCache.onError` toasts transient (`saveFailed*`); shared `routeFormError` (`lib/formErrors.ts`) routes Validation→fields / Conflict+Unauthorized→root / transient→nothing (toast owns) across all 6 mutation screens; non-form delete toasts `deleteFailed` for non-transient kinds (else the optimistic rollback is silent). **Backend coupling ([6]):** `spring.datasource.hikari.connection-timeout=5000` (`${DB_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT}`) — MUST stay under the 15s client timeout. The 30s default let a DB-down request the client had already abandoned (15s) still COMPLETE server-side and rotate a refresh token; the client's next retry then presented the now-revoked token → `RefreshTokenService.handleReuse` burned the family → **spurious logout on DB recovery**. Fail-fast keeps client+server in sync and collapses the down-DB hang (~30s→~5s). A valid token + DB-down returns 401 (JWT filter's `findByEmail` throws) not 503 — accepted (refresh then 500s fast → session preserved). **Windows+Docker dev learnings:** (1) Metro 9P-watcher gap ([22]) silently serves a STALE bundle → on-device "the fix doesn't work" is often old code; `pnpm dev:reset` + cold-launch, and PROVE the served bundle is current by grepping it (`curl localhost:8081/index.bundle | grep <marker>`). (2) `[auth]` breadcrumbs in the container's `/tmp/metro.log` are the on-device debug oracle. (3) `adb reverse tcp:8081`+`tcp:8080` don't survive a device reconnect — re-assert them. Spec `docs/specs/2026-06-16-frontend-error-handling-design.md`; builds on [19][10][7][22]. Full P1–P7 trace in task 049. ↗

[27] **Authorization Policy Middleware** (Task 050, Slice 0a task 2) - The single authorization layer (INV-002), the RBAC that [6] had **parked** until now. **Mechanism:** a hand-rolled `common/policy/PolicyService.require(Account, Capability)` bean — NOT Spring `@PreAuthorize`/authorities (the principal carries empty authorities; derived roles cost 3 DB lookups via [25]'s `RoleResolver`, so eager per-request authority population would tax hot owner-self routes; pilot admin actions are CLI/service-layer not HTTP, so a service-layer call guards both). `require` resolves roles → `Role.setOf(AccountRoles)` → intersects `PolicyMatrix.rolesFor(cap)`; empty intersection ⇒ audit `denied` + throw `ForbiddenException`→**403** `FORBIDDEN`. `can(...)` is a silent probe (no audit/throw). **Scope decision (3 tiers):** T1 owner/medic self-scoped = authentication+ownership, **existing routes untouched, NOT matrix-gated**; T2 owner-vs-medic action gates = profile-existence, no endpoints yet; **T3 admin = where the matrix does real work**. Full v1.4 matrix (26 capabilities) modeled as data in `PolicyMatrix` (`@Component`, **injectable** — public no-arg = real map, package-private `Map` ctor = test seam; **fail-closed** `getOrDefault(…,Set.of())`). `Capability`/`Role` enums; `Role` projects `AccountRoles`(owner/medic bools + `AdminRole`s) into one set. **First real consumer (so INV-002 has a live target):** `POST /api/admin/accounts/{id}/suspend`+`/reactivate` (`admin/` pkg) → `require(SUSPEND_REACTIVATE_ACCOUNTS→{platform_ops,superadmin})`, capability check **before** the `{id}` lookup (no existence leak — non-admin on a missing id gets 403 not 404), flips `Account.status` active↔suspended, `deleted` terminal→409 `ACCOUNT_STATUS_CONFLICT`. **F1 — suspension is enforced:** `JwtAuthenticationFilter` now rejects a non-`active` principal (after the `isRevokedByLogout` gate, `status != active → return` ⇒ no SecurityContext ⇒ 401), mirroring the logout-revocation pattern — otherwise suspend flipped a column nothing read. **Audit (durable, built now — roles-permissions-v1.4 principle 3):** `common/audit/` + **V16** `audit_log` (append-only; `acting_account_id` bare FK — accounts soft-delete via status so the FK never blocks survival; indexed FK + `created_at`). `AuditService.recordDenied` uses **`REQUIRES_NEW`** (survives the caller tx's rollback when `ForbiddenException` propagates); `recordAllowed` joins the mutation's tx (atomic). **INV-002 tests:** exhaustive matrix-fidelity (regression guard; initial fidelity = review-verified, F3) + fail-closed unit (F2) + `InvariantINV002SuspendIntegrationTest` (owner/medic/finance/clinic_admin→403, ops/superadmin→success, deleted→409, denial+success audited). Suite 216→242, 0 skipped. **Deferred:** read-audit-logs API (`READ_AUDIT_LOGS` modeled, no consumer); T2 action-gate enforcement (task 3+); clinic-scoping (no Clinic table); INV-001 (needs interactions). Spec `docs/specs/2026-06-17-slice0a-policy-middleware-design.md`, plan `…-plan.md`. See [6][25][12].

[28] **Clinical-Spine Schema** (Task 051, Slice 0a task 3) - The core domain schema every later AloVet slice hangs off — **schema + write-guard only**, no behavior (matching/dispatch/payments/state-machine are later slices). Forward-only **V18–V22** off the v2.6 identity model [25]; suite 242→278, 0 skipped. **Packages:** `taxonomy/` (PatientCategory + SpeciesMap), `pet/` (Pet), `order/` (Order, OrderLine, PaymentRecord), `interaction/` (Interaction, InteractionPet + guard/service). **V18 taxonomy:** `patient_categories` (code, **name JSONB locale-map** via `@JdbcTypeCode(SqlTypes.JSON)` — data-model decision 9, adding a language is data not migration) + `species_map` (species→category); seeded 4 pilot categories (dogs_cats/small_mammals/birds/reptiles_exotics, ro+en) + 10 species. **V19 Pet:** owner-owned; `patient_category_id` denormalized from species at write (decision 3), **NULL = needs_review** (unmapped species, unmatchable) — `PetService.create` derives it via `SpeciesMapRepository.findBySpecies`; `temperament_flags` JSONB; `weight_kg` NUMERIC (a measurement — NOT money). **V20 Order / V22 OrderLine+PaymentRecord:** the billing layer (decision 2 — money attaches to Order, never Interaction). **All money is `long`/`Long` minor units → BIGINT, never float/BigDecimal (INV-003)**, proved by a JDBC-metadata reflection test. **V21 Interaction — the spine** (decision 1): `order_id` **NOT NULL** (1 order = 1 interaction; `InteractionService.create` assembles a draft Order in-tx — F5), `owner_id`→Owner, `medic_id?`→Medic (null until matched), `address_id?`→addresses, `city_id` plain BIGINT (no City table yet), `address_snapshot` JSONB, 19-state `status` cache (set at create: instant→open, scheduled→requested (v2.7 amendment, Slice 3a; was booked), visit→requested; transitions are the slice-0b runner's job — now built, [30]). **INV-024/025 enforced TWICE** — `InteractionWriteGuard` (`@Component`, pure methods, friendly 400 `INVALID_INTERACTION`) **AND** a DB `CHECK chk_interaction_combination` so the invariant survives a caller that bypasses the service (decision 11). **Pilot writes only 3 combos** (not the matrix's 4): teleconsult (no modality/geo, scheduled|instant) + visit·home·scheduled (city+address required); **`visit|clinic` is enum-valued but creation-disabled** (F1, data-model :93). INV-026 (instant single-pet) + INV-001 (no self-interaction, owner≠medic account) stay app-level (cross-row/cross-account, not single-row CHECKs). **Deferred:** OrderLine/PaymentRecord repos (no consumer yet — entities+tables only); INV-019 partial-unique (needs instant-dispatch write path); INV-001 real enforcement at the medic-assignment transition (medicId null at create — coverage here is the pure predicate + synthetic pairing); `Account.isTest` derivation (**done — task 060 [34]**); address_snapshot freeze; category-recompute job. Spec `docs/specs/2026-06-17-slice0a-policy-middleware-design.md` sibling; data model `alovet-data-model-v2.7.md` §2. See [6][25][27].

[29] **Invariant Test Harness** (Task 052, **closes Slice 0a**) - Formalizes the `INV-xxx` test convention into a machine-checked backbone (guiding-principle 2: "every invariant a named test; a failing invariant stops the build"). **Not new behavior tests** — the 6 0a-provable invariants already had named tests ([27][28]); this wires the ledger + guard. **`support/InvariantRegistry`** (test-scope enum, 38 constants INV-001…038): each is `COVERED`→a `Class<?>` test ref, or `DEFERRED`→`targetSlice`+`reason`. COVERED = {001,002,003,024,025,026} (identity, money/schema-shape, combinations); the other 32 are deferred with the missing machinery named (money→Slice 3/0b, dispatch_and_state→0b/4, matching→1/3/6/7, lineage/privacy→various). **`InvariantRegistryTest`** (single `@Test`, +1 → suite **279**) is build-stopping if the ledger drifts: 38 contiguous ids; covered set == pinned `EXPECTED_COVERED_0A`; each covered test's simpleName `startsWith("InvariantINV"+%03d)` (exact prefix — an unanchored `0*` regex would let INV-002 match INV-024's class, plan-review C1); each covered class carries a matching **`@Tag("INV-xxx")`** verified via `getAnnotationsByType` (fuses registry↔tag, C2); each deferred has slice+reason. **Convention (the pattern PRs reference):** name `InvariantINV###*` + `@Tag("INV-xxx")`; run one via `run-backend-tests -Dtest.groups=INV-024 test` (**`-Dtest.groups`, NOT `-Dgroups`** — the POM binds `<groups>${test.groups}</groups>`, plan-review I1). To convert a slice 0a invariant test to public/cross-package the 6 classes were made `public`. Coverage matrix: `docs/slice0a/INVARIANT_COVERAGE.md`; convention also in `backend/CLAUDE.md`. **Deferred:** registry↔`invariants.yaml` id-set cross-check needs the full repo (runner mounts only `backend/`) → CI step (task 003); each deferred invariant's real test lands with its slice's machinery (flip the registry + `EXPECTED_COVERED_0A` then). **Slice 0a now complete** (tasks 047/050/051/052: identity, policy, clinical spine, invariant harness). See [25][27][28]. *(Task 053 flipped INV-021/022 to COVERED — `EXPECTED_COVERED_0A` renamed `EXPECTED_COVERED`, now 8 covered; see [30]. Task 055 flipped INV-031, now 9 covered; see [32].)* *(Task 056, Slice 0b task 8 part 1, audit-only: re-audited all 29 deferred vs the 5/6/7 machinery → **zero newly provable**, no flips (`EXPECTED_COVERED` stays 9). Key reframing: "task 8 — remaining invariants" is NOT a batch-wiring step — exec-strategy §5 mandates invariants land **incrementally with their consuming slice**, and tasks 5/6/7 already did their own flips, so nothing remains that 0b machinery can prove. Corrected 5 stale target-slices/reasons that read "Slice 0b/X" but whose real consumers are later slices: INV-009→Slice 3, INV-016→Slice 4, INV-020→Slice 4, INV-032 (reason sharpened: `is_test` on clinical entities but no Account.isTest root + no analytics), INV-033→Slice 8 (task 059). Registry↔`INVARIANT_COVERAGE.md` re-diffed all 29 rows = in sync.)*

[30] **State-Machine Runner** (Task 053, Slice 0b task 5) - The engine that legally moves an Interaction's status — the behavior [28] deferred as "the slice-0b runner's job". **Pure kernel only**: no guards/side-effects (every YAML `guard:`/`side_effect:` references Slice 3/4 machinery — payments, presence, VideoSession — so they're the *caller's* job; `fire` returns the `Transition` for a future orchestrator to act on in the same tx). **`common/statemachine/`** (generic, zero JPA/Spring, `String`-typed unit-tested): `Transition<S,E>` record, `TransitionTable<S,E>` (builder; `(from,event)→to` `Map.copyOf`; expands YAML `from`-lists; ambiguous-key→`IllegalStateException`, identical-dup no-op; self-loops legal), `StateMachine<S,E>` (`fire`→target or `UndeclaredTransitionException` = INV-022's behavioral half). **`interaction/` consumers:** `InteractionEvent` (28-event union), `InteractionStateMachines` (`@Component`; 3 code tables — instant/scheduled/visit — transcribed verbatim from YAML, built in ctor so a bad edge fails app-start; `forInteraction` selects via per-machine `applies_when` predicates — teleconsult keys `{type,bookingMode}`, visit `{type,modality}` — throwing on BOTH zero and >1 matches; package-private `Registered`+list-ctor test seam), `InteractionTransitionRunner` (`@Service`): `fire` re-fetches by id (mutates a **managed** entity so `setStatus` flushes — a detached caller would silently break INV-021), `machine.fire` → append `InteractionStatusEvent` → `setStatus`, all one tx; `initialize` sets status+saves+writes the **genesis** row (from/event null) so every status is event-backed from birth (INV-021). **INV-021 enforced (app-level, single enforcement):** `Interaction.setStatus` is now `@Setter(AccessLevel.PACKAGE)` — the runner is the **sole** status mutator (compile-blocked elsewhere); a DB-trigger "second enforcement" is consciously deferred (status==latest-event is cross-row, not a single-row CHECK like INV-024/025). **Ledger:** **V23** `interaction_status_events` (append-only, real FK to interactions, ordered by `BIGSERIAL id` NOT `occurred_at` — a genesis-then-fire in one tx can share a timestamp). **Source of truth = code tables**; the 3 interaction YAMLs **relocated** `docs/slice0a/statemachines/`→`backend/src/test/resources/statemachines/` (pointer README left) so the build-stopping **`InvariantINV022InteractionFidelityTest`** (snakeyaml, via Spring Boot transitive) can assert code==YAML set-equal (transitions/states/terminals) — drift either way is red (proven by mutating a YAML). `InvariantINV021StatusEventLedgerIntegrationTest` proves status==latest-event after create/fire/self-loop + atomic no-write-on-reject. INV-021/022 flipped to COVERED [29]. **Deferred:** Order/DispatchOffer/Medic.presence machines (their slices — S3/S4); background jobs (task 6 — now built, [31]); concurrency/idempotency (task 7); remaining invariants+OpenAPI (task 8); the end-of-0b scripted demo. See [28][29][6].

[31] **Background-Job Engine** (Task 054, Slice 0b task 6) - DB-backed, durable, at-least-once job queue — the home the data-model repeatedly names as "the job subsystem" for ~11 time-based duties. **Kernel-only** (same discipline as [30]): ship the generic engine + the ONE duty whose machinery exists today (category-recompute); every other listed duty (presence auto-expiry, dispatch/confirm timeouts, 8-min watchdog, medic_no_show, T-48h auth, SlotHold expiry, reminder materialization, webhook retries, demo-env refresh) references later-slice entities (DispatchOffer/Medic.presence/SlotHold/StripeEvent/Reminder/VideoSession) and lands with its slice — the engine is the seam. **`jobs/`** (generic): `ScheduledJob` entity + **V24** `scheduled_jobs` (id, job_type, `payload` JSONB, **4-state** `status` pending|running|succeeded|dead — a retryable failure rests in `pending` with `run_at` advanced, no separate `failed` state; run_at, attempts/max_attempts, lease_owner/lease_expires_at, last_error, dedupe_key, created/updated; index `(status,run_at)` for the poller, partial-unique `dedupe_key` **scoped to live rows** (`WHERE … status IN ('pending','running')`) so duplicates coalesce while live but a key frees on terminal → recurring duties re-enqueue (impl-review [1])). `JobService`: `enqueue` (dedupe → return existing) + `claimDueJobs` (the load-bearing atomic claim) + `markSucceeded`/`markFailed`(backoff→pending or dead at max)/`markDead`(permanent, no retry). **Claim = select-then-update**, not UPDATE…RETURNING (avoids Hibernate `@Modifying`+RETURNING friction): `ScheduledJobRepository.lockDueJobIds` native `SELECT id … WHERE due-pending OR lease-expired-running … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT n` then flip the locked rows to running + lease + attempts++ in the SAME `@Transactional` (lock held to commit) — so two workers can never both claim a row (proven by a deterministic two-thread/`CountDownLatch` contention test, not a thread-race). `JobBackoff` (pure, `min(base·2^(n-1),cap)`, base 10s cap 1h, overflow-guarded, exhaustively unit-tested). **Transaction split (the [30]/proxy lesson, three beans):** `JobRunner` (`@Service @Transactional run(id)` — load managed entity, dispatch handler, markSucceeded; handler writes + success commit atomically, a throw rolls back handler work); `JobExecutor` (`@Component`, NO tx — `try run / catch PermanentJobException→markDead / catch Exception→markFailed`, each bookkeeping its OWN tx so it survives the handler rollback; **never propagates** → one poison job can't stall the loop); `JobWorker` (`@Component` — `drain()` = claim batch (own committed tx, lease durable before any handler runs) then `executor.execute(id)` per job; `@Scheduled scheduledPoll()` gated by `JobWorkerProperties.enabled`, test profile false so the timer never races `drain()`). **SPI:** `JobHandler` (`type()`/`handle(job)`; throw `PermanentJobException` = non-recoverable → dead-letter, anything else = transient → retry); `JobHandlerRegistry` (`@Component`, type→handler map built in ctor, **duplicate type fails app-start**). **First real handler:** `pet/CategoryRecomputeHandler` (engine in `jobs/`, consumers in their domain — [30]'s split) re-derives `patient_category_id` for **all active pets** of a remapped species (NOT needs_review-only — a remap to a different category must move already-categorized pets) via the **shared `PetService.applyCategory`** path that `create` also uses (no derivation divergence); missing/blank `species` payload → permanent failure. **Tests:** +40 (306→**346**, 0 skipped) — contention claim-once, retry/backoff/dead-letter, **stale-lease reclaim** (crash recovery), poison-doesn't-stall + bookkeeping-failure-doesn't-propagate, **handler side-write rolls back on throw while the failure is still recorded** (the tx-split payoff), recompute null→set + remap-move + idempotent, dedupe-key-frees-on-terminal. **Impl-review:** 2 fixed (per-live dedupe; `execute` never propagates even if bookkeeping throws), 3 parked as later-slice/scaling — unbounded recompute scan (pilot-scale OK), lease-renewal for slow handlers, lease-owner CAS guard (all multi-instance/long-handler → task 7). Committing job tests carry `@AfterEach TRUNCATE scheduled_jobs` (`(status,run_at)`-queried table bleeds JVM-wide otherwise — the `PerfDataSeeder` trap). **No new INV covered** (the engine enables later duties' invariants — INV-014/015/031/036 etc. — which land with task 7/8). Composes with [15]'s `@Scheduled` cleanup (doesn't replace it). Convention in `backend/CLAUDE.md`. **Deferred:** all duties except recompute (their slices); the ops action that enqueues recompute on a SpeciesMap edit (admin tooling); multi-instance coordination beyond the row lease. See [28][30][15][6].

[32] **Concurrency & Idempotency Scaffolding** (Task 055, Slice 0b task 7) - The race-safe seam (data-model decision 11) the money/dispatch/booking flows consume — built **before** any flow needs it so slices 3/4/6 don't each hand-roll racy claim logic. **Kernel-only** (same discipline as [30][31]): ship the primitives + prove the invariants provable *now*; defer those whose consumers don't exist yet. **V25** `slot_holds` + `stripe_events`. **Three primitives:** (1) **`StripeEventDedupeService.processOnce(eventId,type,handler)`** (`order/`) — webhook dedupe: **SELECT-for-existence first** (the real path — Stripe retries are sequential, seconds apart), the `stripe_events.stripe_event_id` UNIQUE index is the **concurrent-race backstop only**; a unique violation aborts the whole PG tx + marks it rollback-only (plan-review C1), so the rare concurrent loser **rolls back entirely** (handler work undone with the ledger row, in one tx — at-least-once + idempotent) rather than "catch-and-continue". (2) **`MedicReservationService.tryReserve(medicId, reservedUntil)`** (`account/`) — medic-exclusivity reservation **gate**: one atomic JPQL `@Modifying UPDATE Medic SET presence=reserved WHERE id=? AND presence=online` (`MedicRepository.reserveIfOnline`) returning affected-row count; exactly one concurrent caller gets 1 (PG row-lock + WHERE re-check / EvalPlanQual). Caller supplies `reservedUntil` (flow owns its confirm-timeout — primitive stays policy-free). Pure gate, **no offer side-effects**. (3) **`booking/` SlotHold** — `SlotHoldService.createHold` runs a **per-medic `pg_advisory_xact_lock`** (acquired via shared `@PersistenceContext EntityManager`, held to commit — same connection as the overlap-check + insert) → app-level overlap feasibility (half-open `start<end' AND end>start'`, status IN held|converted, per-(medic,channel)) → insert `held` or `Optional.empty()`; `convert` (held→converted, expiry-guarded); `expireStaleHolds` (the future SlotHold-expiry job's body). **Overlap is app-level inside the lock, NOT a `btree_gist` EXCLUDE** — no PG extensions are registered (project convention, V7 note) and the advisory lock already serializes the critical section. Lock is **per-medic** (covers both channels); overlap predicate is **per-(medic,channel)** so a field hold + a teleconsult hold at the same wall-clock both succeed. **Contention-test discipline (plan-review C2):** `pg_advisory_xact_lock` **blocks** (unlike [31]'s non-blocking `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`), so the 054 latch shape would **deadlock** if copied — B runs on its own thread and blocking is proven by a bounded `bDone.await(2s)`=false while A holds the lock, then proceeds after A commits. **Invariants:** only **INV-031** flips to COVERED (`InvariantINV031SlotHoldOverlapIntegrationTest`, `@Tag`, made `public` for the cross-package registry ref — the [29]/task-052 pattern); **INV-014 & INV-015 stay DEFERRED** — their consumers (real Stripe webhook handlers / the DispatchOffer fan-out's offer-voiding half) are absent (slices 3/4), so the *primitives* ship but the invariants aren't yet provable end-to-end (consumer-existence test applied uniformly, plan-review I3). Registry reasons updated to name primitive-built / consumer-missing; `EXPECTED_COVERED` 8→9. **Tests:** +13 (346→**359**, 0 skipped) — dedupe sequential-replay + concurrent backstop (UNIQUE-violation rollback observed); reservation win/lose/offline (sequential, per I5 — SQL guarantees atomicity, two-thread harness would only re-test PG + risk the C2 deadlock); SlotHold contention (blocked-then-overlap-rejected) + boundary (touching/partial/nested/identical/cross-channel/expired). Committing tests carry `@AfterEach TRUNCATE slot_holds`/`stripe_events` (non-unique-key-queried → bleed, the `PerfDataSeeder` trap). Convention in `backend/CLAUDE.md`. **Deferred/Parked:** generic client request-idempotency store (no endpoint yet → Slice 3, keyed off `PaymentRecord.idempotencyKey`); the SlotHold-expiry job *handler* ([31] engine seam exists); advisory-lock-key namespacing (only user today). See [28][30][31][29][6].

[33] **OpenAPI Contract** (Task 057, Slice 0b task 8 part 2) - The machine-readable HTTP contract (exec-strategy §7.8 "source of truth for the AI-driven build"). **Approach decision (user-chosen): generate-from-code, NOT spec-first** — springdoc introspects the live controllers/DTOs so the spec can never silently lie about the code; the prescriptive "what to build next" already lives in `slice0a/` + the data model (§3), so the OpenAPI's job is to publish the *current* surface for the frontend monorepo + later slices. **Dep:** `springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-api` **3.0.3** (the **3.0.x line is Boot-4/Jackson-3**; 2.8.x is Boot-3 — verified via Maven Central metadata, not assumed). Spiked first (the one real risk): springdoc boots clean on Boot 4.0.6 and renders `components.schemas` fully under Jackson 3 (the feared swagger-core-Jackson-2 empty-schema failure mode did NOT occur). **Artifact:** committed **`backend/openapi.yaml`** (16 operations across 12 paths == every `@*Mapping` in the 6 controllers). **Drift guard:** `OpenApiContractTest` (build-stopping) regenerates the spec and compares **parsed-structure equality via snakeyaml — NOT a byte-diff** (formatting/key-order changes don't false-RED; same discipline as INV-022 fidelity). Regen ritual: `run-backend-tests -Dtest=OpenApiContractTest -Dopenapi.regenerate=true test` then commit. **The 2 contracts springdoc can't infer live in `common/openapi/OpenApiConfig`:** (1) global `bearerAuth` JWT scheme + a customizer that strips it from the permitAll `/api/auth/{register,login,refresh}` ops (`PUBLIC_PATHS` must stay in sync with `SecurityConfig`); (2) `ErrorResponse` — controllers only *throw* (handler builds it), so it's invisible to springdoc → a customizer registers the schema (`ModelConverters`) and attaches a `default` (any-error) response to all 16 ops. `PagedResponse<T>` needed NO hint (the concrete return type reifies the generic → springdoc emits `PagedResponseAddressResponse`). **Gotcha:** `/v3/api-docs.yaml` carries no charset → MockMvc `getContentAsString()` defaults to ISO-8859-1 and mojibakes non-ASCII; the test decodes `getContentAsByteArray()` as UTF-8 (and config literals stay ASCII). `/v3/api-docs/**` permitAll in `SecurityConfig` (shape-only, no data). **`backend/API.md` demoted** to a pointer (the old prose catalog was badly stale — wrong shapes, missing half the controllers); exact fields live only in the spec now. `springdoc.writer-with-order-by-keys=true` for deterministic output. Tests 360→**361**. Convention in `backend/CLAUDE.md`. **No swagger-UI** (the `-api` starter, deliverable is the YAML, not a hosted console); no future-slice endpoints (generate-from-code documents what exists). See [6][32][28].

[34] **Account.isTest — test-lineage root** (Task 060, follow-up to 059) - The data-model **decision-8** "platform-wide test lineage" root, surfaced by 059 impl-review F1 (it was deferred in BOTH [25] and [28] with no slice owning it). **Scope B (user-chosen): root + first derivation, defer enforcement** — same "primitive before consumers" discipline as [31]/[32]. **V26** `accounts.is_test BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false` (add-with-default, NOT bare NOT NULL — so the column-omitting `PerfDataSeeder` raw INSERT keeps working; the V12 rename trap does NOT bite a defaulted column). **Lombok gotcha:** primitive `boolean isTest` → getter `isTest()`, setter **`setTest(...)`** (the `is`-prefix strips to property "Test") — the existing `Interaction/Order/PaymentRecord.is_test` flags were fully **dormant** (never read/written anywhere) until now. **Derivation** in `InteractionService.create`: `isTest = owner.account.isTest() || (medic != null && medic.account.isTest())`, set on Order (before save) + Interaction (before `runner.initialize`) — activates the dormant flags. **Create-time only** (F5): medic is normally null at create, so assignment-time re-derivation (a real owner matched to a test medic) is the **matching slice's job (S4)** — parked, not built here. **Marking (user-chosen: full REST endpoint):** `POST /api/admin/accounts/{id}/test`+`/untest` → `AccountAdminService.setTestFlag` mirroring `suspend` — `policy.require(`**`SUSPEND_REACTIVATE_ACCOUNTS`**`)` (capability-first, before lookup, no existence leak) + `audit.recordAllowed("mark_test"/"unmark_test")`; `AccountAdminResponse` gained an `isTest` field. **Capability reused, not invented** (roles-permissions-v1.4 has zero "test" mentions → a new Capability would drift the matrix); `SUSPEND_REACTIVATE_ACCOUNTS`={platform_ops,superadmin} fits (account-admin mutation) — NOT `CONFIGURE_PLATFORM_PARAMS` (finance-held, plan-review F2). **No profile gate** (plan-review F1): test lineage is a *production* capability per decision 8 ("safe E2E testing on the live environment, permanent"); the capability is the control. **INV-032 does NOT flip** — root+derivation built but the *enforcement* halves (matching never-mixes S4, analytics/aggregate exclusion via the pipeline) are absent; reason+targetSlice refined (registry + COVERAGE.md), `EXPECTED_COVERED` stays 9. **Capability blast-radius caveat (impl-review F2, for the S3 wiring to revisit):** mark-test reuses `SUSPEND_REACTIVATE_ACCOUNTS` (account-admin, no matrix drift), but once Stripe-test-keys + analytics-exclusion enforcement land, a platform_ops holder marking an account test affects its *real* billing/reporting — Slice 3 should reconsider whether mark-test needs a finance/superadmin-only capability. **Deferred:** PaymentRecord.isTest derivation + Stripe test keys (S3, no payment path); the 3 enforcement rules (S3/S4/pipeline); INV-032 flip. Tests 361→**369** (+8: 4 derivation, 4 endpoint); OpenAPI regenerated. See [25][28][29][33].

[35] **Frontend Dev Env — Apple Silicon (Task 002, marius, closed 2026-05-29)** - End-to-end native arm64 frontend dev loop on macOS Apple Silicon: backend + container + emulator + Gradle `assembleDebug` + APK install + app running. Zero Rosetta in the build pipeline. **All Dockerfile arm64 changes gated on `TARGETARCH=arm64`** — cristi's Windows amd64 builds are bit-for-bit unchanged. Components on arm64: **NDK** HomuHomu833/android-ndk-custom r27d (`linux-aarch64-musl`, pinned URL), extracted into `/opt/android-sdk/ndk/27.1.12297006/` (renamed from the tarball dir — AGP looks up NDK by directory name, not source.properties version); **aapt2** HomuHomu833/android-sdk-custom 36.0.2 (`linux-aarch64-musl`) binary copied to `/usr/local/bin/aapt2` (Debian's aapt2 v2.19 too old for android-36 resource tables); **cmake/ninja/adb** from Ubuntu noble apt, symlinked over sdkmanager's x86_64 binaries. **AGP aapt2 override**: `frontend/android/gradle.properties` carries `android.aapt2FromMavenOverride=/usr/local/bin/aapt2` (forces AGP off the x86_64 Maven aapt2; `frontend/android/` is gitignored so cristi never sees it). **Two cross-cutting gotchas**: (1) the `rn_android_cxx` named volume blocks the first `expo prebuild` (rmdir EBUSY on the live mountpoint) → `frontend/docker-compose.first-build.yml` override, first-time-only (DOCKER.md §First-time setup §5); (2) Docker Desktop's grpcfuse layer stamps `com.docker.grpcfuse.ownership={mode:200}` xattr on host files written from inside the container → container sees mode 0200 regardless of macOS's 0644 → aapt2 icon outputs unreadable on later Gradle runs (`:app:packageDebugResources` AccessDeniedException); fix `xattr -d com.docker.grpcfuse.ownership <file>`. **Supply-chain**: two HomuHomu833 repos (one-dev community project, active Mar 2026); fallback to full Rosetta (`platform: linux/amd64`) is ~30 min if it stalls (Task 002 §Parked). Extends [8] Frontend Build Environment (the Windows-centric reference doc) for macOS. See [36][37].

[36] **Frontend Dev Runner — `frontend/scripts/dev-android.sh` (Task 003, marius, closed 2026-05-30)** - One host-run command that drives the steady-state RN dev loop and streams Metro logs into the terminal: container up → auto-detect device → reverse tunnel(s) → build+install (only if missing or `--build`) → start/reuse Metro → `am start` → `tail -f /tmp/metro.log`. Host-side ORCHESTRATOR only — every tool call is `docker compose exec` into `rn-dev`, honoring [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]] (Metro/Gradle stay in-container). Builds on [35] Apple-Silicon dev env (assumes that arm64 toolchain + a populated `android/`). Launch activity `com.airingvet.dev/.MainActivity`; `--restart-metro` + `--build` flags. **Core reason it exists — `expo run:android`/`pnpm android` are UNUSABLE in Mac+Docker+emulator**: Expo's device resolution runs `adb -s emulator-5554 emu avd name`, an emulator *console* command (port 5554). Normal adb (install/shell/logcat, 5555) proxies fine through the host adb server (`ADB_SERVER_SOCKET`), but the emulator binds console+adb ports to the host's `127.0.0.1` only, so the console hop is unreachable from the container → `could not connect to TCP port 5554` before any build. No upstream fix (expo/expo#11030 unresolved; emulator localhost-only console is by-design, issuetracker 36944754). Blessed path = the split build (`gradlew assembleDebug` → `adb install` → `am start` → Metro), which the script automates. adb client/server version skew (container 34.x vs host 37.x) is harmless (container is a pure client of the host server). **Backend URL trap**: the app's JS runtime on the emulator must use `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://10.0.2.2:8080` (10.0.2.2 = emulator's host alias), NOT the compose default `host.docker.internal:8080` (resolves only inside the container, not in-app). Because `EXPO_PUBLIC_*` is baked into the bundle at Metro start, the script always starts Metro with 10.0.2.2 and, when reusing a running Metro, reads the **node** expo process's effective URL (the bash launcher's own env is the misleading container default) and restarts Metro if it differs. Verified end-to-end: emulator `nc 10.0.2.2 8080` GET `/actuator/health` → `HTTP/1.1 200`. Documented in `DOCKER.md` (Daily commands + Troubleshooting). Connected to [35] Apple-Silicon dev env, [8] Frontend Build Environment, [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]].

[37] **iOS Build Env + `frontend/scripts/dev-ios.sh` (Task 004, marius, closed 2026-06-02)** - First iOS build path — Simulator working end-to-end, physical-device groundwork documented. **iOS is the documented exception to [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]]**: Xcode/CocoaPods are macOS-only and CocoaPods resolves RN/Expo package paths via a **host-side** `node_modules`, so the native build (`expo run:ios` → `xcodebuild` + pods) and the Simulator run on the host while **Metro stays in the `rn-dev` container**. The Simulator shares the host network → backend at `http://localhost:8080`, Metro at `localhost:8081` (container-published). iOS analog of [36]'s `10.0.2.2` Android trap, but simpler (`localhost`). Unlike `expo run:android` ([36], unusable in Mac+Docker), **`expo run:ios` works** because Xcode + Simulator are both host-side — pass `--no-bundler` so it never starts a 2nd Metro on `:8081`. **Host toolchain (verified on marius's Mac)**: Xcode 26.4.1, CocoaPods 1.16.2 under **Homebrew Ruby 3.4.1** (system Ruby 2.6.10 NOT used, so the predicted EOL-Ruby pod-install failure never applied); all pods compiled clean under Xcode 26 / iOS 26 SDK incl. reanimated 4.1.1 (requires new-arch → `newArchEnabled` can't be disabled as a fallback), react-native-maps 1.20.1, Sentry, SDWebImage — no deployment-target/EXCLUDED_ARCHS workaround. **Setup traps handled by `dev-ios.sh`**: (1) **Node ≥ 22 required** — pnpm 11 uses `node:sqlite`; host default Node 20 crashes it (the real cause behind the "corepack ERR_VM_DYNAMIC_IMPORT" symptom) → `nvm install 24`, `frontend/.nvmrc` pins 24, then `corepack disable` + `npm i -g pnpm@11`; (2) **Sentry build phase** errors `An organization ID or slug is required` when no Sentry org is set → `SENTRY_DISABLE_AUTO_UPLOAD=true`; (3) **ATS blocks cleartext localhost** → `ios.infoPlist.NSAppTransportSecurity.NSAllowsLocalNetworking=true` (+ `NSExceptionDomains.localhost`) in `app.config.ts` BEFORE prebuild (App-Store-safe; `ios/` is gitignored, set ATS in config not the generated plist); (4) **`.env` is authoritative for `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` (THE login-network-error bug, fixed 2026-06-02)** — Expo SDK 54 compiles `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_*` into a virtual module built from the **`.env` FILE**, which overrides BOTH the shell var passed to `expo start` AND `.env.local` (verified empirically); the shared `.env` hardcoding Android's `10.0.2.2:8080` → an iOS Simulator can't resolve it → silent RN "Network Error" (request never leaves the app). Fix: shared `ensure_env_url()` in `_metro.sh` rewrites the `.env` line to the platform URL (ios→localhost:8080, android→10.0.2.2:8080) before each Metro start, paired with `expo start --clear` (inlining is transform-cached); switching platforms self-heals. Verified end-to-end: iOS Simulator login → `POST /api/auth/login` → `200 OK`. **Dual `node_modules`**: the container resolves JS from the `rn_node_modules` named volume (host-invisible); host `frontend/node_modules` is a SEPARATE tree for pods/Xcode — any dep change must run in BOTH (`pnpm install` on host AND in the container), then re-`expo prebuild --platform ios`. **`dev-ios.sh`** mirrors [36]'s structure/`metro_api_url` baking-trap restart logic: Node 24 → container up → boot+show Simulator (exact name/UDID match, prefer Booted — substring `iPhone 17` would also hit `iPhone 17 Pro`) → `ensure_env_url` writes `.env`=localhost:8080 + Metro `--clear` → build+install only if missing (`xcrun simctl get_app_container`) or `--build` → relaunch → tail `/tmp/metro.log`; flags `--build`/`--restart-metro`/`--device`/`-h`; scheme/workspace **AIRingVetdev**, bundle `com.airingvet.dev`. Verified: cold build (Build Succeeded, `iOS Bundled 2900 modules`, app live on iPhone 17) + warm re-run (reuse Metro, relaunch, no build). **Shared helpers (`dc`/`metro_up`/`metro_api_url`/`wait_for_metro`/`stop_metro`/`ensure_env_url`) live in `_metro.sh`, sourced by BOTH runners** ([36] refactored to source it). **`stop_metro` pkill gotcha (fixed)**: must use `pkill -f 'exp[o] start'`, NOT `'expo start'` — `pkill -f` matches the full cmdline incl. the parent `bash -lc "pkill …"` shell's own argv, so a literal pattern kills its own shell → `docker compose exec` non-zero → `set -e` aborts (false "Compose error"); the `[o]` class avoids the self-match. Only the URL-mismatch restart path reaches `stop_metro`. Connected to [35] Apple-Silicon dev env, [36] dev-android.sh, [8] Frontend Build Environment, [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]].

[38] **Concurrent iOS+Android Dev Loop + Fast Refresh Truth (Task 005, marius, closed 2026-06-04)** - Run the iOS Simulator and Android emulator **at the same time off ONE Metro**, Fast Refresh on both. **THE key correction**: Fast Refresh was believed broken in the Docker dev loop — it is NOT, on macOS. Proven on-screen (`xcrun simctl io screenshot` + `adb exec-out screencap`): edit one `.tsx` → both apps update in seconds, no reload. The Task-004 "FR broken" finding was a **false negative** — Metro pushes HMR over the websocket and prints NOTHING to its log, so all "metro.log didn't grow" checks misled; the user's "neither reloaded" was the stale-`10.0.2.2`-bundle/network-error episode (disconnected HMR client). **Mac needs NO watcher fix** (`fs.watch`, even recursive/nested, sees host edits over the Docker Desktop bind mount on macOS; `CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING`/`WATCHPACK_POLLING` are INERT for Metro — they only affect chokidar/webpack; comments corrected in `docker-compose.yml` + `DOCKER.md`). **Concurrency mechanism**: one Metro bakes ONE `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`; `localhost` is iOS-only and `10.0.2.2` is Android-only, so they can't be shared — BUT both the sim and the emulator reach the **Mac LAN IP** (verified: emulator `nc <lan-ip>:8080` → 200). So bake `http://<lan-ip>:8080`: one Metro serves both, both reach the backend, FR pushes to both. New `detect_lan_ip()` in `_metro.sh` (`ipconfig getifaddr en0/en1`); `dev-ios.sh` + `dev-android.sh` both resolve `http://<lan-ip>:8080` (graceful fallback to localhost/10.0.2.2 if no LAN IP → single-platform). Because both bake the SAME URL, the 2nd runner **reuses** the 1st's Metro (no restart) → they coexist. **LAN IP is DHCP-dependent** (changed .139→.106 mid-task) — runners re-detect per run. Verified end-to-end: both screenshots showed the edited label. **DROPPED/parked**: Windows FR support (cristi owns it; his `dev:reset` is a manual nuke-and-reload, NOT auto-FR, and his "watcher dead on Windows" conclusion is UNVERIFIED with on-screen testing → deserves the same skepticism that overturned the Mac claim) and the `docker compose watch` volume-model rework (only needed if Windows FR is confirmed broken). Connected to [37] iOS build env, [36] dev-android.sh, [8] Frontend Build Environment, [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]].

[39] **Metro Reuse-vs-Restart Hardening (Task 006, marius, closed 2026-06-18)** - Closes Task 005 review-finding #5 (no guard on the byte-identical-URL concurrency contract), prompted by a lived failure that same session: running both runners gave Fast Refresh on Android but not iOS. Root cause — the 2nd runner **silently `--clear`-restarted a healthy Metro** instead of reusing it, orphaning the already-attached app's HMR websocket (Metro logs NOTHING on a dead client, so it looked like "FR broken" and took live process/log forensics to diagnose). The contract from [38] ("both bake the same LAN-IP URL → 2nd reuses the 1st's Metro") breaks whenever the URLs diverge — typically when one runner started before the backend was reachable on the LAN IP and `resolve_api_base_url` fell back to a per-platform URL (localhost / 10.0.2.2), or the LAN IP changed (DHCP). The OLD code *warned* on divergence but **restarted anyway**. **THE FIX — policy: never restart a HEALTHY Metro on a mere URL mismatch.** New shared `decide_metro_action()` in `_metro.sh` (sourced by both runners — no drift) classifies into a single stdout token (`reuse`|`restart`|`fresh`) and prints the loud ✓/⚠ explanation to **stderr** (same stdout=token / stderr=human split as `resolve_api_base_url`). Default on a divergent-but-healthy Metro is **`reuse`** (⚠ naming running-vs-ours URLs + the `--restart-metro` escape hatch); empty-proc-probe also → `reuse` (a `metro_up` Metro is healthy; replaces the old iOS empty→restart-to-be-safe, removing a spurious-restart trigger); `restart` happens ONLY on explicit `--restart-metro`; `fresh` when no Metro is up. Each runner `case`s on the token: iOS reuse→no-op / restart→`stop_metro;start_metro_bg` / fresh→`start_metro_bg`; android reuse→`launch_app`+`exec tail` / restart→`stop_metro;run_metro_interactive` / fresh→`run_metro_interactive`. `stop_metro`'s `exp[o] start` self-match-avoidance pattern ([37]) untouched. **Verification**: `bash -n` + `shellcheck -S warning` CLEAN on all three; 6/6 stubbed dry-run assertions (incl. `url-diverge→reuse`); live PASS against the real running Metro (LAN IP, node PID 222) — forced-divergent `API_BASE_URL` → token `reuse`, PID unchanged, user's live iOS+Android session untouched. Connected to [38] Concurrent dev loop, [37] dev-ios.sh, [36] dev-android.sh, [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]].

[40] **Metro Reuse — Logging + Warning Robustness Fixes (Task 007, marius, closed 2026-06-18)** - Fixes three review findings on [39] (surfaced by two headless claude judges — the panel couldn't run multi-model: codex not installed, agy 429-quota-exhausted; `.agent/models.json` `default_judge=claude` so bare plan/impl-review stop demanding codex). **#1 (logging, the user's original "connected to metro for logging" gap)**: `dev-android.sh:run_metro_interactive` ran `pnpm expo start` in the foreground with NO `/tmp/metro.log` redirect, while `dev-ios.sh` backgrounds Metro *with* it — yet BOTH reuse arms `tail -f /tmp/metro.log`, so android-first → an iOS reuse tailed a stale/missing file. Fix: wrap the interactive Metro in **`script -q -e -f -c 'pnpm expo start …' /tmp/metro.log`** (util-linux `script` allocates a PTY → `process.stdout.isTTY` stays true so expo's `r/j/m` keys keep working; a plain `| tee` would make stdout non-TTY and drop expo to non-interactive). **`-f` is mandatory** — without it `script` buffers and the reuse-tailer sees nothing until Metro exits. **#2 (silent-failure guard)**: both runners now capture `metro_action="$(decide_metro_action)"` once and the `case` has a `*)` arm (loud msg + `exit 1`) — an unexpected/empty token no longer silently no-ops. **N3 (divergence-warning robustness)**: `_metro.sh:metro_api_url` selected the expo node by cmdline shape `*node*expo*start*` (brittle); replaced with **exclude-shells** selection — skip PIDs whose `/proc/PID/comm` is `bash|sh|dash|""` (the launcher carrying the misleading container-default `host.docker.internal` — the [36] trap), take the first survivor's `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`. **KEY GOTCHA (caught live)**: could NOT allowlist `comm=="node"` — Node reports `comm=MainThread` for its main thread here, so a node-allowlist returned empty and would have silently defeated the very warning N3 protects; excluding shells is the safe rule. Verified live: `metro_api_url → http://192.168.100.106:8080` (LAN IP, launcher's host.docker.internal not selected). All three: `bash -n` + `shellcheck -S warning` CLEAN. **Cold-relaunch (4th fix, surfaced by the live e2e)**: the e2e proved #1/N3 end-to-end (interactive `script`-wrapped Metro writes `/tmp/metro.log`, iOS `tail -F` streamed the session, keys preserved, `✓` reuse contract) — BUT also exposed that after a Metro restart BOTH apps lost Fast Refresh, because the runners relaunch WARM: `am start` / `simctl launch` just foreground an already-running app, still bound to the OLD/dead Metro. Manually cold-restarting both apps reconnected them (user-confirmed FR restored). Fix baked in: `dev-android.sh:launch_app` force-stops before `am start`; `dev-ios.sh` relaunch path terminates before `simctl launch` — every launch now cold-reconnects HMR to the current Metro (the `expo run:ios` build path already cold-launches). **This is the recurring "FR dead after a Metro restart" gotcha resolved at the source.** **PARKED (documented, not done)**: N1 cold-start TOCTOU lock (two simultaneous `fresh` starters race port 8081), N2 atomic/reconciled `.env` rewrite, N4 verify-packager-is-ours-before-reuse, first-pass #3 reachability-wording, #4 token→action mapping test. Connected to [39] reuse-vs-restart hardening, [38] Concurrent dev loop, [36] dev-android.sh, [[feedback-frontend-docker-only]].

[41] **iOS Push Enablement + Cross-Platform Build Durability** (Task 010, marius, 2026-06-19) - Post-merge the from-scratch iOS build was broken and Android push regressed; fixed both so iOS Simulator + Android emulator build/run/**push** end-to-end. Cause: merge `e802012` pulled the **Android-only** push rail [23] ("iOS later") into the iOS-capable branch. **iOS build:** `@react-native-firebase/app` plugin hard-requires `ios.googleServicesFile` (app.json) + `expo-build-properties` `ios.useFrameworks:"static"`+`forceStaticLinking:["RNFBApp","RNFBMessaging"]` (RNFirebase v24 + Expo 54 recipe); cold `pod install` needs `--repo-update` (Firebase 12.10.0 absent from a stale spec cache). **iOS push app-side:** the dropped `expo-notifications` had been adding the push capability — re-add in `app.config.ts`: `ios.entitlements.aps-environment` (profile-aware: dev/staging→`development`/sandbox, prod→`production`) + `ios.infoPlist.UIBackgroundModes:["remote-notification"]`; and `getFcmToken` must call `messaging().registerDeviceForRemoteMessages()` before `getToken()` on iOS (the RNFirebase **messaging** config plugin is **Android-only** — its build dir has only `android/`). **iOS push — the real blocker:** the Xcode **Signing & Capabilities Development Team MUST be set**; an unsigned/no-team app gets an invalid APNs entitlement → an APNs token FCM can't route → backend `FcmClient` logs `INVALID_ARGUMENT` and iOS receives nothing (Android unaffected, which is why it looked iOS-specific). Persisted via `app.config.ts` `ios.appleTeamId` (`H6PV4KW8J5`) so `expo prebuild` bakes `DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` into the gitignored Xcode project (else re-pick after every prebuild). **The iOS Simulator DOES receive FCM push once properly code-signed** (Apple-Silicon, Xcode 14+, APNs sandbox) — the "simulator is unreliable with FCM" lore is wrong here. **Backend:** `app.firebase.enabled` defaults **TRUE** → the SB backend won't start without the gitignored admin-SDK key (`backend/ringvet-dev-firebase-adminsdk-*.json`); a stale May-26 image (predating the push endpoint) 404'd `/api/users/me/push-tokens` until `docker compose up -d --build backend` (then `--force-recreate` to clear a file-vs-dir mount left when the key was absent). **Android build:** needs `frontend/google-services.json` (gitignored, pkg `com.airingvet.dev`) **and** `expo prebuild --platform android` to apply the `com.google.gms.google-services` gradle plugin — the merge added the dep but never re-prebuilt, so the plugin stayed unapplied → native FirebaseApp init fails → `index.js` top-level `messaging()` throws pre-mount → **app stuck on splash**. **Android arm64 NDK fix (durable):** node [35]'s rename trick (r27d NDK in a dir named `27.1.12297006`) fails reanimated's CMake **`CXX5304`** check (dir-name vs internal `Pkg.Revision=27.3.13750724`) on a cold native build (a warm `.cxx` cache had hidden it); the Dockerfile now rewrites the NDK `source.properties` `Pkg.Revision`/`BaseRevision` to match the dir, **arm64-gated** (amd64/Windows uses Google's real 27.1 NDK — untouched). Surfaced as a misleading "Gradle daemon disappeared"; **memory was a red herring** (3.5 GiB free) — no worker cap added. **From-scratch-after-merge checklist:** run host `pnpm install` (iOS pods resolve the *host* `node_modules`, [37]); rebuild the backend image; ensure all three gitignored Firebase files are present (`GoogleService-Info.plist`, `google-services.json`, admin-SDK key). See [23][35][37][6].

[42] **Pet API — first AloVet product HTTP surface** (Task 066, Slice 1 backend, cristi, 2026-07-01) - Owner-scoped Pet CRUD over `/api/pets` — the **first AloVet endpoint beyond the pre-AloVet auth/account/address/push controllers**; unblocks the frontend Slice 1 pet-profile screens (contract-first, backend-leads per CLAUDE.md Collaboration Model). A **thin HTTP layer over the schema-only `pet/` domain** [28]: `PetController` (GET paged / GET{id} / POST 201 / PATCH / DELETE 204) mirrors `AddressController`; `PetService` gained Account-scoped `list/get/create/update/softDelete` resolving the caller's `Owner` via `OwnerRepository.findByAccountId` (cross-owner → **404 not 403**, no existence leak; self-scoped owner route, **NOT** `PolicyService`-gated). DTOs are records in `pet/dto/`: `PetResponse` (never exposes `owner` — the B4-F1 leak class; surfaces `patientCategoryCode`+`needsReview`, where a null category = species unmapped/needs_review, decision 3), `PetCreateRequest` (@NotBlank name/species), `PetUpdateRequest` (**partial: null = leave unchanged**; clearing an optional field unsupported for now). **Soft-delete** → `PetStatus.deleted` (retained so historical interactions never dangle); all reads exclude `deleted` via `findByOwnerAndStatusNot`. **Plan-review caught 5 blueprint-divergence traps** (the Address blueprint hides them — Address hard-deletes + touches no lazy relation): F1 `PetStatus` has no `inactive` (it's `active|deceased|deleted`); F2 soft-deleted rows must be excluded from list/get; **F3 N+1** — `Pet.patientCategory` is `@ManyToOne(LAZY)` read by `PetResponse.from`, fixed with `@EntityGraph("patientCategory")` on the paged finder + a Tier-1 `QueryCount` guard (baseline **4**, row-independent); F4 PATCH null-ambiguity; F5 `@Size` caps on the unbounded `text`/JSONB fields (Task-029 discipline). Contract regenerated → `backend/openapi.yaml` (+`/api/pets` paths + Pet schemas, purely additive); suite **370→389**, 0 skipped. Tests: `PetResponseTest`, `PetServiceIntegrationTest`, `PetControllerTest`, `PetOwnershipIntegrationTest` (attacker entry-point: cross-owner 404, unauth 401 — this surfaced+fixed a `SecurityContextHolder` thread-local leak in a unit test's missing `@AfterEach`), `PetQueryCountGuardTest`. No `InvariantRegistry` change (matching invariants land with Slice 3). See [28][25][6]; blueprint `address/` [11][19].

[43] **GDPR Consent Capture API** (Task 067, Slice 1 backend, cristi, 2026-07-01) - Append-only `/api/consents` (record grant / list current / withdraw) — the 2nd Slice-1 owner-side surface after [42], completing onboarding's GDPR consent capture (backend-leads, contract-first). Per-**Account** (consent is per-person, not a role) — `@AuthenticationPrincipal Account`, no `PolicyService`. **V29 `consents`** `(account_id, type, version, granted_at, withdrawn_at?)` — **bare FK, no `ON DELETE` (mirrors V16 audit_log)** because consent is a `retain`-forever legal record that must outlive the account (gdpr-erase-behavior :60); append-only — a withdrawal stamps `withdrawn_at`, never deletes. `ConsentType` enum {TERMS_OF_SERVICE, PRIVACY_POLICY, MARKETING} — minimal+extensible; API is **policy-light** (records grants; *which* are mandatory is onboarding UX). **Semantics (plan-review-shaped):** grant is **idempotent** when the active row already matches `{type,version}` (an identical re-tap must NOT fabricate a withdraw+regrant in a legal record — F1), else **supersede-then-append** (`saveAndFlush` the old active's `withdrawnAt` before inserting the new, so the index sees it); a **partial unique index `(account_id,type) WHERE withdrawn_at IS NULL`** enforces ≤1 active per type at the DB (concurrency backstop — F5); `GET` = current-only (active per type) as `PagedResponse` (F3). **Cross-cutting fix (F4):** `GlobalExceptionHandler` now maps `HttpMessageNotReadableException` + `MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException` → **400 `VALIDATION_FAILED`** (was 500 via the catch-all) — a bad enum in a path/body is a client error; benefits every enum-bearing endpoint. `Account.acceptedTermsAt` stays as the separate register-time terms stamp (not replaced). Deferred: erase/export **jobs** + `Account.erasedAt` (Slice 8, INV-033); a `?history` param for the full ledger. Contract regenerated (+`/api/consents`); suite **389→407**, 0 skipped. Tests: `ConsentResponseTest`, `ConsentServiceIntegrationTest`, `ConsentControllerTest`, `ConsentIntegrationTest`. See [42][25][6]; spec `docs/specs/2026-06-19-gdpr-erase-behavior.md`.

[44] **Backend/Frontend Split Plan — the two-lane relay** (Task 068, cristi, 2026-07-02) - `docs/specs/2026-07-02-backend-frontend-split-plan.md` (+ committed swimlane `...split-plan.html`) is the **sequence source** for the two-dev split [3]: lane assignment of every pilot-plan v3.3 bullet (BE/FE/PAIRED/OPS), current position, and each dev's next units — the mind map stays the *status* source. **Backend order: B1 ✅ done (Task 069, 2026-07-02)** — `/api/addresses` extended additively (label/lat/lng/sector/floor/intercom/parkingNotes + ops-set `flag` (exposed) with `flagReason` (ops-only, never on the owner wire) + soft-delete `deletedAt` + new `GET /{id}`; V30; PUT never touches ops fields; geo both-or-neither error = fieldErrors field `geoComplete`; suite 407→421; split-plan D6 records the street-vs-streetAddress divergence). Two infra fixes rode along: `run-backend-tests` now builds **off-mount** (a 2026-07-02 Docker Desktop update made WSL2 bind mounts report all files root:root → setLastModified EPERM as uid 1000 — Maven resource copy dies; source copied to /tmp/app in-container, openapi.yaml + surefire-reports copied back) and `application-test.yml` raises the shared-context pre-auth IP bucket to 100000 (the whole suite shares one "IP"; dedicated rate-limit tests pin capacity=3 via @TestPropertySource and are unaffected) → **B2 deferred, reordered after B4 (Task 070, audit-only, 2026-07-02, no code):** design-phase investigation + freehand discussion with the user found the split-plan's B2-before-B4 dependency arrow was wrong — B3 (the 0b demo) is *"a scripted end-to-end dry run through the state-machine runner"* (exec-strategy §7) needing only a couple of fixture rows, not a Clinic/directory/vet-onboarding API surface; B4 doesn't need B2 either. A partial build (just a demo-env owner/pet generator) was also rejected: it relieves no current blocker (Marius can hand-create test pets against live `/api/pets`) and v3.3 principle 7 defines the demo env as "fictional vets across sectors, ~30 pets" — vets first, so seeding pets alone doesn't serve that purpose at smaller scale, and building the job's idempotency/refresh shape now would guess ahead of B4's not-yet-built Clinic/Medic schema. **New backend order: B4 → B2 → B3.** **B4 foundation ✅ done (Task 071, 2026-07-02)** — see [45]; **B4 chunk 2 ✅ ScheduleBlock CRUD done (Task 072, 2026-07-03, [52])** — working-hours (recurring weekday+time) + block-out (explicit) write API, V34. **B2 ✅ done (Task 077, 2026-07-05, [56])** — Clinic entity + audited CLI (clinic-upsert/medic-onboard/medic-affiliate/demo-env-refresh), no HTTP surface per this row's own "Scripts, no HTTP surface" note; reopened once B4 fully shipped (Task 076, [55]). **B3 ✅ done (Task 078, 2026-07-05, [57])** — the Slice-0b scripted demo, built as a narrated integration test (no UI/HTTP surface exists yet for a literal two-phone demo) exercising the state-machine runner + job engine + concurrency primitive end to end. **Slice 0b is now fully closed — the entire backend B-lane (B1/B2/B3/B4) is done; only `003-deploy-to-google-cloud-cicd-testing` remains open in the tracker.** **Frontend order: F1 ✅ done (Task 014 [48]) → F2 ✅ done (Task 015 [49]) → F3 ✅ done (Task 016 [50], 2026-07-04)** — owner-side S1 screens (consent → pets → address home-visit) all shipped; **Slice-1 owner demo dry-run ✅ DONE (Task 017 [51], 2026-07-05)** — full v3.3:38 script driven on-device (3:22, 2 UX fixes landed), closing Slice 1's owner side; next **F4** monorepo restructure → **F5** medic screens (after B4). **Operative defaults (override by saying so): D1** pet photos = device-local URI until Slice-5 media ingest (no S1 upload endpoint); **D2** Marius builds Slice-1 screens first, restructures after the S1 demo (S2 backend 0% — restructuring now would idle him; screens double as restructure regression suite); **D3** email rail → first consumer (S3/S5; push-only today, no JavaMailSender anywhere); **D4** analytics pipeline → first emitter (S3/4 funnel, taxonomy pre-agreed); **D5** GDPR delete/export jobs → Slice 8 (059 decision). **Corrected a stale [4] claim:** Slice 0b was NOT "done minus demo" — seed tooling (v3.3:35) and the email rail (:32) were unaccounted; 0b remainder = B2+B3 with 0b.3-0b.5 explicit deferrals (B2/B3 now sequenced after B4 per the Task-070 reorder above). PAIRED (unsplittable, synchronous) phases: 3c video SDK, S4 dispatch transport (CallKit/FCM), S8 observability+buffer. Gates: O8→3b only; O9→live consults + S5-7 content; legal O4/O5→S7. See [3][4][42][43][6][7][45].

[45] **Medic APIs — B4 foundation** (Task 071, Slice 2 backend, cristi, 2026-07-02) - The first Slice-2 HTTP surface (`/api/medic/*`) — scoped down from the full B4 (5 separable chunks of very different size/risk, surfaced this task) to just schema + self-scoped reads, mirroring the Pet/Address blueprint [42][43]. **Schema (V32/V33):** `Service` (content table, seeded `teleconsult_instant`/`teleconsult_scheduled`, data-model :83) + medic-owned `MedicService`/`ServiceArea`/`ScheduleBlock`/`MedicCategory` (data-model :67,:85-87) — new `medic/` package (package-by-feature). **`PricingConfig` deliberately dropped from scope** (panel-review finding, two judges independently converging): no consumer until Slice 3's price-resolution flow exists; seeding a real price now would bake an unratified number into a forward-only migration — the exact "no consumer yet" trap [44]'s own B2 audit flagged elsewhere, caught here in this task's own plan. Its NULL-uniqueness gotcha (a plain UNIQUE lets multiple `patient_category_id IS NULL` global-default rows coexist — needs a `V29`-style partial unique index) is recorded for whoever builds it alongside Slice 3. **Read endpoints:** `GET /api/medic/profile` (bio + `instantBookOptIn`/`homeVisitOptIn` + category/service **codes**, not raw i18n names — no locale resolver exists, mirrors `PetResponse.patientCategoryCode`), `GET /api/medic/service-areas`, `GET /api/medic/schedule` (raw rows, **not** computed day-view) — all self-scoped (ownership via `MedicRepository.findByAccountId`, non-medic → 404, **not** `PolicyService` — `MANAGE_OWN_AVAILABILITY_PRESENCE`/`EDIT_OWN_SERVICES_PRICES` capabilities exist in the matrix since 0a but stay unconsumed by read-only routes, same as Pet/Address). **Fetch-strategy fix (panel-review):** the profile composes `Medic`+categories+services — avoided a single `@EntityGraph` over two `@OneToMany` collections (Hibernate's Cartesian-duplicate/multi-bag-fetch trap, `Medic` had zero collections modeled before this task) in favor of split scalar-Medic + dedicated `MedicCategoryRepository`/`MedicServiceRepository` queries, each graphing only its own `@ManyToOne` target; `MedicProfileQueryCountGuardTest` pins the baseline at **4**, unchanged 2→4 categories. **`ScheduleBlock.channel` reuses `booking.SlotChannel`** (not a new enum) — exact columns (`weekday`/`rrule`/`start_at`/`end_at`, all nullable) pinned now since Flyway is forward-only, but the explicit-vs-recurring shape CHECK is deferred to the future CRUD task (no write path yet). `ServiceArea` carries the data-model's explicitly reserved nullable polygon placeholder (JSONB, no PostGIS — no new PG extensions) and NOT-NULL `city_id`/`sector`. Naming gotcha for future work in this package: the entity `Service` collides by simple name with `org.springframework.stereotype.Service` — fully-qualify the Spring annotation (`@org.springframework.stereotype.Service`) in any class needing both, don't rename the entity. **Impl-review (4 fixes):** filtered `teleconsult_instant` out of `medic_services` reads (`Service.INSTANT_CONSULT_CODE` + a dedicated repo query) so a stray per-medic row for the platform-priced instant consult can never reach the wire; `MedicServiceSummary.durationMin` now resolves the **effective** duration (override else `Service.defaultDurationMin`) instead of surfacing `null`; added `schedule_blocks_weekday_range`/`schedule_blocks_interval_order` CHECKs (narrower than the deferred explicit-vs-recurring exclusivity check); replaced a misleadingly-named constraint test that only proved a *valid* value round-trips with 8 raw-SQL tests actually violating each DB guard. Suite **421→440→451** after fixes, 0 skipped. **Panel rerun (task reopened 2026-07-02, 5/5 judges — agy cured by the reapply-script stdin fix [see reapply script fix 5], codex:gpt-5.5 by `--timeout 600`):** 6 more fixes — deleted the unfiltered `findByMedicIdOrderByIdAsc` (3-judge convergence: dead code AND the exact leak vector the mapper re-filter was defending against; the excluding query is now the only read of a medic's services), override-wins duration test (the ternary's override branch was mutation-invisible before), non-medic-404 asserted on all three endpoints (was `/profile` only), `MedicServiceSummary.travelFeeModel` String→`TravelFeeModel` enum (OpenAPI now carries `flat_per_sector|per_km` instead of arbitrary string), `schedule_blocks_interval_pair` CHECK (start/end both-or-neither) + raw-SQL test, `*.stackdump` gitignored. Rejected with schema evidence: "missing medic_id indexes" (the three composite `UNIQUE (medic_id, ...)` have medic_id LEADING → they serve per-medic reads; schedule_blocks alone lacks a UNIQUE, hence its explicit index) and "durationMin should be Integer" (V32 `default_duration_min NOT NULL CHECK (>0)` + primitive `int` entity field make the effective duration total). Suite **457**, 0 skipped. Parked when this foundation shipped (named explicitly, not silently dropped): ScheduleBlock CRUD (**now built — Task 072, [52]**), presence state machine + expiry job (**now built — Task 073, [53]**: medic-facing offline↔online + `@Scheduled` expiry sweep + re-confirm job; reserved/in_consultation edges still deferred to Slice 4), day-view computed availability, Stripe Connect (zero SDK/outbound code exists), `PricingConfig`, `Specialty`/`MedicSpecialty`/`SymptomCategory`/`InteractionSymptom`, owner-facing medic directory read. See [44][42][43][25][28][6][52].

[46] **Owner/Medic Target Split — env-driven app variants** (Task 012, marius) - One Expo codebase → two apps (**AloVet** owner / **AloVet Medic** medic) selected by `EXPO_PUBLIC_APP_ROLE` (default owner) — the idiomatic Expo "app variants" pattern, deliberately NOT native flavors (evaluated against sister project `alodoc`'s bare-RN flavor matrix; env-driven fits our CNG setup, keeps cristi's prebuild path; decision brief shared). `app.config.ts` composes identity per `(role × profile)`: `com.airingvet.<role>[.<profile>]`, scheme `alovet`/`alovet-medic`, display name, and a **per-role `googleServicesFile` set AFTER the `...config` spread** — this **retires the old "googleServicesFile must be in app.json" rule** ([6]/CLAUDE.md): verified via `expo config` that app.config.ts overrides app.json at config resolution. Separate `app/(owner)` + `app/(medic)` route trees, role-gated in root `_layout.tsx` via `<Stack.Protected guard={isAuthenticated && isOwner/isMedic}>` (routing role from the inlined `EXPO_PUBLIC_APP_ROLE` via `@/config/appRole`, NOT `Constants.extra` — no dev-client manifest-refresh ambiguity). **Role has two consumers at two times:** app.config.ts reads `process.env` at **prebuild** (native identity); Metro/JS reads the `.env`-inlined value at **transform** (route tree) — so the launchers write role to `.env` via `ensure_env_role` (a shell `export` is ignored by the EXPO_PUBLIC_* inliner). **Launchers** (`dev-android.sh`/`dev-ios.sh`/`dev-android.ps1`) gain `--role owner|medic`: resolve the id from `expo config` (single source of truth via `scripts/_appid.js`, no parallel table), **auto-`prebuild --clean` on a baked-role mismatch** (Android uses the `docker-compose.first-build.yml` override to dodge the `.cxx` EBUSY; iOS adds `pod install --repo-update`), Metro **clear-restart unless the running role positively matches**, and a launch-time role-reconciliation assert. **Single active role locally**; two-phone demo = **standalone builds**; concurrent live dual-role Metro **deferred** (would need an 8082 compose port + role-isolated `.env`). **Dev-only scope:** 4 dev Firebase registrations under `firebase/<role>/` (gitignored) + committed `firebase/placeholder/` dummies so prebuild/config run before real files land (`firebase/README.md`); `owner-dev`/`medic-dev` EAS profiles with per-role OTA channels; staging/prod deferred. Pure `composeAppIdentity` (jest-tested over the (role×profile) matrix) is the formula spec — app.config.ts **inlines** it because the Expo config loader can't `require` a project `.ts`. **⚠ ps1 + all device paths (reprebuild / role-switch / launch) are UNVERIFIED** — the build ran with no Windows/device; verification = `bash -n` + config-resolution + 189 green tests. Panel-reviewed (5-judge plan + 5-judge impl, findings applied). **Live-verification wave (same task, macOS device runs 2026-07-02)** — the macOS device paths are now verified; what it surfaced: **(a) arm64 aapt2 override survives prebuild** via config plugin `frontend/plugins/withArm64Aapt2.js` (gated on `process.arch==='arm64' && /usr/local/bin/aapt2` exists — cristi's amd64 unaffected; a `prebuild --clean` had silently wiped the manual gradle.properties line → x86-64 Maven aapt2 → rosetta crash); launchers also `xattr -c` the regenerated res tree (grpcfuse ownership-xattr → container AccessDenied). **(b) iOS dev-client launch contract**: an expo-dev-client build gets its Metro URL ONLY through the `<bundleid>://expo-development-client/?url=<metro>` deep link via `simctl openurl` — plain `simctl launch` and `-RCT_jsLocation` both yield the "(null) script URL" redbox; `dev-ios.sh` fires it SERIALIZED (wait for `run:ios`'s own async launch to register → terminate → poll launchd until the process is GONE → openurl) and refuses to launch while Metro's `/status` doesn't answer. **(c) THE recurring-redbox root cause — Metro watched `ios/`+`android/`**: `resolver.blockList` (→ metro-file-map `ignorePattern`) now excludes both project-root native trees; before, a role-switch prebuild+pod-install (~10k files over gRPC-FUSE, no watchman) pegged Metro's main process for minutes (observed 368s vs 2s bundle), storming the attached Android app with "Refreshing…" and nulling the iOS launch probe. **(d) Metro transform cache** now persistent (FileStore on the `~/.expo` volume) + `cacheVersion` keyed on `.env`'s EXPO_PUBLIC_* — launchers dropped blanket `--clear` (kept on `--restart-metro`); role/URL changes self-invalidate (cold ~33s → warm ~2-18s). **(e)** medic app mounts its placeholder WITHOUT auth (`guard={isMedic}` alone; login stays owner-scoped — user decision); Home titles show `appName + roleLabel` ("AloVet Owner"/"AloVet Medic") via `@/config/appRole.roleLabel`; Ionicons.ttf embedded via expo-font plugin (Metro fetch was flaky → missing tab icons). **(f) Offline/release builds** probed and parked → Task 013 — **now COMPLETE, see [47]** (arm64 hermesc source-built in the image; --release verified on both platforms; Sentry-upload solved via `--no-daemon` + `SENTRY_DISABLE_AUTO_UPLOAD=true`; release signs with the debug keystore). **⚠ ps1 paths remain Windows-unverified.** See [7][8][9][35][41][6]. **Emulator backend-URL made WiFi-independent (Task 014 follow-up, 2026-07-03):** `dev-android.sh` no longer prefers the Mac LAN IP for the *emulator* — it always bakes `http://10.0.2.2:8080` (the emulator's built-in host-loopback alias, immune to network changes), matching what the tunnel section + the Windows `.ps1` already did.
