The LAN-IP preference (kept for physical devices) was baking a WiFi-tied IP that went stale on every network switch → "emulator lost connectivity" until relaunch. Cost: emulator+iOS-sim on ONE Metro (Task 005) is now opt-in via an explicit `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://<lan-ip>:8080` (the env override still wins). Physical devices unchanged (need the LAN + same WiFi; no host alias).

[47] **Offline/Release Local Builds — arm64 hermesc unblock** (Task 013, marius, 2026-07-02) - `--release` on both macOS launchers now produces a fully offline, Metro-less build (Release config, JS embedded as Hermes bytecode, Android signed with the debug keystore) — verified live: owner Android (emulator, real API traffic incl. refresh rotation), medic Android (placeholder home), owner iOS (Simulator). Completes [46](f). **The hermesc gap:** RN ships no arm64-Linux Hermes compiler, so the rn-dev image **builds hermesc from official facebook/hermes source at RN's `sdks/.hermesversion` pin** (TARGETARCH=arm64-gated; ~50s; HBC-96 matches the runtime by construction; download by the commit SHA embedded in the tag string → immutable; tag baked to `/usr/local/share/hermes-tag`). `plugins/withArm64Hermesc.js` (registered in app.json) injects a **Gradle-time Groovy conditional** into the `react{}` block (os.name linux + os.arch aarch64 + binary exists → `hermesCommand=/usr/local/bin/hermesc`) — gradle.properties CANNOT work (the react plugin never reads hermesCommand from properties, `PathUtils.kt`; the CNG template's `%OS-BIN%` value errors first), and prebuild-time gating would bake the wrong answer when android/ is regenerated outside the container. amd64 (cristi): Dockerfile block skipped, conditional evaluates false → stock linux64-bin hermesc. **Release-only traps fixed (each found live):** (a) **stale-role autolinking poison** — expo-autolinking generated outputs survive owner↔medic switches as UP-TO-DATE → release compile fails with "package com.airingvet.<other-role>.dev does not exist"; release path purges both `generated/autolinking` dirs. (b) **cleartext block** — only debug manifests carry `usesCleartextTraffic`; release app rendered but ALL API calls died ("Can't reach AloVet"); app.config.ts merges `usesCleartextTraffic: profile==='dev'` into expo-build-properties (Android counterpart of the iOS ATS local-networking block; prod/staging stay locked → prod backend must be HTTPS). (c) **release-over-dev install trap** — a --release install replaces the dev app, so the next plain dev run would launch a Metro-less binary ("FR died"); both launchers detect it (Android: no DEBUGGABLE pkgFlag; iOS: embedded main.jsbundle) and force a dev rebuild. (d) **Dockerfile purge**: NodeSource nodejs DEPENDS on python3 — purging python3 removed node (build toolchain purge now leaves python3; libicu74 kept explicitly for hermesc's runtime link). **Release path hardening (impl-panel):** unconditional non-clean prebuild + post-asserts (hermesc marker AND manifest cleartext), hermes-tag drift preflight (workspace `.hermesversion` vs baked tag, + binary-exists) failing fast with a rebuild pointer, verified launch (`am start` fatal + bounded pidof poll). Panel-reviewed (5-judge plan + 6-judge impl; NOTE: fable-5/opus judges exceeded the runner's $2 USD cap — bump it or expect 4 substantive judges). Keep `HERMES_TAG` in sync with `.hermesversion` on RN upgrades (preflight catches drift at run time). iOS release needed no toolchain work (Xcode's hermes-engine pod ships macOS hermesc); `dev-ios.sh --release` skips all Metro/dev-client plumbing and plain-launches. `.ps1`/Windows: no `-Release` yet (amd64 needs no hermesc machinery — stock linux64-bin works; flag itself unbuilt). See [46][35][41][8].

[48] **Owner Consent UX — Slice 1 F1** (Task 014, marius, 2026-07-03; planned on Fable, implemented on Opus per the plan/impl model-split) - First frontend consumer of the shipped `/api/consents` contract [43]; completes onboarding's GDPR consent capture. **`src/features/consents/`** (addresses [11] blueprint): contract types, services (`listConsents` — no `size` param, ≤3 active rows fit the default page; `grantConsent`; `withdrawConsent` **swallows 404** since backend withdraw isn't idempotent; `grantInitialConsents` = `Promise.allSettled` over the signup set), pinned version constants + pure **`missingMandatoryConsents`** (absent OR `version≠pinned` → the version-bump re-prompt path, designed in not bolted on), TanStack hooks (`useConsents` spreads `readQueryOptions()` for `refetchOnMount:true` — the global default is false and would blind the gate; grant/withdraw **`refetchQueries` not invalidate** so the gate observes new state to dismiss). **Capture rides the register screen** (not a separate step — keeps the <3-min demo flow): two mandatory checkboxes (ToS + Privacy, granular per GDPR) + optional Marketing, granting orchestrated **inside `useRegister.onSuccess`** (NOT a screen-level mutate callback — that can be unmounted when auth flips): `setSession` (sync) → `await grantInitialConsents` (failures swallowed, session never reverted — Task-035 opt (a)) → `setQueryData` to warm the gate. **`ConsentGate`** in `app/(owner)/_layout.tsx` wraps the `<Tabs>` (one level below the [46] root role guards), with `useProfile`/`useRegisterPushNotifications` moved INSIDE it so no profile/push traffic fires pre-consent: Skeleton while loading, **fail-CLOSED when online** with missing/unknown mandatory state (blocking repair screen), **fail-open only offline** (mutations can't POST offline; onlineManager refetch repairs on reconnect). Extracted the inline register checkbox → owned **`ui/Checkbox`** primitive (3 call sites). New **Privacy & consents** account screen (`account/privacy.tsx`): read-only ToS/Privacy rows + a Marketing `<Switch>` deriving from server data (disabled while mutating/refetching — no optimistic lie). **Device-verified on Pixel** (repair-screen flow end-to-end). **30 new jest tests** (helper/services/hooks/gate/checkbox + useRegister 2-vs-3-POSTs & swallow); full suite 223→ green, tsc + eslint clean. **Panel plan-review** (5/6 judges) materially reshaped the design — the hook-level orchestration, fail-closed gate, refetch-not-invalidate, and 404-swallow all came from convergent findings; agy's checkmark-color finding (initially rejected) proved right on-device → fixed via inline `tokens.primaryForeground` (node [22]: theme colors via inline style, the `Text` primitive's default color wins over a NativeWind class). **Ops:** review budget $2→$7 + panel timeout 300→600s baked into `reapply-playbook-32kb-fix.py` (rerun after plugin upgrades). **⚠ Cross-lane backend fix (user-approved, flag to Cristian):** `backend/pom.xml` had `jackson-datatype-jsr310` at `test` scope → the assembled jar crashed on boot (`NoClassDefFoundError: JavaTimeModule`, springdoc 3.0.3 [33] needs it at startup) even though all tests passed — no packaged jar with springdoc had ever run (the local Docker backend predated Task 057). Widened to runtime scope; backend now boots clean. Recommend a jar-boot smoke test in CI. F2 pets (015) **DONE [49]**; F3 addresses (016) **DONE [50]**; F4 demo (017) stubbed. See [43][44][7][11][46][22][33].

[49] **Owner Pet CRUD Screens — Slice 1 F2** (Task 015, marius, 2026-07-03/04; planned on Fable, implemented on Opus per the plan/impl model-split) - Second frontend consumer of a shipped contract [42] (`/api/pets`); the demo centerpiece. **New `src/features/pets/`** (addresses [11]/consents [48] blueprint): `types` (`status` RESPONSE-ONLY; all optionals nullable), `services` (paged unwrap at `?size=100` + loud `hasNext` warn — server clamp verified in `PetController`), `hooks` (`usePets` readQueryOptions+`gcTime:Infinity`; `usePet` seeds `initialData` from list cache + `initialDataUpdatedAt:0` so the refetch still fires; optimistic-delete rollback + non-transient toast), and the **contract-semantics core `requestBody.ts`**: `buildCreateBody` (blank optionals OMITTED not `""`), `buildPatchBody` (dirty-only, POST-normalization diff — RHF re-creates arrays each render), `formatLocalDate` (local components, never `toISOString` → no UTC day-shift). **The `""`-clears vs omit nuance (verified against `PetService.applyOptionalFields`): the backend applies any non-null value, so a blanked CLEARABLE STRING (breed/sex/allergies/medications/photo) emits `""` to clear and empty `temperamentFlags` emits `[]`; but `weightKg`(number)/`birthDate`(date) have NO wire clear-sentinel so blanking them is omitted (accepted limitation).** New tab **`app/(owner)/pets/`** (paw icon, between Home/Account) with its own Stack `_layout`; list/detail/new/edit screens reuse the addresses read pattern (resolveQueryView + stale-notice + useRevalidateOnFocus) and a shared **`PetForm`** (RHF+Zod). Owned pet-domain components: `SpeciesPicker` (10 V18-mapped presets + Other→free-text; presets are UX-convenience, any string legal), `SexPicker` (unknown→`""`), `DateField` (Android imperative dialog / iOS inline), `TemperamentFlagsField` (7 user-approved preset chips), `PhotoField`/`PetThumb`, `CategoryBadge`, `Chip` (selected-label via inline `tokens.*`, node [22]). **Photos device-local (decision D1, Slice-5 resolves):** `photo` stores a device-RELATIVE `pet-photos/<uuid>.jpg` (absolute URIs go stale per-install on iOS; launchers reinstall constantly), resolved to a URI at render with a missing-file placeholder; vendor SDKs behind two `src/lib/` facades — `fileStore` (expo-file-system v19 **NEW** File/Directory/Paths API — legacy moved to `/legacy`) + `imagePicker`. **Native deps** (expo-image-picker, @react-native-community/datetimepicker, expo-file-system) forced a dev-client rebuild — **the dual-`node_modules` burden bit: `pnpm expo install` in the container populated only the `rn_node_modules` volume; the macOS launcher prebuilds on the HOST, whose separate on-disk node_modules lacked them → `PluginError: Failed to resolve plugin` — fix is `pnpm install` on BOTH (DOCKER.md §dual-node_modules).** Built + launched clean on iPhone 17 Pro Max for BOTH roles (owner + medic; role-poison trap [47a] did NOT fire — the 3 modules are role-agnostic). **Panel-reviewed twice (6 judges each):** plan-review reshaped the design (T1–T16: the `""`-clear semantics, relative-photo-path T4, post-normalization diff T7, seeded-404 T9); **impl-review caught a real shipped bug (3-judge convergence, IR1): the T9 not-found branch was UNREACHABLE** — `resolveQueryView` returns `content` whenever `data` is defined and `usePet` seeds it, so a refetch-404 rendered stale editable content; fixed by an explicit `failureReason instanceof NotFoundError` guard in the detail/edit screens before the content path. 6 more impl fixes: edit form freezes the diff-base to the seeded pet (IR2, else a background refetch reverts untouched fields cross-device), photo orphan-cleanup on re-pick/unmount (IR3), comma-decimal weight for the RO market (IR4), birthDate not-future in Zod (IR5), localized temperament labels in detail (IR6), server-error slot on DateField (IR7). **~50 jest tests; full suite 279 green, tsc + eslint 0-error.** Interactive on-device CRUD/photo walkthrough handed to Task 017 (Slice-1 demo dry-run — "two pets with photos"). **⚠ Cross-lane flags for Cristian:** (1) the `""`-clears-a-string behavior is load-bearing for the edit form but the `PetUpdateRequest` javadoc calls clearing "unsupported" — bless it as contract or land a sentinel; (2) the 7 temperament tokens (`friendly/anxious/aggressive/bites/needs_muzzle/energetic/shy`) have no backend taxonomy — adopt them for Slice-3 matching. See [42][44][48][11][7][22][46][47].

[50] **Owner Address Home-Visit Screens — Slice 1 F3** (Task 016, marius, 2026-07-04; planned on Fable, implemented on Opus per the plan/impl model-split) - Third frontend consumer of a shipped contract — B1's additively-extended `/api/addresses` [44]; the last owner-side S1 screen unit before the demo. **Contract layer (`src/features/addresses/`):** `types` drop `userId` (gone from the wire in B1), add the B1 fields as required-nullable + **`flag?: AddressFlag` OPTIONAL** (a pre-B1 address in the 7-day persisted cache predates the field — `undefined` must render no badge, P3); ONE `AddressRequestBody` for POST **and** PUT (the contract has a single `AddressRequest`); `getAddress(id)` added. **`requestBody.ts` (contract-semantics core):** `buildAddressBody` — blank optional **OMITTED = CLEARED under PUT full-replace** (D6), `formValuesOf` inverse, and the **round-trip invariant** `buildAddressBody(formValuesOf(a))` reproduces every optional (so an untouched edit re-writes the identical complete shape — how F3 survives full-replace). Numeric discipline (P10): `0` is a value (omission keys off `null`/`''`, never falsiness); a non-finite coordinate **THROWS** rather than omitting (an omit may only come from a blank — a silent omit would be a destructive clear). `normalizeDecimalInput` extracted pets→**`src/lib/number.ts`** (shared, no cross-feature import). **Geo via GPS (user-chosen):** `src/lib/location.ts` facade over **expo-location** (typed `ok|denied|unavailable`, never throws — degrades to manual entry); `app.json` plugin + iOS `locationWhenInUsePermission`; `expo config` resolves it for **both roles** (role-poison [47a] did NOT fire — location is role-agnostic). **Hooks:** `useAddress` seeds from the list cache + `initialDataUpdatedAt:0` (guaranteed refetch), **NO `gcTime:Infinity`** (the list stays last-known-good; per-id entries would outlive deleted ids — P4); update `setQueryData`-writes the PUT response into the detail cache (P5); delete evicts the detail entry. **Shared `AddressForm`** (RHF+Zod built inline → localized messages): `Chip` **promoted pets→`src/components/ui/Chip.tsx`** (P7, +dev gallery); `GeoField` (two editable decimal inputs + "Use my location" + clear), `SectorField` (Bucharest 1–6 chips, tap-again→null), `AddressFlagBadge` (caution/blocked only, colors inline via `tokens.*` node [22]). **`geoComplete` convergence (P2, critical):** the both-or-neither client Zod `superRefine` targets `path:['geoComplete']` AND the server's synthetic `fieldErrors[].field="geoComplete"` (routed verbatim by the **UNMODIFIED** `routeFormError`) both land in GeoField's one shared error slot — no shared-lib change. zipcode keyboard switched numeric→alphanumeric (contract allows letters/spaces/hyphens — P9). **Edit-screen freshness gate (P1, critical):** the editable form mounts ONLY after a **confirmed-fresh** detail fetch (`dataUpdatedAt>0`); a stale list-cache seed can never produce a PUT (else full-replace wipes the B1 fields the seed lacks — the exact D6 hazard), and seeding once removes the mid-edit reset-clobber by construction; a refetch-404 is a terminal not-found branch (IR1 pattern from [49]). List rows lead with `label` when set. **Panel plan-review (5/6 judges, agy timed out) materially reshaped the design** — P1 (4/5 convergent) and P2 (5/5) were both critical; 8 more accepted (P3–P10), zero rejects. **38 new jest tests** (requestBody round-trip/clear-semantics, location facade, hooks seed/writethrough/404, AddressForm geo+GPS+server-error, screens freshness-gate/not-found/flag-matrix); full suite **279→317** (39 suites), tsc + eslint **0-error**. **Device pass deferred to Task 017** (demo dry-run): `prebuild --clean` both roles + interactive create-w-GPS → edit-blank-clears → lone-lat error → ops-flag badge → delete/not-found — the address flow is part of the <3-min script. **⚠ Cross-lane flags for Cristian:** (1) **`geoComplete` is now a hard frontend dependency** — the synthetic `fieldErrors[].field` name is contract; document it in D6 / don't rename without flagging. (2) Unlike pets' "" -clears (which the `PetUpdateRequest` javadoc calls "unsupported"), addresses' **omit-clears-under-PUT is the DELIBERATE full-replace contract** (D6) and is load-bearing for the edit form — keep `applyRequest` setting all writable fields. (3) `flag` values `none|caution|blocked` are now surfaced as owner-facing badges (with `flagCaution/flagBlocked` copy); `flagReason` stays ops-only. See [44][48][49][11][7][22][46][47][33].

Soft-delete confirmed (`deleted_at`, owner reads exclude). **Timed run: 3:22** (missed <3:00 by 22s — pure first-run human overhead: reading steps, typing test data, 2 permission prompts; app fast; a rehearsed presenter clears it). **4 friction items found; 2 fixed, 2 parked.** Fixed: **FR-1/FR-5 universal scroll-to-first-error** — forms never passed `field.ref`, so RHF's focus-on-error no-op'd; fixed by wiring `field.ref` + explicit `setFocus`, then (FR-5, user directive "every form") centralized an **`onLayout`-based** scroll-to-errored-field in the shared `FormField` + `useFormAutoScroll` hook (initial `measureLayout` approach hit a **Fabric/New-Arch trap: `measureLayout` rejects a `findNodeHandle` number, demands a native ref, and ScrollView is composite** → switched to onLayout base+field-Y). **FR-2/FR-4:** 404 not-found screen showed "Server error…" (NotFoundError had no `QueryErrorState` branch → added `errors.notFound` + PackageX icon, en+ro); register screen had no back button (`(auth)` Stack `headerShown:false` → per-screen `<Stack.Screen headerShown:true title:''>`, "‹ login"). **Parked (user-triaged):** FR-3 push-permission prompt interrupts post-signup (pre-grant/exclude from timer, not a code bug); address-list rows don't show the flag badge (detail-only today — product call); **agy panel judge times out at 600s repeatedly** (3rd task running — rerun reapply-script or bump its timeout). **New shared code:** `src/components/ui/formFocus.ts` (`firstInvalidField`, tested) + `formScroll.tsx` (`useFormAutoScroll`, onLayout-based). Suite **317→324** (40 suites; +formFocus 4, +QueryErrorState not-found 1, +…), tsc + eslint 0-error. **Env prep tooling worth reusing:** `simctl location set`, `simctl addmedia`, uninstall+`simctl install` the built `.app` + dev-client deep-link (`<bundle>://expo-development-client/?url=…8081`) for a cold-state reset without a recompile; osascript sim-driving needs macOS Accessibility grant + HID-level events (session-tap clicks are ignored). **No backend/contract defects surfaced** — the 3 [50] cross-lane flags for Cristian stand unchanged; nothing new for his lane. **Impl panel review (6/6 judges) caught that the first FR-5 fix was incomplete/fragile → reworked (W10):** the scroll-to-first-error is now a shared, `onLayout`-based mechanism in `FormField` + `useFormAutoScroll` (`formScroll.tsx`) that (a) picks the errored field with the **smallest registered Y** via the pure, tested `smallestYErrorField` — immune to RHF/Zod key order which is schema- not render-ordered (IR-B); (b) **guards `setFocus`** so a target with no focusable ref can't crash the submit (IR-C, verified against RHF 7.75 source); (c) registers the bare **GeoField** under `geoComplete` via a new `FieldScrollAnchor` so geo errors scroll (IR-A, 5-judge convergence); (d) is wired into **all 6 RHF forms** (register/pets/address + login/account-edit/account-password) with `field.ref` on every input — satisfying the user's "every form" directive (IR-D/IR-E). **FR-6 (user, device):** validation error text was rendering **gray not red** — the node [22] trap (caption variant's `text-mutedForeground` beats an appended `text-destructive` class since `cn` has no tailwind-merge); fixed with a shared **`FieldError`** primitive using inline `style={{ color: tokens.destructive }}`, replacing all 12 error sites → device-confirmed red on all forms. **New shared UI:** `formFocus.ts` (`firstInvalidField`+`smallestYErrorField`, tested), `formScroll.tsx` (`useFormAutoScroll`+`FieldScrollAnchor`), `FieldError.tsx`. Suite **317→329**, tsc+eslint 0-error. **Also discovered + root-caused a recurring infra bug:** `.claude/bin/tasks` (the playbook CLI wrapper) had been silently truncated to 0 bytes (3rd+ occurrence) → every `tasks` command no-op'd; cause = non-atomic `cat >` regen in the plugin's `gate-echo-lib.sh` killed mid-write during panel-review's process-group timeout; `git restore`d + full writeup in `docs/playbook-tasks-wrapper-truncation-bug.md` (for an upstream playbook-plugin fix) + memory `project_tasks_wrapper_truncation`. **F-lane now: F1/F2/F3 shipped + demo'd → next F4 monorepo restructure (D2).** See [49][50][48][44][7][22][46][47].

[52] **Medic ScheduleBlock CRUD — B4 chunk 2** (Task 072, Slice 2 backend, cristi, 2026-07-03) - The write half of `/api/medic/schedule` (the [45] foundation shipped it read-only): a medic self-manages their own working-hours + block-out rows. **Working-hours model (user decision, resolving a real schema gap the plan-review Critical finding surfaced):** V33's committed `weekday`/`rrule`/`start_at`/`end_at` could not express "Mondays 09:00–17:00" — a `weekday`-only row is meaningless as working hours. Chose **"add TIME columns now"** over narrowing-to-explicit or overloading start_at/end_at → **V34** adds `start_time`/`end_time` (SQL `TIME`) and the exclusivity CHECK V33 deferred to this task. A block is exactly ONE complete shape: **recurring** = `weekday` + `start_time` + `end_time`; **explicit** = `start_at` + `end_at` (one-off block-out). `schedule_blocks_shape_exclusive` enforces recurring-XOR-explicit-with-`rrule`-NULL; `schedule_blocks_recurring_time_order` enforces `end_time > start_time`. **Two forward-only-schema decisions the impl-panel surfaced, both user-decided:** (1) **`weekday` uses ISO/`java.time.DayOfWeek` numbering `1=Monday..7=Sunday` (== `DayOfWeek.getValue()`)** — V34 DROPs V33's placeholder 0-6 range CHECK + re-adds 1-7, so the Slice-3 day-view can compare `LocalDate.getDayOfWeek().getValue()` 1:1 with no off-by-one (the original 0=Monday would have diverged from both java.time Mon=1 and PG DOW Sun=0); (2) **recurring windows may NOT cross midnight** (`end_time > start_time`) — an overnight/on-call shift is entered as two blocks (Mon 20:00–24:00 + Tue 00:00–02:00) so the day-view engine only sums simple ordered intervals. `start_time`/`end_time` are zoneless `TIME` = **Europe/Bucharest wall-clock** (pilot single-zone, documented on V34/entity/DTO). **`rrule` is reserved** — no consumer parses it, weekday+time covers pilot hours; the write DTO omits it and the CHECK requires it NULL (a future complex-recurrence task relaxes that with its own migration). **Endpoints:** `POST /schedule` (201), `GET /schedule/{id}`, `PUT /schedule/{id}` (**full-replace** — sidesteps PATCH's "null = unchanged vs clear" ambiguity for the shape check; `MedicScheduleService.apply()` nulls the unused shape's fields so a recurring→explicit switch leaves nothing stale), `DELETE /schedule/{id}` (204, hard delete — schedule inputs aren't historical records). Self-scoped exactly like [45]/[42]/[43]: `MedicRepository.findByAccountId` + `ScheduleBlockRepository.findByIdAndMedic` → cross-medic/unknown/non-medic all **404**, no existence leak, NOT `PolicyService`. New `MedicScheduleService` (not fattening the read-only `MedicProfileService`; `@org.springframework.stereotype.Service` fully-qualified per [45]'s `Service`-entity name-clash). Validation is the `AddressRequest.isGeoComplete()` pattern — `@JsonIgnore @AssertTrue` synthetic getters (`isShapeValid`/`isRecurringTimeOrdered`/`isExplicitTimeOrdered`) give clean 400s with `fieldErrors[].field` = getter name; `OpenApiContractTest` now asserts those synthetic names never leak into the `ScheduleBlockRequest` wire schema (plan-review F3). **Deliberately NOT built (overlap is not an error):** a `blocked` block overlapping `working` IS the block-out mechanism and "bookable windows are computed, never stored" (data-model :87/:215) — so no overlap rejection (the booking/`SlotHold` advisory-lock overlap primitive [32] is for Slice-3 reservations, a different concept). **Panel F-P1 caught pre-existing weekday-only test fixtures that V34 would break** — migrated them to complete recurring shapes in the same change (else vacuous/red). OpenAPI additive. Suite **457→478→482** (impl-panel fixes: java.time weekday, boundary + positive-overlap tests), 0 skipped. **Reviews:** plan-review (codex, 1 Critical schema-gap + 3 Important) → panel plan-review (only codex survived Windows `WinError 206`, 3 accepted) → impl-review (2, both rejected on the `AddressRequest` precedent — cross-field validity lives in the error envelope not OpenAPI, reserved fields are Jackson-ignored) → **panel impl-review (4/5, only agy failed): haiku's `.doesNotExist()` CRITICAL rejected (false positive — Spring passes it for null-valued paths, suite green); the 2 weekday/midnight decisions above + zone-doc + coverage accepted; per-user rate-limit gap Parked as cross-cutting (pre-existing — `/api/pets` uncovered too).** **Recurring hours now have a real time-of-day representation the Slice-3 day-view/availability engine will consume**; remaining B4 chunks after this: presence machine, day-view, Stripe Connect, `PricingConfig`. See [45][44][42][43][32][6].

[53] **Medic Consult Presence — B4 chunk 3** (Task 073, Slice 2 backend, cristi, 2026-07-03) - The medic-facing half of the data-model's planned sixth machine `medic_presence.yaml` (v2.6:57): `GET`/`PUT /api/medic/presence` so a medic goes ONLINE (available for consults) / OFFLINE, with ONLINE auto-expiring and a re-confirm push before expiry (v3.3:48, "the only live status in the system"; PRD ≥99% presence accuracy / zero stale ONLINE). **Scope decision (user-ratified): ship only the `offline↔online` edges as guarded conditional updates; DEFER the full 6-state machine + `reserved`/`in_consultation` transitions to Slice 4** — their consumers (DispatchOffer accept, VideoSession start/end) don't exist yet, so building them now is the "no consumer" trap [45]/[30]/[31]/[32] all avoided. **Decisive precedent:** `MedicReservationService.tryReserve` already ships `online→reserved` as a guarded `UPDATE … WHERE presence=online`, documented as a deferred conditional-event of that machine — this task mirrors it. **No migration** (`presence`/`presence_expires_at` in V15). **Endpoints self-scoped** exactly like [45]/[52]: `MedicRepository.findByAccountId` → non-medic 404, NOT `PolicyService` (the matrix's `MANAGE_OWN_AVAILABILITY_PRESENCE` capability stays intentionally unwired — presence is auth+ownership; documented, not a miss). **New `MedicPresenceService`** (`@org.springframework.stereotype.Service` fully-qualified per the `Service`-entity clash): `setPresence` loads the medic FIRST (404) — **load-bearing ordering** so a 0-row guarded update unambiguously means a system-set state (409) — then `setPresenceIfToggleable` (`UPDATE … SET presence,expiry WHERE id=? AND presence IN (offline,online)`); online stamps `expiry=now+ttl` (a second online **extends** = heartbeat), offline clears it; `reserved`/`in_consultation` current state OR a 0-row race → **409** `PresenceConflictException`/`MEDIC_PRESENCE_CONFLICT` (a NEW code, not the account-specific one). **Input enum `PresenceCommand{online,offline}`** makes `reserved`/`in_consultation` un-representable in the PUT body → clean **400** `VALIDATION_FAILED` (existing `handleUnreadable`), never reaching the state guard. **Expiry = a `@Scheduled` sweep, NOT per-medic jobs** (opus+codex panel convergence): the job engine has no recurring/cron primitive and per-medic epoch-keyed jobs leak a live no-op job per heartbeat (a stable key would never reschedule → medic never expires), so `MedicPresenceExpirySweep` (flag-gated `@Scheduled`, off in test profile like the `RefreshTokenCleanup`/`JobWorker` precedent) runs one batched `expireStalePresence` UPDATE flipping all stale online→offline; touches only `online` (reserved-reclaim is Slice 4). **Read-projection honesty:** `MedicPresenceResponse.effectivePresence` presents a stored-online-but-expired row as `offline` on BOTH `/presence` and `/profile` (added `presenceExpiresAt` additively) so reads are honest *before* the sweep flips the row. **F1 hardening (panel critical):** `reserveIfOnline` now requires `presenceExpiresAt > now` — an expired-but-unflipped ONLINE can never be reserved (the one defense independent of sweep timeliness); `reserveIfOnline`'s stale "watchdog reclaims reserved" javadoc corrected (that reclaim is parked to Slice 4). **Re-confirm push = a per-medic engine job** (`MedicPresenceReconfirmHandler`, type `medic-presence-reconfirm`) scheduled at `expiry−reconfirmLead`, payload `{medicId, expectedExpiresAt}` where the token is built from the **reloaded persisted** expiry (TIMESTAMPTZ round-trip precision, codex); guard = still online AND `presenceExpiresAt==token` (a heartbeat moved the epoch → stale job skips) AND not yet expired → `NotificationService.sendToSelf` (IDs-only, INV-036); **best-effort/at-least-once** (crash-resend + swallowed FCM failures — acceptable, correctness rests on the sweep + reserve-guard). **Config** `app.medic.presence.*` (`@Validated` record, compact-ctor `reconfirmLead<onlineTtl` guard): `online-ttl=PT1H`, `reconfirm-lead=PT10M` (user-ratified arbitrary pilot numbers, flagged for future ratification), `sweep-enabled`. **Reviews:** single-judge plan-review (codex, 5 findings — expired-reservable, stale-reconfirm-token, profile-stale-presence, 409-contract, missing-401 — all accepted) → **panel plan-review (3/4: haiku/opus/codex; sonnet-5 timed out)** whose opus+codex convergence drove the sweep-over-per-medic-jobs redesign + the token-from-persisted-expiry + reserved-reclaim-park + best-effort-reframe. **Impl-review (codex, 3 accepted):** total config guard (reject non-positive ttl/lead, not just lead≥ttl) + its missing test, a service unit test for the 0-row concurrent-reserve→409 branch (integration only hit the pre-check), and refreshing INV-036's push inventory to name this new live IDs-only producer. **Panel impl-review (user-requested, 2/4 — haiku/sonnet; opus+codex timed out; no live defect found):** hardening only — re-confirm token compared via `Instant.equals(Instant.parse(token))` not string equality (representation-independent), `flushAutomatically=true` on the guarded updates (latent pre-mutation trap), + sweep `==now` boundary and end-to-end refresh→stale-skip tests. Suite **482→510**, 0 skipped. Remaining B4 after this: day-view computed availability (**now built — Task 075, [54]**), Stripe Connect, `PricingConfig`. See [45][52][44][32][31][30][6][54].

[54] **Medic Day-View Computed Availability — B4 chunk 4** (Task 075, Slice 2 backend, cristi, 2026-07-03) - `GET /api/medic/day-view?date=YYYY-MM-DD` — the medic's **computed** bookable windows for one date, per channel (data-model :87 "bookable windows are computed, never stored"; pilot v3.3:50 "day view = schedule shell"). The Slice-2 shell: **NO booking/SlotHold overlay** (Slice 3, its consumer), no travel-aware windows (Slice 6). Unblocks F5's day-view screen. **Algorithm:** expand each candidate `ScheduleBlock` to an absolute `Interval`, then per `SlotChannel` (field/teleconsult are independent calendars — never cross-subtract) return `IntervalMath.subtract(union(working), union(blocked))`. Recurring rows resolve wall-clock `startTime`/`endTime` on the date through the pilot zone; explicit rows clip their `[startAt,endAt]` to the day. **New pure, domain-free `common/time/` core** (deliberately neutral so Slice-3 owner-search reuses it, not buried in the service): `Interval` (half-open `[start,end)`, compact-ctor enforces `start<end` — degenerate not representable), `IntervalMath.union`/`subtract`/`clip→Optional`, `PilotZone.ZONE = Europe/Bucharest` (single-zone pilot, config-promotion parked like Address's cityId). **DST-correct** via `date.atTime(t).atZone(ZONE)` (java.time shifts the spring gap, widens the autumn overlap) — verified with exact Instants: a 00:00–06:00 recurring window is **5 real hours** on spring-forward 2026-03-29 and **7** on fall-back 2026-10-25. **DST-gap drop (panel P4/codex, the sharpest finding):** a recurring window sitting entirely in the skipped hour (03:00–04:00 on the spring day) collapses to zero length after zone resolution; the service **drops a non-positive-length interval** rather than construct an illegal `Interval` (which would 500) — `Interval` stays strict, the service filters. **Query:** `ScheduleBlockRepository.findForDay(medic, dow, dayStart, dayEnd)` — one medic-scoped JPQL (`b.medic=:medic AND (b.weekday=:dow OR (b.startAt<:dayEnd AND b.endAt>:dayStart))`), the two shape-branches mutually exclusive by V34's CHECK; the **service is the single owner** of the `dayStart`/`dayEnd`/ISO-`dow` computation and passes them in (panel P3, no test-vs-service drift). **Self-scoped** exactly like [45]/[52]/[53]: `MedicRepository.findByAccountId`→ non-medic **404**, NOT `PolicyService`. **DTO** `DayViewResponse{date, zone, channels:[{channel, windows:[{start,end}]}]}` — windows are **absolute Instants** (compose with Slice-3 Instant-based holds), **both channels always present** (windows possibly empty) for a stable contract; `date` **required** (deterministic, no server-clock dependency). **Error-contract fix (F1):** a missing required query param threw `MissingServletRequestParameterException` → fell through to the 500 handler; added it to `GlobalExceptionHandler.handleUnreadable` → missing `date` now 400 `VALIDATION_FAILED` (a general fix, every required-param endpoint benefits). **Reviews:** single-judge plan-review (codex, **5 accepted** — missing-param 500, query-scoping-not-proven-by-count, channel-isolation-untested, missing-401, DST-fall-back-underspecified) → **panel plan-review 4/4** (haiku/opus/sonnet/codex — first full-panel success in a while) **4 more accepted** — clip as a tested pure op (P1), explicit-`working` block is an orthogonal untested code path (P2, sonnet+codex converged), day-bounds single-owner (P3), DST-gap-500 drop (P4). Tests: `IntervalMathTest` (25, pure edge cases incl. inside-splits-into-two + composed containment invariant), `MedicDayViewServiceTest` (15, expansion/partition/clip/DST spring+fall+partial-gap+gap-drop/bounds-captor), `MedicDayViewIntegrationTest` (11, real JPQL weekday filter + cross-medic scoping + boundary-touch + 401/400/404/200). OpenAPI additive (`getDayView`, `DayViewResponse`/`ChannelAvailability`/`Window`). **Impl reviews:** single-judge (codex) clean; **panel impl 4/4** — 2 applied (DST partial-gap javadoc over-claimed the collapse: only an exact-gap-width aligned window collapses, a *partial*-gap window shifts forward and survives shorter — narrowed doc + characterization test; + explicit-shape channel-isolation test), **2 parked** (precise `ZoneRules` gap-edge clipping — current independent-endpoint resolution under-reports ≤gap on ~2 DST days/yr but fails safe; and an extreme parseable `date` like `+999999999-12-31` → `plusDays` `DateTimeException` → catch-all 500, needs a date-range guard, a contract decision). Suite **510→561**, 0 skipped. Remaining B4: Stripe Connect (**now built — Task 076, [55]**), `PricingConfig` (no consumer until Slice 3). See [45][52][53][44][6][55].

[55] **Medic Stripe Connect Onboarding — B4 chunk 5** (Task 076, Slice 2 backend, cristi, 2026-07-03) - `POST /api/medic/stripe/connect-link` (create the vet's connected account on first call, return a hosted onboarding URL) + `GET /api/medic/stripe/status` (v2 capability readiness). Pilot v3.3:51 "Stripe Connect test-mode onboarding via hosted link; connected-status visible in app" — the last B4 chunk, unblocks F5's Stripe screen. **PricingConfig deferred whole to Slice 3a** (no consumer; its resolution fn is built there — the same "no consumer yet" discipline [45]/[44] applied). **Accounts v2, NOT legacy `type:express`** (Stripe skill critical rule; `type: express|custom|standard` deprecated): a **Recipient** account (`configuration.recipient.capabilities.stripe_balance.stripe_transfers.requested`, `dashboard=express`, `fees/losses=application`) — the O8-compatible posture serving BOTH destination and separate-charges (data-model :163), so the O8 fiscal gate does NOT force an account-shape decision now. Money movement (charges/transfers/refunds/ledger) stays Slice-3b/O8-gated; webhooks (`account.updated`) deferred to 3b (`stripe_events` dedupe already exists) — pull-on-demand `GET /status` suffices for the pilot. **SDK** `com.stripe:stripe-java:33.1.0` via `StripeClient` (v23+ path to v2). **Neutral `stripe/` package** (reused by Slice 3): `StripeProperties` (`app.stripe.*`, `@Validated` compact-ctor guard — enabled ⇒ nonblank secret + absolute http(s) onboarding URLs + **`sk_test_`/`rk_test_` unless `allowLiveKey`** [pilot fail-safe] + positive connect/read timeouts), `StripeConfig` (`@ConditionalOnProperty app.stripe.enabled` → `StripeClient` bean w/ timeouts), `StripeConnectGateway` interface (the mockable seam — tests replace it, real SDK exercised only in the live verify) + `LiveStripeConnectGateway` (ALWAYS `@Component`, `@Nullable StripeClient` injected — disabled ⇒ `requireClient()` throws `StripeNotConfiguredException`; an absent bean can't throw, the FcmClient pattern), `StripeNotConfiguredException`. **Medic-facing (`medic/`):** `MedicStripeController` (self-scoped auth+ownership → non-medic 404, NOT PolicyService, like [45]-[54]), `MedicStripeService` (orchestrator, NOT `@Transactional`), `MedicStripeAccountWriter` (separate proxied bean, `@Transactional(REQUIRES_NEW)`). **Concurrency/idempotency — key, not lock (the plan-panel's biggest win):** a deterministic Stripe **idempotency key** `medic-{id}-connect-acct` on `accounts().create` makes concurrent double-taps + lost-response retries return the SAME account, so **no DB lock is held across the Stripe network call** (the pessimistic-lock-across-I/O the single-judge first proposed was itself the pool-stall bug — sonnet caught it). **Orphan guard (F1):** the account id commits in the writer's own short tx BEFORE the link call, so a link failure never orphans a created account; retry reuses the id. **v2-only status DTO** `StripeAccountStatusResponse{state, transfersStatus, outstandingRequirements, pastDue, statusReason}` — NO deprecated v1 `chargesEnabled`/`payoutsEnabled`/`detailsSubmitted`, NO redundant `connected`/`transfersActive` booleans (FE derives from `state`; one source of truth). **v2 `Requirements` is entry-based** (`getEntries()`+per-entry deadline/impact + capability `getStatusDetails()`), NOT the v1 flat due-lists the docs implied — verified real getters via `javap` on the jar at the CHECKPOINT gate and adapted the mapping. State: `active⇒CONNECTED`; `restricted`/`unsupported` OR restricting/past-due requirement `⇒RESTRICTED`; else `PENDING`; no id `⇒NOT_STARTED`. **Errors:** `StripeException→502 STRIPE_ERROR` (logged w/ code+requestId, nothing leaked), `StripeNotConfiguredException→503 STRIPE_NOT_CONFIGURED`. **Reviews:** plan single-judge (5, all accepted) → **plan panel 4/4 (10 accepted)** — idempotency-key-over-lock (B/C), REQUIRES_NEW-in-separate-bean (D), test-key guard (F), v2-only DTO (F4/H), explicit state table (G); impl single-judge (F2 real defect — Stripe call was still inside the writer's tx → moved outside; F3 status-mapping; F4 concurrency-test-hardening) → **impl panel 4/4** — **converged (sonnet+codex) that `status()` re-had the same tx-across-I/O bug on the higher-traffic endpoint** (removed `@Transactional`) + the writer's propagation was implicit not REQUIRES_NEW (made explicit) + 2 null guards; parked (idempotency-key hardening, gateway-parsing unit tests → Slice 3b/follow-up), rejected the active-before-flags reorder (active = payouts work now). **W9 LIVE VERIFY against real Stripe test mode (the "properly" bar, user supplied keys):** ran the real HTTP round-trip via docker-compose — `POST /connect-link`→200 real `acct_…` + hosted URL, persisted, `GET /status`→200 `RESTRICTED` w/ real requirements, idempotent (2 calls→1 account). **Three real Stripe requirements surfaced ONLY by the live run** (mocks couldn't): recipient needs `contactEmail` (threaded medic email through), `identity.country` required before `configuration.recipient` (set `RO`, single-country pilot const), and — **incidental pre-existing, NOT Stripe** — the packaged jar couldn't boot: `jackson-datatype-jsr310` was `test`-scoped but springdoc needs it at runtime (`NoClassDefFoundError`) → moved to compile scope (**blocks Task 003 deploy too**; the first real packaged-jar run caught it). Pre-Stripe blockers the user cleared: restricted key lacked Connect:Write, then Connect/Accounts-v2 not enabled on the sandbox → platform-setup. **Suite 561→597**, 0 skipped. B4 now COMPLETE (PricingConfig is the only named leftover, correctly deferred to Slice 3a). Next backend units per [44]: B2 seed/config tooling → B3 0b demo. See [45][52][53][54][44][6][23].

[56] **B2 Seed/Config Tooling — Clinic + audited CLI + demo-env** (Task 077, Slice 0b remainder, cristi, 2026-07-05) - Reopened task 070's audit-only defer now that B4 shipped complete [55]. **Critical correction mid-design (plan-review, verified against `docs/specs/2026-07-02-backend-frontend-split-plan.md:115`):** B2 is explicitly "Scripts, no HTTP surface" — the initial REST-admin-endpoint design (mirroring `AccountAdminController`) was wrong for this item; rebuilt as audited CLI commands, `openapi.yaml` untouched. **`Clinic` entity (V35)** — the 0a deferral finally built, data-model v2.6:59's exact field list (`legalName, fiscalId` UNIQUE natural key, `stripeAccountId?, directoryName, address, lat/lng, phone, facilitiesNotes, sector, cityId` plain-BIGINT single-seeded-value like `ServiceArea.cityId`, `visibleInFallbackDirectory, createdBy`) + its own `is_test` leaf (no Account to derive lineage from, unlike task-060's Account/Interaction/Order chain). FKs added on **both** `medics.clinic_id` and `admin_grants.clinic_id` (V15 left both bare BIGINT — plan-review caught the second one I'd missed); safe with zero backfill (zero Medic rows exist anywhere, confirmed via `new Medic(` appearing only in test fixtures). **CLI foundation (`admin/cli/`):** a property-gated `ApplicationRunner` (`app.admin.command` blank ⇒ no-op, normal boot unaffected) dispatching to `AdminCommand` beans indexed by `AdminCommandRegistry` (mirrors `JobHandlerRegistry`'s fail-fast-on-duplicate-name pattern, same `spotbugs-exclude.xml` `CT_CONSTRUCTOR_THROW` carve-out). **Checks actor `AccountStatus.active` itself** — the one gate HTTP callers get for free from `JwtAuthenticationFilter` that a CLI entry point has no other path to (panel-review Critical: `PolicyService.require` alone never checks status). `dispatch(commandName, actorEmail, payloadJson)` takes explicit params (not `@Value` fields) so it's Mockito-unit-testable with zero risk of a stray `System.exit` inside a test JVM. **Real bug the tests caught, not review:** `AdminPayloadParser` first injected Spring's `ObjectMapper` — **this project has no autoconfigured `ObjectMapper` bean at all** (confirmed by `OpenApiContractTest` itself failing the same way once requested; matches `RateLimitFilter`'s pre-existing hand-written-JSON comment) — fixed by building one locally (`new ObjectMapper().registerModule(new JavaTimeModule())`). **Three commands, three capabilities, one authorization layer each (no bolted-on scope checks):** `clinic-upsert` (`EDIT_CLINIC_DIRECTORY`, idempotent by `fiscalId`), `medic-onboard` (`VERIFY_MEDIC_LICENSES` — roles-permissions-v1.4:42's "verify licenses, approve onboarding" is one action; **first code anywhere to set `MedicStatus.active`**, gated on `licenseVerifiedAt` being supplied; race-safe via pre-check + outer `DataIntegrityViolationException→MedicAlreadyOnboardedException` catch, mirroring `StripeEventDedupeService`'s "unique constraint is the concurrent-race backstop, never a resumable branch"), `medic-affiliate` (`MANAGE_CLINIC_AFFILIATIONS`). **Deliberately un-scoped clinic_admin gap named, not fixed:** the matrix grants `CLINIC_ADMIN` both clinic capabilities unscoped by `clinicId` (`RoleResolver.rolesOf()` discards each grant's `clinicId`) — real but pre-existing, and moot today since `roles-permissions-v1.4:13` confirms clinic_admin has zero CLI access in the pilot; a panel-suggested "ops-only bolt-on check" was explicitly rejected as itself violating the single-authorization-layer convention. **Demo-env refresh as a job-engine duty** (corrected mid-plan from "bare service call" after panel review cited v3.3:29 naming it a background-job-subsystem duty): `DemoEnvRefreshHandler implements JobHandler` wraps `DemoEnvService.refresh`, the CLI command `enqueue`s with `dedupeKey="demo-env-refresh"` then `worker.drain()`s synchronously in-process — dedupe collapses a concurrent second trigger at the queue level (reused primitive, no new advisory-lock code). Gated `SUSPEND_REACTIVATE_ACCOUNTS` (task-060's test-lineage capability, reused rather than adding a new enum value — `Capability`'s doc comment locks it 1:1 to the roles-permissions spec, enforced by `PolicyMatrixTest`'s exact `expected()` map, so a demo-env-specific capability would itself be the spec-fidelity drift that test exists to catch; disambiguated via the audit `action="demo_env_refresh"` string instead, same mechanism as `mark_test`/`unmark_test`). **Fixed dataset** (corrected arithmetic mid-build — "one per clinic + one independent" didn't sum to 6): 3 clinics × 2 medics = 6 medics, 6 owners × 5 pets = 30 pets, every demo medic gets one `ServiceArea` + one recurring `ScheduleBlock`. Composes `ClinicDirectoryService.upsert(actor, cmd, true)` (made public for cross-package reuse) and `MedicOnboardingService.onboard` rather than duplicating entity-construction — both sub-actions' capability checks pass automatically since `SUSPEND_REACTIVATE_ACCOUNTS`/`EDIT_CLINIC_DIRECTORY`/`VERIFY_MEDIC_LICENSES` all resolve to the same `{PLATFORM_OPS, SUPERADMIN}` role set; each nested action gets its own audit row too (a feature — full transparency into what a refresh touched). Idempotent by natural key throughout: accounts/clinics by their real UNIQUE constraints, `ServiceArea` by its real UNIQUE, `ScheduleBlock` by an application-level predicate (no DB constraint exists — the table deliberately allows overlaps, [52]), `Pet` by `(owner, name)` where the name **is** the fixed index ("Demo Pet 1".."5"). Explicitly NOT built: Interaction/history seeding for demo pets (needs the not-yet-built Slice-3 booking flow, same park as task 070) — B3's scripted demo works around this live rather than pre-seeding it. **Reviews:** single-judge plan-review (5 accepted, the Critical REST→CLI correction) → 4-model panel plan-review (haiku/opus/sonnet/codex, 2 Critical + 7 Important — all triaged: 6 accepted as-is, 1 accepted-with-simplification (drop the clinic_admin bolt-on check per the single-layer convention), 1 rejected (new capability would violate `PolicyMatrixTest`'s locked spec-fidelity contract)). **A real test-isolation bug the full-suite run caught** (not production): `DemoEnvServiceIntegrationTest`'s audit-count assertion wasn't scoped to its own actor, over-counting rows left by the CLI integration test in the same shared embedded-postgres — fixed by filtering on `actingAccountId`. **Suite 597→636**, 0 skipped. Runbook: `docs/specs/2026-07-05-manual-onboarding-runbook.md` (real `docker compose run --rm backend` invocation shape, checked against `Dockerfile`'s `ENTRYPOINT`). **Parked:** clinic_admin request/ops-approval two-party affiliation workflow (needs the `RoleResolver` clinicId-scoping fix first), clinic-directory CSV/bulk import, full demo-medic pricing/service graphs, Interaction/history seeding for demo pets. **B2 now COMPLETE — B3 (the 0b scripted demo) is the only remaining Slice-0b-remainder unit.** See [44][45][52][55][6][31].

[57] **B3 — Slice-0b Scripted Demo** (Task 078, Slice 0b remainder, cristi, 2026-07-05) - Closes Slice 0b: the last unit of the split-plan B-lane [44] (B1/B2/B4 already done). **Investigated before building (not assumed):** exec-strategy §7's "a scripted end-to-end dry run through the state-machine runner" reads like a two-phone UI demo, but verified there is **no `InteractionController` anywhere** and **no `DispatchOffer` entity yet** (Slice 3/4 unbuilt) — `InteractionStateMachines`/`InteractionTransitionRunner` are real, complete, `@Tag("INV-022")`-fidelity-tested (3 real machines: teleconsult_instant, teleconsult_scheduled, visit_home) and `InteractionService.create()` is a real write path, but all exercised only by tests today — there is no HTTP/UI surface to click through. v3.3's own two-phone-demo framing (principle 4) applies to consumer slices (1+); 0a's and 0b's demos are explicitly non-UI ("test suite passes on CI" / "scripted dry run") — same register. **User confirmed the format** (asked directly rather than assumed): a single narrated integration test, not a new CLI command or a doc-only runbook — mirrors 0a's own demo precedent and doubles as permanent regression coverage. **`Slice0bScriptedDemoIntegrationTest`** (`interaction` package, extends `AbstractClinicalSpineIntegrationTest`) walks all three Slice-0b pillars (exec-strategy tasks 5-7) as one story, each section independently asserted: (1) creates a real Interaction via `InteractionService.create()`; (2) fires the teleconsult_instant happy path via `InteractionTransitionRunner.fire()` — explicitly framed as a **kernel-only** dry run (the runner's own javadoc: guards/side-effects are a future orchestrator's job, not here) — status asserted after every fire, plus the **exact** 5-row ledger tuple sequence (genesis + 4 transitions) via a new real, reusable `InteractionStatusEventRepository.findByInteractionOrderByIdAsc`; (3) a negative case — an undeclared transition from the terminal state throws `UndeclaredTransitionException` with status/ledger provably unchanged; (4) job engine: `CategoryRecomputeHandler` (a genuine pre-existing Slice-1 handler, not B2's own demo-env-refresh job — deliberately showing a different corner of the engine) recomputes a pet's category via a real `enqueue`+`drain()`, asserted against the actual DB row change, not just "drain didn't throw"; (5) concurrency: `MedicReservationService.tryReserve` — one winner flips presence to `reserved`, a second loses and leaves the first's expiry untouched (sequential proof, same discipline `MedicReservationContentionTest` already established — no two-thread harness). **Plan-review (5 Important, all accepted):** sharpened the state-machine step to explicit kernel-only framing (was overclaiming a product flow); caught the ledger proof was too weak (count-only misses a wrong intermediate tuple); caught the job-engine fixture was wrong in a subtle way (`dog`/`cat` are already seeded in `species_map`, and the inherited `newPet()` bypasses `PetService.create`'s derivation entirely — using either alone would test the wrong thing); caught the reservation fixture would fail for the wrong reason (`Medic` defaults `offline` with no expiry); enforced test-before-mind-map-update sequencing. **One real bug the implementation itself then caught:** a Postgres TIMESTAMPTZ-vs-Java-`Instant` nanosecond-precision mismatch on the reservation-expiry equality assertion — fixed by comparing against the re-fetched DB value, not the original `Instant.now()`-derived one (same fix `MedicReservationContentionTest` already uses, for the same reason). **Suite 645→646**, 0 skipped. **Slice 0b is now fully closed — B1/B2/B3/B4 all done.** **Slice 3a (scheduled consult booking) shipped next (Task 079, [58]):** owner-facing medic search/profile/availability + the hold→confirm→medic-decision booking flow + PricingConfig's first consumer + itemized order display; the teleconsult_scheduled machine gained its `requested` medic-confirm entry state (data-model v2.7, user-ratified); INV-027/INV-008 flipped covered. 3b (payments) stays O8-gated; 003 deploy/CI-CD is the only other open pending task. See [44][45][52][55][56][30][31][32][58].

sequential ids never let another owner confirm) +`interaction_id` UNIQUE (idempotency backstop; table cleared first — zero pre-3a product writes), `idx_interactions_medic_window`. **`matching/`:** `MatchabilityService` — THE one chain every surface (search/profile/availability/hold/confirm) resolves through: owner→owned-active-pet→category-first (needs_review→400 `PET_NEEDS_REVIEW`, new code)→one candidate JPQL (profile AND account active — suspension only mutates Account.status; no-self; `isTest` equality both ways; offers-scheduled; optional specialty hard filter; capped+logged). `MedicSearchService`: batch child fetches (1 stmt/kind), in-memory integer min-max composite (config `MatchingProperties`, New<threshold→neutral 400, id tiebreak), sort=composite|rating|price|available, in-memory paging; `GET /api/medics`, `/{id}`, `/{id}/availability?petId&date`, `GET /api/specialties` (content listing WITH i18n name map — chips need labels; deliberate new precedent). **`booking/`:** `AvailabilityService` (free = extracted-`ScheduleWindows` [shared with day-view; now enforces recurring-weekday-vs-date itself — surfaced+fixed a task-075 DST-test fixture bug] − busy non-terminal scheduled teleconsults − LIVE holds − now+minStartLead; window-anchored slot discretization; 3-stmt batch `earliestSlotStartByMedic` → "Disponibil din…"); `BookingService.hold` (matchability re-validated → **lock-then-feasibility-then-insert** via new `SlotHoldService.lockMedic` seam + reentrant `createHold`; `countOverlappingLiveHolds` fixed to ignore expired-unswept `held` rows; createHold idempotent branch now requires ownerId equality — client-chosen checkoutRef must never resolve cross-owner); `BookingService.confirm` (FOR-UPDATE owner-scoped hold; linked→same booking; Interaction created WITH window via extended `CreateInteractionCommand`; hold expiry extended to min(24h, slot start); OrderLine + `recomputeTotals` in-tx = INV-008; instant-book→shared accept path, else IDs-only medic push + `HoldExpiryJobHandler` job); `BookingDecisionService` (the orchestrator the kernel-runner reserved: row-lock interaction→hold, post-lock re-check, fire+convert/release same tx; losers 409 `BOOKING_NOT_PENDING`, never a second ledger row; expiry path stale-job-immune); `GET/POST /api/bookings(+/hold,/{id})`, medic `GET /api/medic/booking-requests` + `/confirm|/decline`. Day-view gains ADDITIVE `consults[]` (same busy query availability subtracts — can't disagree). **Demo env (codex-F5):** refreshMedics now seeds category+priced-scheduled-row (varied 115-190 RON so ranking shows)+rotating specialty; instantBookOptIn now a MIX (all-true made the demo's confirm step unreachable); proven by demo-owner login→search→6 ranked medics. **Invariants:** INV-027 + INV-008 flipped covered (EXPECTED_COVERED 9→11); INV-028/INV-032 reasons sharpened (hard-filter/scheduled-never-mix halves BUILT here; prompt/instant/analytics halves await S4+) — 056 discipline. **Tier-1 guards:** search 11 stmts flat 3→8 medics; availability 9 flat; display 8 — all exact-pinned. **Concurrency proofs (blocking-lock discipline):** service-layer two-thread hold contention (B blocked on advisory lock, bounded-await-false; exactly one hold) + decision race (decline blocked on row lock while accept's tx open → 409 + genesis+1 rows).
